metadata.name: AI Career Threat Index
metadata.creator: MeritForge AI
metadata.description: Open dataset scoring 300 professions on AI displacement risk (MIT licensed). Each role carries four published sub-scores (task automation potential, tool maturity, employer adoption, agentic exposure) combined by an open formula into a 0-100 score (higher = greater displacement risk). Scores are structured editorial estimates against a published rubric, not measurements.
metadata.riskBandsNote: Scores within 5 points of a band boundary may retain their prior band across quarterly reviews for stability.

roles.title: IT Support Specialist

rationales.taskAutomation: The bulk of tier-1 work -- resets, provisioning, install/update requests, connectivity diagnosis, ticket routing -- is scripted digital interaction AI already resolves end-to-end.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI helpdesk chatbots and automated ticketing/remediation platforms are mature, commodity, and standard in enterprise IT stacks.
rationales.adoption: AI-first tier-1 deflection is now a mainstream expectation for enterprise IT support functions.
rationales.agenticExposure: Tier-1 support is a textbook agent-ownable pipeline: intake, diagnosis, remediation, and closure with minimal human handoff.

tasksGrowing: Complex, multi-system integration troubleshooting
Cybersecurity incident first response
Administering and tuning AI support agents for the org
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1232 (Computer User Support Specialists), p25-p75
roles.insight: AI helpdesk agents now resolve most tier-1 tickets -- resets, installs, routing -- without a human touch, and that volume was this role's core. The specialists still hired are the ones running and tuning those agents, or moving into security first-response and cloud identity work.

roles.title: Systems Administrator

rationales.taskAutomation: Server provisioning, patching, account management, and backup scheduling are routine digital work AI-driven automation now runs unattended.
rationales.toolMaturity: Cloud automation and AI-ops tooling are mature and standard in enterprise infrastructure operations.
rationales.adoption: Common across large firms and spreading to mid-market as cloud migration continues.
rationales.agenticExposure: Provisioning, patch management, and backup verification are agent-ownable back-office pipelines today.

tasksGrowing: Hybrid and multi-cloud migration strategy
Security hardening and zero-trust implementation
Automation architecture and orchestration design
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1244 (Network and Computer Systems Administrators), p25-p75
roles.insight: Cloud automation and AI-ops tooling now run provisioning, patching, and backup verification without a human in the loop. The clearest path forward is cloud architecture, security hardening, and automation design rather than day-to-day server administration.

roles.title: Business Intelligence Analyst

rationales.taskAutomation: Standard dashboard builds, recurring KPI reports, and routine SQL extraction make up most of the role's core output, and AI now produces that directly.
rationales.toolMaturity: BI copilots (Power BI Copilot, Looker AI) are mature and widely shipped inside standard enterprise BI stacks.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted BI reporting is now a mainstream expectation across large and mid-market firms.
rationales.agenticExposure: Dashboard refresh and recurring report maintenance are agent-ownable pipelines with minimal human review needed.

tasksAtRisk: Building and maintaining recurring KPI dashboards
Standard SQL data extraction and reporting
tasksGrowing: Defining new metrics for ambiguous business questions
Cross-functional stakeholder alignment on what to measure
Root-cause investigation behind metric anomalies
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1211 (Computer Systems Analysts, closest BI-analyst mapping), p25-p75

defenseSkills.skill: Directing AI BI Copilots (Power BI, Looker)

roles.insight: AI BI copilots now build and refresh most recurring dashboards without an analyst touching the query. The role's future is in deciding what should be measured in the first place and chasing down why a metric moved, not in maintaining the pipeline that reports it.

roles.title: Network Support Specialist

rationales.taskAutomation: Routine connectivity troubleshooting, config resets, and standard ticket triage are scripted digital work AI now handles end-to-end for known issue types.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted network diagnostics and chatbot-driven triage tools are mature and standard in enterprise NOC tooling.
rationales.adoption: Common in enterprise network operations centers running AI-assisted triage.
rationales.agenticExposure: Tier-1 network ticket triage and known-fix remediation are agent-ownable pipelines today.

tasksGrowing: Complex, multi-site outage diagnosis
Network security incident first response
Administering AI-driven NOC triage tooling
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1231 (Computer Network Support Specialists), p25-p75
roles.insight: AI triage tools now resolve most known-fix network tickets before a specialist sees them. The path forward is running that triage layer and handling the multi-site, security-flavored incidents it can't close on its own.

roles.title: QA/Test Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: Regression suite authoring, manual UI checks against a spec, and standard bug write-ups are now largely AI-generated and self-maintained; test strategy and edge-case judgment are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Agentic test-generation and self-healing tools (Testim, Mabl, AI-assisted Playwright) are credible and improving fast.
rationales.adoption: Common in tech-forward engineering orgs, spreading into mid-market as agentic test tools mature.
rationales.agenticExposure: Test maintenance and regression-suite upkeep are exactly the kind of scoped, verifiable loop agents already own in production.

tasksAtRisk: Writing and maintaining regression test suites
Manual UI testing against written specifications
Bug report creation for well-understood issues
tasksGrowing: Testing AI/ML system outputs for correctness and drift
Performance, security, and resilience testing strategy
Exploratory testing and edge-case discovery
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1253 (Software Quality Assurance Analysts and Testers), p25-p75
roles.insight: Agentic test tools now write, run, and self-heal most regression suites, which is why routine QA is now a substantially agent-owned workflow. Engineers who validate AI system outputs and own security/performance test strategy are the ones still growing in value.

roles.title: Data Analyst

rationales.taskAutomation: Standard SQL queries, routine dashboard builds, and recurring reports make up half or more of core output, and AI now produces most of that directly.
rationales.toolMaturity: BI copilots (Power BI Copilot, Tableau AI, Looker AI) are mature and widely shipped inside the standard analytics toolchain.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted BI is now a mainstream expectation at large and mid-market firms alike.
rationales.agenticExposure: Dashboard refresh, standard report generation, and recurring query maintenance are agent-ownable pipelines today.

tasksAtRisk: Writing standard SQL queries and reports
Recurring business reporting and metric tracking
Basic data cleaning for known source systems
tasksGrowing: Framing ambiguous business questions into analysis plans
Stakeholder-facing insight synthesis and storytelling
Root-cause investigation on data anomalies
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-2051 (Data Scientists, closest analyst mapping), p25-p75

defenseSkills.skill: Directing AI BI Copilots (Power BI, Tableau, Looker)

roles.insight: AI BI copilots now write the queries and refresh the dashboards that used to be most of this job. Analysts who frame the business question and tell the story behind the numbers are pulling away from analysts who just build reports.

roles.title: Database Administrator

rationales.taskAutomation: Cloud-managed databases now self-tune, self-patch, and self-backup for standard workloads; only architecture, security posture, and complex migrations require a DBA's judgment.
rationales.toolMaturity: Autonomous cloud database services (AWS RDS/Aurora, Azure SQL, GCP AlloyDB) are mature and widely deployed in production.
rationales.adoption: Cloud-managed database adoption is common across large firms and spreading into mid-market as on-prem estates shrink.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can already run provisioning, backup verification, and routine tuning end-to-end; data architecture strategy stays with people.

tasksAtRisk: Query optimization for common access patterns
Routine backup, replication, and recovery procedures
tasksGrowing: Data architecture design for AI and analytics workloads
Database security, access control, and compliance ownership
Multi-cloud data strategy and governance
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1242 (Database Administrators), p25-p75

defenseSkills.skill: Vector Database & RAG Data Layer Management

roles.insight: Autonomous cloud databases now handle tuning, patching, and backup verification without a human in the loop, which is why routine DBA work is the most automated slice of the Technology category. The growth path is data architecture for AI workloads, vector data layers, and security -- not day-to-day administration.

roles.title: Web Developer

rationales.taskAutomation: Landing pages, standard layouts, and common UI components are now built end-to-end by AI page-builders; complex interactive apps and accessibility work are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI page-generation and coding-copilot tools for standard web stacks are mature and widely deployed.
rationales.adoption: Common across agencies and product teams; spreading into small-business and freelance work.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can scaffold and ship standard pages and components end-to-end; complex app logic stays with developers.

tasksAtRisk: Building landing pages from design files
Writing standard CSS and responsive layouts
tasksGrowing: Complex interactive application development
Performance optimization and accessibility engineering
AI feature and agent integration into web apps
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1254 (Web Developers), p25-p75
roles.insight: AI page-builders now ship standard sites and components with little human editing. Developers who own complex application logic, accessibility, and AI feature integration are the ones still commanding a premium.

roles.title: DevOps Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: Standard CI/CD configs, IaC templates, and routine monitoring/alerting rules are now generated end-to-end by AI tooling; incident command and security architecture are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AIOps platforms and IaC copilots (GitHub Actions Copilot, Pulumi AI, AIOps alerting) are mature and embedded in mainstream pipelines.
rationales.adoption: Common practice at tech-forward firms and large engineering orgs; spreading fast given the direct cost savings.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can own pipeline configuration, routine provisioning, and first-pass incident triage end-to-end within a product cycle.

tasksAtRisk: Writing and maintaining CI/CD pipeline configurations
Routine log monitoring and alert-rule tuning
tasksGrowing: Incident command and complex distributed-systems troubleshooting
Security-first infrastructure and zero-trust design
Multi-cloud architecture strategy and cost optimization
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1252 (Software Developers, closest DevOps mapping), p25-p75
roles.insight: AI now writes and maintains most CI/CD configuration and IaC boilerplate directly. Engineers who move into platform strategy, incident command, and security architecture are the ones whose value keeps climbing as the routine plumbing disappears.

roles.title: Data Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: Standard ETL construction, schema migrations, and data-quality checks are now largely AI-generated; enterprise data architecture at scale still requires engineering judgment.
rationales.toolMaturity: Pipeline-as-code frameworks paired with AI codegen (dbt Copilot-style tools, AI-assisted Airflow/Spark authoring) are mature and improving.
rationales.adoption: Common practice at data-forward firms; spreading to mid-market as managed pipeline platforms lower the bar.
rationales.agenticExposure: Pipeline monitoring, self-healing data-quality checks, and routine migration scripts are agent-ownable today.

tasksGrowing: Enterprise data architecture and platform strategy at scale
Debugging complex, cross-system data-quality failures
Governance and lineage design for AI training data
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1252 (Software Developers, closest data-pipeline mapping), p25-p75
roles.insight: AI now writes and self-heals most routine pipeline code, from ETL scripts to data-quality checks. The lasting work is architecting data platforms for AI training and RAG workloads, where governance and lineage decisions still need a person accountable for them.

roles.title: Mobile Developer

rationales.taskAutomation: Standard screens, boilerplate components, and common feature tickets are now AI-generated for both iOS and Android; complex native performance work is not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI coding copilots for Swift and Kotlin are mature and standard in mobile engineering workflows.
rationales.adoption: Common practice across app-development teams and agencies.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can scaffold standard screens and simple feature tickets end-to-end; platform-specific performance debugging stays with developers.

tasksAtRisk: Building standard UI screens from design specs
Implementing common app features from templates
Writing unit and UI tests for standard components
App store release and metadata packaging
tasksGrowing: Complex native performance and battery optimization
Cross-platform architecture decisions
Platform-specific edge-case debugging (iOS/Android fragmentation)
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1252 (Software Developers), p25-p75
roles.insight: AI now scaffolds standard screens and common features for both major mobile platforms directly from a spec. Developers who own cross-platform architecture and can debug real device fragmentation issues are the ones clients still need.

roles.title: Site Reliability Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: Runbook execution, routine alert triage, and standard monitoring config are automatable end-to-end; novel outage diagnosis and reliability strategy are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AIOps and incident-automation platforms are mature and embedded in standard on-call tooling.
rationales.adoption: Common at tech-forward engineering orgs running production services at scale.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run first-pass incident triage and execute known runbooks; root-cause analysis on novel outages stays human.

tasksAtRisk: Routine alert triage and noise reduction
tasksGrowing: Novel outage root-cause analysis
Reliability architecture and capacity strategy
Cross-team incident command for major outages
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1252 (Software Developers, closest SRE mapping), p25-p75
roles.insight: Agents now run first-pass triage and execute known runbooks before a human is paged. The SREs still in demand are the ones who own reliability architecture and can run incident command when the runbook doesn't cover it.

roles.title: UX Designer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI now generates wireframes, mockup variations, and design-system components directly, but user research and strategic design judgment still gate real product outcomes.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI design tools (Figma AI, Galileo) are credible and improving fast, not yet as embedded as coding copilots.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is spreading across tech-forward product teams but not yet a universal baseline.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can generate and iterate on component variations; user research and interaction strategy stay with designers.

tasksGrowing: User research and behavioral insight synthesis
AI interaction design and conversational UX
Strategic design thinking for ambiguous, high-stakes products
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1255 (Web and Digital Interface Designers), p25-p75

defenseSkills.skill: Directing AI Design Tools (Figma AI, Galileo)

roles.insight: AI can generate mockups and component variations fast, but understanding real user needs and designing for AI-human interaction remain judgment calls a model can't make on its own.

roles.title: AI Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: Prompt scaffolding, RAG pipeline wiring, and eval harness construction are largely AI-assisted today; agent safety design and failure-mode judgment are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Agent frameworks and LLM tooling are improving fast but still immature relative to mainstream coding tools -- patterns shift every few months.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is spreading quickly given competitive pressure to ship AI features, though best practices are still stabilizing.
rationales.agenticExposure: This role builds agentic systems, so meaningful chunks of its own scaffolding and eval work are themselves agent-ownable.

tasksAtRisk: Wiring standard RAG and retrieval pipelines
Writing prompt templates and few-shot scaffolding
Building eval harnesses for known task types
Integrating third-party model APIs into applications
tasksGrowing: Agent safety, guardrail, and failure-mode design
Orchestrating multi-agent systems for novel workflows
Cost, latency, and reliability tradeoffs across model providers
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1252 (Software Developers), p25-p75, extended for AI-specialist market postings
roles.insight: The tooling this role builds with is itself good enough to automate a meaningful share of its own scaffolding -- prompt templates, RAG wiring, eval harnesses. Engineers who own guardrail design and multi-agent orchestration for genuinely novel workflows are the ones the market is still short of.

roles.title: Platform Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: Golden-path templates, self-service tooling config, and standard internal-platform scaffolding are automatable; cross-team platform strategy is not.
rationales.toolMaturity: IaC and platform-engineering copilots are mature and increasingly standard in internal developer platform work.
rationales.adoption: Spreading among tech-forward firms investing in internal developer platforms to reduce cognitive load on product teams.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can maintain golden-path templates and CI/CD scaffolding; cross-team platform architecture decisions stay human.

tasksAtRisk: Standard CI/CD scaffolding for product teams
tasksGrowing: Cross-team platform strategy and adoption management
Balancing platform standardization against team autonomy
Cost and reliability architecture across the whole platform
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1252 (Software Developers, closest platform-engineering mapping), p25-p75
roles.insight: AI now maintains a lot of the golden-path templates and CI/CD scaffolding platform teams used to hand-tend. The differentiator is driving adoption across teams and making the standardization-versus-autonomy tradeoff correctly, which no template can decide.

roles.title: Data Scientist

rationales.taskAutomation: AutoML and agentic notebooks now run EDA, standard model builds, and visualization end-to-end; problem framing and stakeholder translation are not automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: AutoML platforms and LLM-assisted analysis tools are credible and improving fast, short of the maturity of mainstream coding copilots.
rationales.adoption: Common at large firms and tech-forward teams; spreading to mid-market analytics functions.
rationales.agenticExposure: Multi-step agents can now run full research-clean-model-report loops on structured data with human review at the end.

tasksAtRisk: Exploratory data analysis on structured datasets
Building standard ML models (regression, classification) via AutoML
Routine data visualization and dashboard generation
tasksGrowing: Framing ambiguous business problems as data problems
Designing novel ML approaches for unique challenges
Interpreting and defending results to non-technical stakeholders
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-2051 (Data Scientists), p25-p75
roles.insight: Agentic notebooks can now run an entire clean-model-report loop on structured data unattended, which pushes routine modeling toward a commodity task. Data scientists who frame the right problem, design the experiment, and translate results into a business decision remain the ones firms pay for.

roles.title: Machine Learning Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: Data pipeline glue code, training-loop boilerplate, and hyperparameter sweeps are automatable end-to-end; production architecture and model-risk judgment are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: MLOps platforms and AI coding copilots are credible and improving fast, though model-specific agent tooling still lags general-purpose coding tools.
rationales.adoption: Common at tech-forward firms building production ML systems; spreading to mid-market as MLOps platforms commoditize.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can already run training-pipeline automation and hyperparameter tuning; novel model design and production risk sign-off stay human.

tasksAtRisk: Writing data pipeline and feature-engineering boilerplate
Running standard training loops and hyperparameter sweeps
Building evaluation harnesses for known model types
Model deployment scaffolding and CI/CD for ML
tasksGrowing: Production model architecture and risk sign-off
Debugging novel model failure modes and data drift
MLOps platform strategy and cost/latency tradeoffs
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1252 (Software Developers), p25-p75, extended for ML-specialist market postings
roles.insight: Training pipelines and hyperparameter sweeps are now largely agent-run, but production risk sign-off and novel failure-mode debugging still require an engineer who understands the model, not just the framework. The premium is shifting toward engineers who can evaluate and govern what agents ship.

roles.title: Security Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: Security tooling configuration, automated scan remediation, and standard hardening scripts are automatable; security architecture and zero-day response are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted security engineering tools (automated remediation, policy-as-code generation) are mature and improving.
rationales.adoption: Common at tech-forward firms building security into CI/CD pipelines.
rationales.agenticExposure: Automated remediation and alert triage are agent-ownable; architecture decisions and novel exploit response stay human.

tasksAtRisk: Configuring standard security tooling and scanners
Automated remediation of known vulnerability classes
tasksGrowing: Security architecture for novel systems and AI applications
Zero-day and novel exploit response
Threat modeling for new product launches
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1212 (Information Security Analysts, closest engineering mapping), p25-p75
roles.insight: Automated remediation now closes most known vulnerability classes without an engineer touching a ticket. The differentiator is security architecture for genuinely new systems -- including the AI applications the rest of the org is shipping.

roles.title: Software Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: Boilerplate, tests and docs are automatable end-to-end; architecture and cross-team judgment are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Coding copilots and agentic dev tools are mature and embedded in the standard toolchain.
rationales.adoption: Most large employers now expect AI-assisted development in this function.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can own test maintenance and small scoped changes, not system design.

tasksGrowing: System architecture and cross-cutting design decisions
Directing and reviewing autonomous coding agents
Cross-team technical leadership
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1252 (Software Developers), p25-p75

defenseSkills.skill: Directing Agentic Coding Tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot Workspace)

roles.insight: AI coding agents now handle scaffolding, tests, and routine bug fixes end-to-end, but architecture, security tradeoffs, and cross-team judgment still gate the work that matters. The role is shifting from writing every line to directing and verifying what agents produce.

roles.title: Cybersecurity Analyst

rationales.taskAutomation: AI SOC copilots now triage alerts, correlate logs, and draft compliance reports end-to-end; novel-threat judgment and incident-response ownership still require an analyst.
rationales.toolMaturity: Vendor SOC copilots (Microsoft Security Copilot, CrowdStrike Charlotte AI) are credible and improving fast, but not yet as embedded as coding copilots.
rationales.adoption: Large enterprises and tech-forward security teams run AI triage in production; mid-market and regulated sectors lag.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can autonomously run tier-1 alert triage and vulnerability scan sweeps; threat hunting and incident command stay human-led.

tasksAtRisk: Tier-1 SOC alert triage and correlation
Routine vulnerability scanning and patch-priority reports
Compliance report drafting from scan output
tasksGrowing: Threat hunting for novel, non-signature attack vectors
AI system security -- prompt injection, model poisoning, data exfiltration defense
Incident response leadership and digital forensics
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1212 (Information Security Analysts), p25-p75
roles.insight: AI SOC copilots now clear most tier-1 alert volume, but they also expand the attack surface faster than they close it. Analysts who supervise agentic triage and specialize in AI-native threats -- prompt injection, model poisoning -- are pulling ahead of generalists.

roles.title: Game Developer

rationales.taskAutomation: Standard gameplay mechanics code, asset-pipeline scripting, and level-layout boilerplate are automatable; creative direction and novel gameplay design are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI codegen and procedural-content tools for game engines are credible and improving fast, adoption uneven across studio sizes.
rationales.adoption: Spreading among tech-forward and indie studios; large AAA studios adopt more cautiously due to pipeline complexity.
rationales.agenticExposure: Build pipelines, QA regression, and routine asset generation are agent-ownable; core creative and gameplay-feel decisions are not.

tasksAtRisk: Writing standard gameplay mechanics and systems code
Build and regression QA pipeline maintenance
tasksGrowing: Creative direction and novel gameplay design
Game feel, balance, and player-experience tuning
Technical direction for engine and performance architecture
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1252 (Software Developers, closest game-development mapping), p25-p75
roles.insight: AI now writes standard gameplay systems code and can generate a fair share of routine assets, but the creative judgment behind what makes a game actually feel good to play is still squarely a human skill.

roles.title: Network Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: Standard routing/switching configuration and templated deployments are automatable; complex network design and security-incident response are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Network automation tooling (Ansible/Terraform for networking, AI-assisted config generation) is credible and improving but less mature than server-side IaC.
rationales.adoption: Spreading in large enterprises and telecom-adjacent firms; slower in mid-market with legacy hardware estates.
rationales.agenticExposure: Config deployment and standard network monitoring are agent-ownable; outage diagnosis on unfamiliar topologies is not.

tasksAtRisk: Routine network monitoring and capacity reporting
tasksGrowing: Complex, multi-vendor network design
Network security incident response
SD-WAN and hybrid-cloud connectivity strategy
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1241 (Computer Network Architects, closest engineering-level mapping), p25-p75
roles.insight: AI can now generate standard router and switch configurations from a template, but multi-vendor network design and diagnosing an unfamiliar topology under outage pressure still need an engineer who has seen the failure mode before.

roles.title: Blockchain Developer

rationales.taskAutomation: Standard smart-contract templates and DApp scaffolding are AI-assistable; security auditing and novel protocol design are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generic coding copilots apply, but blockchain-specific tooling (contract auditing agents, formal verification) is still early.
rationales.adoption: Niche and early-adopter, concentrated in crypto-native and fintech-adjacent firms rather than mainstream enterprise.
rationales.agenticExposure: Contract test-suite generation and deployment scripting are agent-ownable; exploit-surface auditing is not.

tasksGrowing: Smart-contract security auditing and exploit analysis
Novel protocol and tokenomics design
Cross-chain interoperability architecture
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1252 (Software Developers, closest blockchain-development mapping), p25-p75
roles.insight: AI can now scaffold a standard smart contract quickly, which makes security auditing -- where a missed edge case costs real money on-chain -- the part of this role that is actually growing in value.

roles.title: Cloud Architect

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can draft standard reference architectures and IaC scaffolding, but vendor tradeoffs, cost governance, and enterprise-scale design decisions remain judgment calls.
rationales.toolMaturity: IaC generation and cloud-design copilots are credible and improving quickly but not yet a mature, near-solved capability.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is spreading across large enterprises undertaking cloud migration and modernization programs.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can generate and validate infrastructure templates against a given design; the design decision itself stays with the architect.

tasksAtRisk: Generating baseline IaC templates for common patterns
Producing cost-estimate spreadsheets for known workloads
tasksGrowing: Enterprise cloud strategy and vendor negotiation
Multi-cloud cost and resilience tradeoff decisions
Security and compliance architecture for regulated workloads
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1241 (Computer Network Architects, closest cloud-architecture mapping), p25-p75
roles.insight: AI can draft a reference architecture and the IaC to match it, but choosing between vendors, owning the cost/resilience tradeoff, and getting a regulated workload through security review still take an accountable architect.

roles.title: Database Architect

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can propose standard schema patterns and normalization, but enterprise data-strategy and cross-system architecture decisions are not automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted schema-design tools are credible and improving but not yet a mature, widely-shipped category.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is spreading among large firms doing platform-scale data architecture work.
rationales.agenticExposure: Schema generation and migration scripting from a stated design are agent-ownable; the architectural decision itself is not.

tasksAtRisk: Drafting standard schema and normalization patterns
tasksGrowing: Enterprise data strategy and cross-system architecture
Data governance and access-model design
Architecture for hybrid transactional/analytical/AI workloads
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1243 (Database Architects), p25-p75
roles.insight: AI can draft a reasonable schema for a well-understood problem, but the decisions that actually matter -- how data flows across systems, who governs it, and how it serves both transactional and AI workloads -- still need an accountable architect.

roles.title: Solutions Architect

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can draft standard integration diagrams and proposal boilerplate; enterprise stakeholder negotiation and architectural tradeoff decisions are not automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI diagramming and proposal-drafting tools are credible but still generic rather than purpose-built for enterprise solution design.
rationales.adoption: Early-to-mainstream adoption in enterprise consulting and pre-sales engineering functions.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can draft technical documentation and diagrams from a stated design; the design decision and client negotiation stay with the architect.

tasksAtRisk: Writing first-pass proposal and SOW boilerplate
tasksGrowing: Enterprise stakeholder negotiation and requirements discovery
Architectural tradeoff decisions under real constraints
Pre-sales technical strategy for complex, multi-vendor deals
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1211 (Computer Systems Analysts, closest solutions-architecture mapping), p25-p75
roles.insight: AI now handles the first draft of the diagram and the proposal document. What still requires a person is sitting across from a client, understanding what they actually need, and owning the tradeoff decision when the textbook answer doesn't fit their constraints.

roles.title: AI Research Scientist

rationales.taskAutomation: Literature synthesis and standard experiment scripting are AI-assistable; novel research direction and theoretical contribution are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI research-assistance tools (literature synthesis, experiment coding) are credible and improving quickly.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is concentrated in frontier AI labs and research-forward tech firms, not yet broad.
rationales.agenticExposure: Early self-driving-lab and agentic-experiment loops are emerging, giving agents a growing but still narrow slice of the work.

tasksAtRisk: Synthesizing literature reviews on established topics
Writing standard experiment and benchmarking code
tasksGrowing: Novel research direction and hypothesis generation
Theoretical contribution and model-architecture innovation
Designing and interpreting genuinely new experiments
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1221 (Computer and Information Research Scientists), p25-p75, extended for frontier-lab market postings

defenseSkills.skill: Agentic Experiment Pipelines & Self-Driving Research Loops

roles.insight: Agentic experiment loops are starting to run their own ablation studies and literature synthesis, but the research direction and theoretical insight that make a result matter still come from a scientist, not the loop.

roles.title: Embedded Systems Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: Driver boilerplate and standard firmware templates can be AI-assisted; hardware-software integration, timing, and safety-critical judgment dominate the role and are not automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI coding tools are less mature for low-level embedded C/C++ and hardware-in-the-loop workflows than for mainstream software stacks.
rationales.adoption: Early-adopter use only; embedded teams are conservative given safety-certification and hardware-cost constraints.
rationales.agenticExposure: Hardware-in-the-loop testing and physical debugging sharply limit how much of this role an agent can own end-to-end.

tasksAtRisk: Generating firmware templates for known microcontrollers
Routine unit testing of isolated firmware modules
Documentation of register maps and interfaces
tasksGrowing: Hardware-software integration and real-time timing debugging
Safety-critical and certification-driven design (automotive, medical, aerospace)
Power and performance optimization on constrained hardware
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2061 (Computer Hardware Engineers, closest embedded-systems mapping), p25-p75
roles.insight: This is one of the least agent-exposed engineering roles today: AI tooling for low-level, hardware-coupled firmware still lags mainstream software copilots badly, and physical hardware-in-the-loop debugging keeps a person firmly in the loop.

roles.title: IT Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Status reporting and budget/vendor documentation are AI-assistable, but team leadership, vendor negotiation, and strategic prioritization dominate the role and are not automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generic AI productivity tools apply to reporting and documentation; no purpose-built management-automation product has real traction.
rationales.adoption: Common for AI-assisted reporting and documentation tasks; the management core of the role remains untouched.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agentic exposure is low -- this is fundamentally a people-management and prioritization role, not a digital-pipeline role.

tasksAtRisk: Drafting routine status and budget reports
Vendor documentation and contract summary prep
tasksGrowing: Team leadership, hiring, and performance management
Vendor negotiation and technology-investment strategy
Cross-functional prioritization under budget constraints
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-3021 (Computer and Information Systems Managers), p25-p75

defenseSkills.skill: Team Leadership Through an AI Transition

roles.insight: AI now drafts the status reports and vendor summaries that used to eat a manager's week, but leading a team and deciding where to invest a shrinking headcount budget is still an entirely human call.

roles.title: Computer Hardware Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: Standard board schematics and routine test-script writing can be AI-assisted; novel chip and system design is not automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted EDA and simulation tools are credible but hardware-design AI remains far less mature than software coding tools.
rationales.adoption: Early-adopter use only; hardware design cycles and fabrication costs make teams cautious about unproven tooling.
rationales.agenticExposure: Physical prototyping and lab validation sharply limit how much of this role can be agent-owned end-to-end.

tasksAtRisk: Drafting standard board schematics from known references
tasksGrowing: Novel chip and system architecture design
Physical prototyping and lab validation
Power, thermal, and manufacturing tradeoff decisions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2061 (Computer Hardware Engineers), p25-p75
roles.insight: AI-assisted simulation and EDA tools can speed up routine schematic work, but hardware design still lives or dies in physical prototyping and lab validation that no model can substitute for.

roles.title: Tax Preparer

rationales.taskAutomation: Standard individual returns, document data entry, and e-filing are now overwhelmingly automatable end to end; only multi-entity, international, or controversy work needs a licensed preparer.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-driven tax software (TurboTax AI, professional e-file platforms with embedded LLM review) is mature and near-commodity for standard returns.
rationales.adoption: Mainstream across both DIY consumer platforms and professional prep chains for standard individual returns.
rationales.agenticExposure: Document intake, data entry, and e-file submission is a fully agent-traversable pipeline for the large majority of individual returns.

tasksGrowing: Complex multi-entity tax planning
International tax strategy
Tax controversy and IRS representation
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-2082 (Tax Preparers), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI tax software now files the majority of standard individual returns with minimal human input, and the segment keeps shrinking. Preparers who specialize in multi-state, international, or business tax planning are the ones with a growing practice left.

roles.title: Insurance Underwriter

rationales.taskAutomation: Standard personal-lines risk scoring, premium calculation, and renewal processing are now largely automated end to end; only complex commercial and emerging risks require human underwriting judgment.
rationales.toolMaturity: Insurtech underwriting engines (Lemonade, Root, and commercial-carrier AI risk platforms) are mature, production-deployed products, not pilots.
rationales.adoption: Mainstream across personal-lines carriers and rapidly spreading into standard commercial underwriting.
rationales.agenticExposure: Application intake, risk scoring, and renewal decisioning form a full agent-ownable pipeline for standard policies; only specialty and catastrophic risk stays with a human.

tasksAtRisk: Standard risk assessment on common policies
tasksGrowing: Complex commercial and specialty underwriting
Emerging risk evaluation (climate, cyber, AI)
Broker relationship management and deal structuring
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-2053 (Insurance Underwriters), p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI risk modeling and insurtech platforms

roles.insight: AI now underwrites the overwhelming majority of standard personal lines with no human touch. The career path forward is complex commercial risk, emerging categories like cyber and AI liability, and broker relationships that carriers still need a person to run.

roles.title: Bank Teller

rationales.taskAutomation: Deposits, withdrawals, transfers, and basic account inquiries are routine transaction processing that ATMs, mobile apps, and AI chat/voice banking already handle at scale.
rationales.toolMaturity: Mobile banking, ITMs (interactive teller machines), and AI chat banking are mature, widely deployed products across nearly all major banks.
rationales.adoption: Mainstream: most routine transactions have already shifted to self-service and mobile channels at large and mid-size banks.
rationales.agenticExposure: Routine transaction handling and basic account servicing form a scripted interaction pipeline agents and self-service kiosks already own.

tasksAtRisk: Basic account balance and transaction inquiries
tasksGrowing: Cross-selling complex financial products in person
Resolving complex or disputed transaction issues
Community banking relationship-building
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-3071 (Tellers), p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Complex product cross-selling and financial guidance

roles.insight: Branch teller headcount has been shrinking for over a decade as mobile and ATM/ITM channels absorb routine transactions, and AI-driven self-service accelerates that shift further.

roles.title: Loan Officer

rationales.taskAutomation: Pre-qualification, document collection, standard-profile credit analysis, and compliance checklists now run through automated lending pipelines end to end; complex and relationship-driven lending doesn't.
rationales.toolMaturity: Fintech lending platforms (Rocket Mortgage AI, online-lender underwriting engines) are mature and embedded in most retail and mortgage lending operations.
rationales.adoption: Mainstream at online lenders and large banks, spreading fast into community and credit-union lending.
rationales.agenticExposure: Intake-to-prequalification-to-approval for standard consumer loans is a fully agent-traversable pipeline today.

tasksAtRisk: Rate comparison and loan product matching
tasksGrowing: Complex lending scenarios (commercial, construction)
Client relationship building and financial counseling
Portfolio risk management and strategy
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-2072 (Loan Officers), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Online lenders now originate most standard consumer loans with essentially no loan officer involvement. Officers who build deep client relationships and master complex commercial or construction lending remain the ones borrowers and banks still need.

roles.title: Accountant

rationales.taskAutomation: Transaction categorization, invoice matching, reconciliation, and monthly close now run end-to-end through AI bookkeeping tools; tax strategy, audit judgment, and client advisory still require a licensed human sign-off.
rationales.toolMaturity: Vic.ai, Botkeeper, and QuickBooks' embedded AI now auto-code and reconcile the large majority of routine transactions in production, not pilot, use.
rationales.adoption: Mainstream expectation at firms of all sizes now runs AI-assisted bookkeeping and close, with mid-market and small-firm adoption catching up fast to large-firm norms.
rationales.agenticExposure: Categorization-reconciliation-close is a closed-loop, verifiable pipeline that agentic tools can own with human review only on exceptions.

tasksGrowing: Complex, multi-entity tax strategy and planning
Forensic accounting and fraud detection
Business advisory and CFO-style financial planning
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-2011 (Accountants and Auditors), p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI accounting platforms (Vic.ai, Botkeeper, QBO AI)

roles.insight: Routine bookkeeping, reconciliation, and standard tax prep are now largely agent-run rather than merely assisted. Accountants who move up into advisory, complex multi-entity tax strategy, or forensic work keep pricing power; those who stay in pure transaction processing compete directly with software.

roles.title: Bookkeeper

rationales.taskAutomation: Ledger entry, bank reconciliation, invoice matching, and payroll data entry are routine digital processing that AI now performs end to end at professional quality.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI bookkeeping platforms (Botkeeper, QuickBooks AI, Xero AI) are mature, widely shipped products already embedded in most small-business accounting stacks.
rationales.adoption: Near-mainstream among small and mid-size businesses that outsource bookkeeping, driven by accounting-firm cost pressure to automate low-margin work.
rationales.agenticExposure: Categorize-reconcile-report is a fully closed loop; an agent can run the entire monthly cycle with a human reviewing only flagged exceptions.

tasksGrowing: Client advisory on cash flow and budgeting
Cleanup and audit-readiness projects
Managing AI tool exceptions and edge cases
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-3031 (Bookkeeping, Accounting, and Auditing Clerks), p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI bookkeeping platform administration (Botkeeper, QBO AI)

roles.insight: Bookkeeping is one of the most fully agent-automatable roles in finance today: categorization, reconciliation, and reporting now run with minimal human input. The path forward is overseeing the AI, handling exceptions, and moving into client-facing advisory work the software can't do.

roles.title: Auditor

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can now test full transaction populations instead of samples and draft standard workpapers, but assessing novel risk, fraud intent, and going-concern judgment remains a human call.
rationales.toolMaturity: Continuous-audit and anomaly-detection tools (data analytics platforms like ACL/IDEA plus LLM-based workpaper drafting) are production-deployed at most mid-size and large firms.
rationales.adoption: 100%-population testing and AI-assisted workpaper drafting are now standard practice at large firms and spreading through mid-market audit practices.
rationales.agenticExposure: Sampling, testing, and reconciliation form an agent-traversable pipeline; full-population continuous auditing is already agent-run at scale in production.

tasksAtRisk: Sampling and testing of routine transactions
tasksGrowing: Risk assessment for complex, novel scenarios
AI system and algorithmic accountability auditing
Fraud investigation and forensic analysis
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-2011 (Accountants and Auditors), p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Data analytics for audit (ACL, IDEA, continuous audit tools)

roles.insight: Full-population continuous auditing has moved from pilot to default at large firms, replacing sample-based testing outright. Auditors who specialize in judgment-heavy risk assessment, AI-system auditing, or fraud analytics are the ones clients still pay a premium for.

roles.title: Financial Analyst

rationales.taskAutomation: Model-building from templates, data pulls, standard dashboards, and recurring variance analysis are now largely automatable; strategic interpretation and deal judgment are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-powered modeling and data-aggregation tools (Copilot in Excel, AlphaSense, Datarails) are mature and standard in most finance toolchains.
rationales.adoption: Mainstream at finance-forward sectors and increasingly default across corporate FP&A and investment research teams.
rationales.agenticExposure: Data-pull-to-dashboard-to-variance-commentary is a closed loop agents can run end to end, escalating only genuine anomalies.

tasksGrowing: Strategic financial advisory for executives
Scenario planning for unprecedented events
M&A due diligence and deal structuring
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-2051 (Financial and Investment Analysts), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now builds the model and pulls the data in minutes; the analysts who keep their seat are the ones translating the output into a judgment call an executive can act on under real uncertainty.

roles.title: Budget Analyst

rationales.taskAutomation: Variance reporting, trend analysis, and cost-allocation math are largely templated digital output; only cross-department negotiation and strategic allocation calls resist automation.
rationales.toolMaturity: FP&A platforms with embedded forecasting AI (Planful, Vena, Datarails) are mature and widely deployed for standard variance and budget reporting.
rationales.adoption: Common practice at large firms and increasingly standard in mid-market finance teams and government budget offices.
rationales.agenticExposure: Recurring variance-report-and-forecast cycles are a scoped, verifiable workflow an agent can own between human budget reviews.

tasksGrowing: Strategic resource allocation advisory
Cross-departmental budget negotiation
AI-driven financial planning and scenario modeling
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-2031 (Budget Analysts), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now closes the variance-report-to-forecast loop with minimal input, but budget analysts who can advise leadership on trade-offs and broker cross-department negotiation still set the agenda.

roles.title: Claims Adjuster

rationales.taskAutomation: Standard auto and property claims now get photo-based damage assessment and automated payout end to end; complex, contested, or catastrophic claims still need adjuster judgment.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI damage-assessment and touchless-claims platforms (Tractable, Lemonade AI) are production-deployed at major carriers, not pilots.
rationales.adoption: Mainstream among large auto and property insurers for standard claims; complex commercial and catastrophe claims remain human-led.
rationales.agenticExposure: Intake-photo assessment-payout is a textbook agent-ownable claims pipeline, explicitly the kind of workflow autonomous agents now traverse end to end.

tasksAtRisk: Photo-based damage assessment for standard auto/property claims
tasksGrowing: Complex, contested, or catastrophic claims investigation
Fraud detection escalation and case-building
Policyholder advocacy in disputed claims
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1031 (Claims Adjusters, Examiners, and Investigators), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Touchless claims already settle most straightforward auto and property losses without a human adjuster. The remaining career path is in complex, contested, and catastrophic claims where judgment and negotiation still decide the outcome.

roles.title: Business Analyst

rationales.taskAutomation: Requirements docs, process maps, and user stories now draft themselves from meeting transcripts and templates, but stakeholder alignment and organizational judgment still gate delivery.
rationales.toolMaturity: LLM-based meeting-to-requirements tools and BI copilots are mature and embedded in standard analyst toolchains (Jira/Confluence AI, BI copilots).
rationales.adoption: Widely adopted in tech and finance, spreading to other sectors as AI-native product and delivery tooling becomes default.
rationales.agenticExposure: Turning meeting notes into requirements and data queries into reports is a scoped, repeatable loop an agent can run with human review of the output.

tasksGrowing: Stakeholder negotiation and alignment
Complex process redesign with AI integration
Product-market fit analysis and validation
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1111 (Management Analysts), p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI process automation and agent orchestration

roles.insight: AI now drafts requirements and pulls reports directly from meetings and data, but reading organizational politics and redesigning workflows around AI remain distinctly human work.

roles.title: Credit Analyst

rationales.taskAutomation: Algorithmic credit scoring and standard financial-statement analysis are now largely automated; complex commercial credit judgment and covenant negotiation are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-driven credit scoring and financial-statement-analysis tools are mature and standard in retail and small-business lending pipelines.
rationales.adoption: Mainstream in consumer and small-business lending; commercial credit decisioning adopts more slowly given deal complexity.
rationales.agenticExposure: Standard credit-file review and scoring is an agent-ownable pipeline; complex commercial covenant analysis needs a human underwriter.

tasksAtRisk: Standard credit scoring and risk-rating calculation
Financial statement ratio analysis for routine borrowers
Credit report data verification and compilation
tasksGrowing: Complex commercial credit and covenant analysis
Industry and portfolio risk trend assessment
Client-facing credit structuring advisory
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-2041 (Credit Analysts), p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Complex commercial credit and covenant analysis

roles.insight: Algorithmic scoring already handles most consumer and small-business credit decisions. Analysts who move into complex commercial deals and portfolio-level risk strategy keep the work software can't yet do.

roles.title: Compliance Officer

rationales.taskAutomation: Regulatory-change monitoring, checklist reviews, and template policy drafting are largely automatable, but interpreting novel regulation and building compliance culture stay human-gated.
rationales.toolMaturity: RegTech platforms for reg-change tracking and policy drafting are credible and improving fast but not yet uniformly embedded across compliance functions.
rationales.adoption: Common at large regulated firms (finance, healthcare); adoption lags in smaller and less-regulated organizations.
rationales.agenticExposure: Monitoring regulatory feeds and tracking training completion are agent-ownable back-office slices; regulatory judgment calls are not.

tasksGrowing: AI governance and ethical AI policy development
Complex regulatory interpretation and strategy
Building compliance culture across organizations
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1041 (Compliance Officers), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI regulation is exploding worldwide, and monitoring/checklist work is automating fast, but every one of those new rules needs a human who can interpret it and build the internal case for compliance. AI-governance specialists are the fastest-growing niche in the field.

roles.title: Fraud Investigator

rationales.taskAutomation: AI transaction-monitoring flags the large majority of anomalies automatically, but confirming intent, interviewing subjects, and building a legally sound case require human investigation.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI/ML transaction-monitoring and AML platforms are mature and production-deployed at most banks and payment processors.
rationales.adoption: Common at large financial institutions and payment processors; adoption lags at smaller institutions with legacy systems.
rationales.agenticExposure: Anomaly detection, alert triage, and case-file assembly are agent-ownable; interviewing and legal case-building are not.

tasksAtRisk: Transaction anomaly flagging and alert triage
Pattern analysis across large transaction datasets
tasksGrowing: Subject interviews and interrogation
Building legally sound cases for prosecution/civil action
Investigating novel fraud typologies (deepfake, synthetic identity)
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-2099 (Financial Specialists, All Other), p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Emerging fraud typology analysis (deepfake, synthetic ID)

roles.insight: AI now catches the overwhelming majority of anomalies automatically, which is actually growing the investigator role: someone still has to confirm intent, interview the subject, and build a case that holds up, and fraud volume keeps climbing.

roles.title: Financial Advisor

rationales.taskAutomation: Robo-advisors handle basic portfolio allocation and rebalancing well, but comprehensive planning, tax-aware decisions, and behavioral coaching through market stress require trust a client extends to a person.
rationales.toolMaturity: Robo-advisory and portfolio-rebalancing engines (Betterment, Schwab Intelligent Portfolios) are mature, widely deployed products.
rationales.adoption: Roughly half of advisory practices now use AI tools for portfolio construction and client reporting, short of universal adoption.
rationales.agenticExposure: Rebalancing and routine portfolio monitoring are agent-ownable; the client relationship and life-event planning around them are not.

tasksAtRisk: Routine client reporting and performance summaries
tasksGrowing: Comprehensive financial planning across life events
Behavioral coaching during market volatility
Complex tax, estate, and business-succession planning
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-2052 (Personal Financial Advisors), p25-p75 rounded; high end reflects commission/AUM-based earnings at senior levels exceeding OES base
roles.insight: Robo-advisors have commoditized basic portfolio management, but the advisors who keep and grow books of business are the ones clients call during a market crash or a life event, not the ones who just rebalance a portfolio.

roles.title: Real Estate Appraiser

rationales.taskAutomation: Automated valuation models (AVMs) handle standard comps and pricing well, but licensed on-site inspection and defensible valuation judgment remain regulatory requirements for most mortgage lending.
rationales.toolMaturity: AVMs (Zillow Zestimate-class tools, lender-integrated valuation engines) are mature and widely deployed for preliminary valuation.
rationales.adoption: Common as a first-pass tool at lenders; regulatory appraisal-waiver programs are expanding but a licensed appraiser is still required for a large share of mortgages.
rationales.agenticExposure: Comp-pulling and preliminary valuation are agent-ownable; certified on-site inspection and sign-off are not.

tasksAtRisk: Comparable sales research and adjustment calculations
tasksGrowing: Complex or unique property valuation requiring on-site judgment
Litigation and dispute-related appraisal work
AVM accuracy review and exception handling
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-2023 (Appraisers and Assessors of Real Estate), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Appraisal-waiver programs and automated valuation models are eroding standard-appraisal volume steadily, but licensed sign-off on complex or disputed properties still requires a certified human on site.

roles.title: Management Consultant

rationales.taskAutomation: AI drafts research summaries, benchmarks, and slide decks fast, but the custom strategic judgment, client trust, and change-management work clients actually pay for is not automatable end to end.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI research and deck-generation tools (Gamma, AlphaSense, internal firm copilots) are credible and improving quickly at top consultancies.
rationales.adoption: Common at major consulting firms; spreading to boutique and mid-market firms as AI-native delivery becomes a client expectation.
rationales.agenticExposure: Research-gather-benchmark-draft is an agent-ownable loop; the strategic recommendation and stakeholder buy-in around it are not.

tasksGrowing: Client relationship management and trust-building
Change management and organizational transformation
Custom strategy for novel business challenges
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1111 (Management Analysts), p25-p75 rounded; high end reflects MBB/Big 4 market postings above the OES median
roles.insight: AI now handles research and deck-building faster than any analyst team, but clients still pay for judgment, relationships, and someone accountable for the outcome. Consultants who specialize in AI transformation itself are in peak demand.

roles.title: Credit Counselor

rationales.taskAutomation: Budget calculations and template debt-management plans are automatable, but empathetic counseling and negotiating directly with creditors on a client's behalf are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Basic budgeting and debt-calculator tools are widely available but generic; nothing purpose-built has displaced counselor-led negotiation.
rationales.adoption: Early-to-mid adoption: nonprofit and for-profit credit counseling agencies use basic AI tools for intake, not for the counseling itself.
rationales.agenticExposure: Budget-plan generation is an agent-ownable back-office slice; the counseling conversation and creditor negotiation are not.

tasksAtRisk: Budget calculation and debt-management plan generation
Client financial data intake and documentation
tasksGrowing: Empathetic financial counseling and behavior change coaching
Direct negotiation with creditors
Complex bankruptcy and debt-relief case guidance
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-2071 (Credit Counselors), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Debt-plan math is easy to automate, but people in financial distress come to a credit counselor for a person who will negotiate on their behalf and help them change behavior, not a calculator.

roles.title: Real Estate Agent

rationales.taskAutomation: Listing descriptions, comparable-sales pulls, and showing scheduling are automatable, but in-person showings, negotiation, and local market judgment dominate the actual work.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-driven listing platforms and automated valuation tools (Zillow, Redfin AI, AI-generated listing copy) are mature and widely used.
rationales.adoption: Common among tech-forward brokerages and national portals; independent agents adopt more slowly.
rationales.agenticExposure: Lead qualification, showing scheduling, and comp generation are agent-ownable slices; closing a deal in person is not.

tasksAtRisk: Listing description and marketing copy generation
tasksGrowing: In-person negotiation and deal closing
Hyper-local market expertise and pricing strategy
Client relationship management through the transaction
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 41-9022 (Real Estate Sales Agents), p25-p75 rounded; commission-based income makes ranges highly variable by market
roles.insight: AI now writes the listing and pulls the comps, but buying and selling a home is still an emotional, high-stakes, in-person negotiation. Agents who lean into local expertise and relationship management outcompete discount and AI-only listing models.

roles.title: Financial Risk Specialist

rationales.taskAutomation: AI runs stress-test and VaR calculations quickly, but model validation, regulatory interpretation, and risk-appetite judgment calls require certified specialist sign-off.
rationales.toolMaturity: Quantitative risk-modeling platforms are credible and improving fast, increasingly used for scenario generation ahead of specialist review.
rationales.adoption: Common at large banks and asset managers; growing further as AI model risk itself becomes a regulatory priority.
rationales.agenticExposure: Running scenario batches and stress-test calculations is agent-ownable; validating and signing off on model risk is not.

tasksGrowing: AI/ML model risk validation and governance
Regulatory capital strategy
Novel risk-scenario design (climate, geopolitical, AI-driven)
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-2054 (Financial Risk Specialists), p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI/ML model risk validation and governance

roles.insight: Regulators now treat AI/ML model risk as a top priority, which is turning financial risk specialists into the certified check on every automated pricing and credit model a bank deploys.

roles.title: Financial Examiner

rationales.taskAutomation: AI accelerates data review during bank and institution exams, but enforcement judgment, regulatory interpretation, and examiner sign-off are statutorily human responsibilities.
rationales.toolMaturity: Data-analytics tools for exam prep exist but are not yet purpose-built or mature for regulatory examination work specifically.
rationales.adoption: Limited: government and regulatory examination functions adopt new tooling slowly given statutory and procedural constraints.
rationales.agenticExposure: Compiling exam data and flagging exceptions is agent-ownable prep work; the certified finding and enforcement action are not.

tasksAtRisk: Financial data compilation and exception flagging
Standard compliance checklist review during exams
Routine call-report and filing data verification
tasksGrowing: Enforcement judgment and regulatory interpretation
Examining novel risks (crypto, AI-driven lending) at institutions
Cross-agency coordination on systemic risk
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-2061 (Financial Examiners), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Examination data review is getting faster with AI, but the statutory finding and enforcement decision stay with a certified examiner, and new risk categories like crypto and AI-driven lending are expanding the job, not shrinking it.

roles.title: Actuary

rationales.taskAutomation: AI accelerates data prep and preliminary modeling, but certified actuarial judgment, regulatory sign-off, and pricing/reserving decisions under professional liability remain human-owned.
rationales.toolMaturity: Predictive modeling and machine-learning tools for pricing and reserving are improving fast but still require actuarial validation before production use.
rationales.adoption: Early-to-mid adoption: larger insurers and reinsurers use AI-assisted modeling; broad industry adoption of AI in certified actuarial sign-off is still limited.
rationales.agenticExposure: Data assembly and preliminary scenario runs are agent-ownable; the certified opinion and regulatory filing are not.

tasksAtRisk: Data assembly and cleaning for pricing models
tasksGrowing: Novel risk modeling (climate, longevity, cyber)
Regulatory strategy and certified opinion work
AI model validation and governance within actuarial functions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-2011 (Actuaries), p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Predictive modeling for novel risk (climate, cyber)

roles.insight: Actuarial work is professionally licensed and carries personal liability for the certified opinion, which keeps AI in an assistive role even as modeling tools improve quickly. Actuaries who also validate AI/ML risk models are becoming the bridge between data science and regulatory-grade certification.

roles.title: Purchasing Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Spend analysis and routine reordering are automatable, but supplier negotiation, contract strategy, and team leadership are managerial judgment work that resists automation.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI spend-analytics and procurement-automation tools are credible and improving but sit alongside, not replace, manager-led sourcing strategy.
rationales.adoption: Common in large-firm procurement functions for spend analytics; supplier negotiation itself stays manager-led almost everywhere.
rationales.agenticExposure: Routine reordering and spend-pattern analysis are agent-ownable; supplier relationship strategy and team management are not.

tasksGrowing: Supplier relationship negotiation and strategy
Supply chain risk management
Team leadership and cross-functional sourcing strategy
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-3061 (Purchasing Managers), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now surfaces spend patterns and reorder points automatically, but negotiating supplier terms and managing a sourcing team under real supply-chain risk stays a human judgment call.

roles.title: Economist

rationales.taskAutomation: AI accelerates literature review, data pulls, and preliminary regressions, but original research design, causal interpretation, and policy judgment resist end-to-end automation.
rationales.toolMaturity: LLM research assistants and statistical-modeling copilots are useful but not purpose-built or fully reliable for original economic research.
rationales.adoption: Early adopters only: research economists experiment with AI for literature review and coding, but core analysis stays human-led.
rationales.agenticExposure: Data gathering and preliminary statistical runs are agent-ownable prep work; the research question and policy interpretation are not.

tasksGrowing: Original research design and causal analysis
Policy interpretation and recommendation
Communicating complex findings to non-technical stakeholders
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 19-3011 (Economists), p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Original research design and causal inference

roles.insight: AI speeds up the literature review and the first regression, but the value of an economist is still in the research question and the judgment call about what the numbers actually mean for policy.

roles.title: Investment Banker

rationales.taskAutomation: AI drafts pitch materials and runs comps quickly, but originating and closing deals depends on client trust, negotiation, and judgment under real financial stakes that resist automation.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted pitch-deck and comparable-company analysis tools exist and are improving, but nothing purpose-built has displaced core deal work.
rationales.adoption: Early adopters only: leading banks pilot AI for research and deck support, but deal execution stays fully human-run.
rationales.agenticExposure: Comp-pulling and first-draft materials are agent-ownable prep work; client-facing deal origination and negotiation are not.

tasksAtRisk: Comparable company and precedent transaction analysis
First-draft pitch book and deck creation
Financial model scaffolding and data pulls
tasksGrowing: Client relationship and deal origination
Complex deal structuring and negotiation
Regulatory and cross-border transaction strategy
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 41-3031 (Securities, Commodities, and Financial Services Sales Agents), p25-p75 rounded; high end reflects analyst/associate base+bonus market postings well above the broader OES median

defenseSkills.skill: Deal origination and client relationship management

roles.insight: AI now drafts the pitch book in a fraction of the time, but the job has always really been about winning and closing the mandate, which is still a trust-and-negotiation business.

roles.title: Telemarketer

rationales.taskAutomation: Scripted outbound calling, basic objection handling, and appointment setting are routine, script-driven interactions current voice AI performs end-to-end at professional quality.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI voice-agent platforms for outbound calling are mature and already run production call volumes for many outbound campaigns.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is spreading fast in large call centers and lead-gen shops but is held back in regulated verticals by consent and TCPA compliance requirements.
rationales.agenticExposure: Scripted outbound calling is close to a pure agent spec: dial, pitch, log, and route the qualified lead, with a human needed only for escalations.

tasksAtRisk: Cold outbound calling from a script
Basic objection handling and FAQ responses
Call outcome logging in the CRM
Lead list dialing and follow-up cadences
tasksGrowing: Complex, regulated-product sales requiring disclosures
Escalation handling for upset or hesitant prospects
Script and offer design for new campaigns
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 41-9041 Telemarketers, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Scripted cold calling is now close to a pure agent job: an AI voice agent dials, pitches, and logs outcomes at a fraction of the cost. The remaining human seats sit in regulated products and campaigns where a compliance disclosure or an angry prospect needs a person on the line, and even those seats are shrinking as call-center operators consolidate onto voice-agent platforms.

roles.title: Content Writer

rationales.taskAutomation: First drafts, repurposing, listicles, and calendar planning are routine digital production that current models complete end-to-end at publishable quality most of the time.
rationales.toolMaturity: LLM drafting tools are commodity-grade and embedded directly in CMSs, briefs, and editorial workflows.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted drafting is now a mainstream expectation for content teams, not a pilot program.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can run the research-draft-revise loop across a content calendar largely unsupervised, though it still needs a human to originate reporting and set editorial direction.

tasksGrowing: Original reporting and expert interviews
Thought leadership with a distinct point of view
Content strategy and editorial direction
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-3043 Writers and Authors, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Generic drafting is now a commodity an agent can run start to finish across a full content calendar. Writers left standing are the ones who bring reporting AI can't originate, subject expertise it can't fake, and editorial judgment over what an agent produces.

roles.title: Email Marketing Specialist

rationales.taskAutomation: Template building, subject-line testing, standard segmentation, and drip-sequence writing are digital production tasks AI tools now execute end-to-end.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-native platforms like Klaviyo and Braze ship mature generation, segmentation, and send-time optimization as embedded features.
rationales.adoption: AI-driven email automation is now the mainstream default across ecommerce and SaaS lifecycle marketing.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can own the full sequence build-test-send loop and deliverability monitoring, though revenue-attribution strategy and lifecycle architecture still need a human owner.

tasksAtRisk: List segmentation based on standard criteria
tasksGrowing: Advanced personalization strategy
Email deliverability and reputation management
Revenue attribution and lifecycle optimization
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1161 Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Build, test, and send is now a loop an agent runs unattended inside modern ESPs. Specialists who own deliverability health and revenue attribution across the lifecycle stay indispensable.

roles.title: Media Buyer

rationales.taskAutomation: Bid management, budget pacing, and placement reporting are digital, rules-driven tasks that programmatic systems now execute end-to-end.
rationales.toolMaturity: Demand-side platforms and AI bid-optimization tools are mature, commodity-grade products standard across the ad-buying industry.
rationales.adoption: Programmatic, AI-managed buying is near-universal across digital advertising, making this one of the most automated marketing functions today.
rationales.agenticExposure: Bid placement, budget reallocation, and pacing reports form a pipeline of digital decisions an agent can traverse continuously across campaigns.

tasksAtRisk: Inventory and rate negotiation for standard buys
tasksGrowing: Upfront and custom-deal negotiation with publishers
Cross-channel media mix strategy
Fraud and brand-safety oversight
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 41-3011 Advertising Sales Agents (closest match for ad-placement transactions), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Bid pacing and reporting now run almost entirely inside AI-managed DSPs. The remaining work is negotiating the deals platforms can't auto-execute and catching the fraud and brand-safety issues an algorithm misses.

roles.title: SEO Specialist

rationales.taskAutomation: Keyword research, meta-tag optimization, technical audits, and content briefs are digital, tool-driven tasks AI now runs end-to-end.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI SEO platforms like Surfer and Clearscope are mature, widely-shipped products embedded in the standard SEO workflow.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted audits and keyword research are now the mainstream default across SEO teams.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run technical audits and content-brief generation loops unsupervised, and AI search itself is reshaping what the role even optimizes for.

tasksGrowing: AI search optimization (AI Overviews, Perplexity)
Content strategy for zero-click search
Technical implementation and site architecture
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1161 Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Audits and keyword clustering now run on agents, and the audience is shifting from search results to AI answers. Specialists who master AI-search visibility and can still touch the technical stack remain in demand.

roles.title: Copywriter

rationales.taskAutomation: Product descriptions, subject lines, ad variants, and landing-page copy from a brief are high-volume, formulaic outputs AI now produces reliably at scale.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI writing tools are mature, commodity products embedded directly in ad platforms, CMSs, and ecommerce catalogs.
rationales.adoption: Most marketing and ecommerce teams already generate first-pass copy with AI as standard practice.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can generate and A/B-test copy variants across a catalog unsupervised, but brand-defining and high-stakes launch copy still needs a human author.

tasksAtRisk: Email subject lines and preview text
tasksGrowing: Brand voice development and guidelines
High-stakes persuasive copy (sales pages, launches)
Creative campaign concepts and storytelling
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-3043 Writers and Authors, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Catalog and variant copy is now an agent job, run and A/B-tested at volumes no human team could match. Copywriters who survive own the brand voice an agent works from and the high-stakes launch copy nobody trusts it to write alone.

roles.title: Growth Marketer

rationales.taskAutomation: Campaign setup, ad-copy variant generation, and funnel analytics are digital, data-driven tasks AI now runs at professional quality across most channels.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI ad platforms, auto-experimentation tools, and analytics dashboards are mature and embedded in the standard growth stack.
rationales.adoption: Growth teams are among the earliest and most consistent adopters of AI-assisted acquisition and experimentation tools.
rationales.agenticExposure: The design-run-analyze experiment loop is substantially agent-traversable, with a human needed mainly to set strategic bets and interpret ambiguous results.

tasksAtRisk: Ad copy and creative variant generation
A/B test execution and statistical analysis
