tasksGrowing: Experiment roadmap and strategic bet selection
Cross-channel growth-loop design
Interpreting ambiguous or conflicting test results
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1161 Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: The build-launch-measure experiment loop is now something an agent runs continuously. Growth marketers who pick the right bets and read ambiguous results correctly are the ones still worth the seat.

roles.title: Social Media Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Caption writing, scheduling, basic graphics, and competitor monitoring are routine digital production AI now handles across most channels.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI content-creation and scheduling tools are mature, widely-shipped products embedded in the standard social workflow.
rationales.adoption: AI-generated posts and scheduling are now the mainstream default for social teams across brands of every size.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run the schedule-post-monitor loop across platforms unsupervised, but real-time community engagement and crisis response stay human.

tasksGrowing: Community building and crisis management
Influencer relationship strategy
Brand voice development and audience insight
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1161 Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Posting, captioning, and basic reporting now run on agents around the clock. Managers who own real-time community trust and crisis response are the ones a brand still can't automate away.

roles.title: Market Research Analyst

rationales.taskAutomation: Survey fielding, cross-tabulation, competitive monitoring, and standard market-sizing are digital-data tasks that AI tools now handle at professional quality most of the time.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-native survey and analytics platforms are credible, fast-improving role-specific products, though not yet as universally embedded as coding or transcription tools.
rationales.adoption: Large firms and tech-forward research shops have made AI-assisted analytics standard, with mid-market adoption still catching up.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run the pull-tabulate-monitor loop for competitive and quantitative tracking, but framing a novel research question and reading qualitative nuance stay human.

tasksGrowing: Qualitative research and customer empathy
Strategic insight generation for leadership
Research design for novel questions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1161 Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Tabulating data and tracking competitors is now agent work. Analysts who ask the sharper research question and translate findings into a strategic story for leadership are the ones that stay in the room.

roles.title: PR Specialist

rationales.taskAutomation: Press release drafting, media list building, and coverage monitoring are digital, template-driven tasks AI tools now handle for most routine releases.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI media-monitoring and drafting tools are mature, widely-shipped products embedded in standard PR workflows.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted monitoring and drafting is now a mainstream expectation across corporate and agency PR teams.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run the monitor-flag-draft loop for routine coverage and releases, but crisis judgment and journalist relationships stay human.

tasksGrowing: Crisis communication and reputation management
Journalist relationship building
Strategic narrative development
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-3031 Public Relations Specialists, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Routine releases and coverage tracking now run themselves. What still needs a person under pressure is a crisis response, a real relationship with a reporter, and a narrative built to survive scrutiny.

roles.title: Advertising Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Bid optimization, audience targeting, and campaign reporting are now handled end-to-end by ad platforms, but budget strategy and agency/creative direction still require a manager's judgment.
rationales.toolMaturity: Programmatic bidding, Meta Advantage+, and Google Performance Max are mature, production-embedded tools that most advertising teams already run on by default.
rationales.adoption: Automated bidding and AI-generated creative variants are now a mainstream expectation across mid-size and large advertisers, not just early adopters.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can already run bid-and-reallocate loops and generate/rotate ad creative variants unsupervised, but cross-channel strategy and client narrative remain human-owned.

tasksAtRisk: Ad creative testing and variant generation
Bid and budget reallocation across channels
tasksGrowing: Creative strategy and brand storytelling
Cross-platform campaign architecture
Privacy-first, cookieless measurement strategy
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-2011 Advertising and Promotions Managers, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Bidding, targeting, and creative rotation now run themselves inside agentic ad platforms. Advertising managers who still own the budget strategy, agency relationships, and the creative narrative across channels are the ones platforms can't route around.

roles.title: Marketing Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Reporting, A/B test setup, scheduling, and template creation are largely automatable, but brand strategy and cross-channel judgment still gate most of the role's core work.
rationales.toolMaturity: Marketing automation and AI campaign platforms are mature, widely-shipped products embedded in the standard marketing stack.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted execution is now a mainstream expectation for marketing teams across most mid-size and large employers.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can own reporting, test setup, and scheduling workflows end-to-end, while positioning, persona synthesis, and channel strategy stay with the manager.

tasksAtRisk: A/B test setup and basic analysis
tasksGrowing: Brand strategy and market positioning
Cross-channel campaign orchestration
Customer insight synthesis and persona development
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-2021 Marketing Managers, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Execution and reporting now run largely on autopilot. Marketing managers who own positioning, customer insight, and orchestration across an ever-multiplying set of channels are becoming more valuable, not less.

roles.title: Sales Representative

rationales.taskAutomation: Lead scoring, CRM updates, and follow-up email drafting are digital tasks AI now performs reliably, but negotiation and deal-closing stay judgment- and trust-gated.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI sales tools like Gong, Outreach, and Clari are mature, widely-adopted products embedded in the standard sales stack.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted prospecting and pipeline management is now a mainstream expectation across B2B sales organizations.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run outbound sequencing, lead scoring, and meeting-prep loops largely unsupervised, but closing complex deals still requires a human in the room.

tasksAtRisk: CRM data entry and pipeline updates
tasksGrowing: Complex deal negotiation and closing
Strategic account relationship management
Consultative selling for technical products
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 41-3091 Sales Representatives of Services, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Prospecting and pipeline admin now run largely on agents. Reps who win are the ones clients trust to understand a nuanced problem and negotiate a complex deal, which no agent closes alone yet.

roles.title: Customer Success Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Onboarding sequences, usage-health monitoring, and renewal reminders are digital and increasingly automated, but enterprise relationship management and escalation judgment gate the core role.
rationales.toolMaturity: Customer-success platforms like Gainsight and ChurnZero now ship credible AI health-scoring and playbook automation that is improving quickly.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted health scoring and automated onboarding are common at scale in SaaS but far from universal outside tech.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run onboarding checklists, usage alerts, and low-touch renewal workflows end-to-end, while high-ACV account strategy stays with the human CSM.

tasksGrowing: Enterprise relationship and escalation management
Strategic account expansion planning
Cross-functional advocacy for at-risk accounts
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-9199 Managers, All Other (closest match; high end extended toward SaaS market postings)
roles.insight: Low-touch onboarding and renewal reminders now run on automated playbooks, which is exactly why headcount is under pressure at SaaS companies in 2026. CSMs who survive own the enterprise accounts where an escalation or expansion conversation still needs a trusted human relationship.

roles.title: Account Executive

rationales.taskAutomation: AI SDR tools and CRM automation now handle prospecting, sequencing, and pipeline hygiene, but negotiating and closing complex, high-value deals stays trust- and judgment-gated.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI sales platforms like Gong, Clari, and AI SDR tools are mature, widely-shipped products embedded in modern sales stacks.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted prospecting and deal intelligence are now mainstream in tech and SaaS sales organizations.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run outbound sequencing, meeting prep, and pipeline updates largely unsupervised, but they don't close enterprise deals on their own.

tasksGrowing: Enterprise deal negotiation and closing
Multi-stakeholder relationship management
Complex solution scoping with technical buyers
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 41-3091 Sales Representatives of Services (base+OTE high end extended toward SaaS market postings)
roles.insight: Prospecting and pipeline admin now run largely on AI SDR agents. What still requires a human is reading the room across a multi-stakeholder buying committee and closing a deal a prospect has to trust.

roles.title: Insurance Sales Agent

rationales.taskAutomation: Quote generation, policy comparison, application paperwork, and renewal reminders are automatable end-to-end; needs analysis, trust-building, and complex-risk placement are relationship-gated.
rationales.toolMaturity: Direct-to-consumer quote engines and AI policy-recommendation tools are mature for personal lines (auto, term life, renters).
rationales.adoption: Carriers and aggregators push automated personal-lines sales hard; independent agents and commercial lines lag well behind.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can already run the quote-compare-bind loop for simple personal lines and own renewal outreach queues; complex commercial placement stays human.

tasksAtRisk: Generating and comparing quotes across carriers
Routine renewal reminders and follow-up outreach
Answering standard coverage and pricing questions
tasksGrowing: Complex commercial and specialty-risk placement
Advisory needs-analysis for life and business clients
Claims advocacy and long-term relationship management
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 41-3021, p25-p75 rounded (commission-heavy; spread widens above p75)
roles.insight: Personal-lines insurance is already sold by software; agents surviving the shift are advisors on complex risk, not quote processors.

roles.title: Wholesale Sales Representative

rationales.taskAutomation: Order processing, reorder automation, and catalog/pricing lookups are digital tasks AI tools now handle well, but buyer relationship management and negotiation gate the core role.
rationales.toolMaturity: B2B e-commerce and AI order-management tools are credible, fast-improving products, though not yet universally embedded across distributors.
rationales.adoption: Larger distributors have adopted AI-assisted ordering and reorder tools, with mid-market and independent reps still catching up.
rationales.agenticExposure: Reorder workflows and catalog lookups are agent-ownable, but the relationship-based selling that wins shelf space stays with the rep.

tasksGrowing: Buyer relationship management and account retention
Negotiating shelf space and volume pricing
New-account prospecting and territory development
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 41-4012 Sales Representatives, Wholesale and Manufacturing (Except Technical/Scientific), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Reordering and catalog lookups are moving to self-service B2B portals. Reps who keep the relationship that wins shelf space and negotiates volume pricing are harder to route around.

roles.title: Brand Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Competitive tracking, brand-guideline documentation, and research synthesis are automatable, but positioning decisions and creative judgment gate most of the role's core work.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI marketing analytics and content tools are mature, widely-shipped products, though brand strategy itself has no dedicated automation category.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted research and tracking are common at large CPG and tech brands, spreading to mid-market.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run competitive-tracking and guideline-documentation workflows, but brand positioning and creative direction stay with the manager.

tasksAtRisk: Brand guideline documentation and asset audits
Research synthesis for brand health studies
tasksGrowing: Brand positioning and portfolio strategy
Packaging and creative direction
Cross-functional brand P&L ownership
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-2021 Marketing Managers (closest match), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now handles the competitive tracking and documentation that used to eat a brand manager's week. The job increasingly is positioning strategy and creative judgment, which is exactly what AI can't originate.

roles.title: Retail Salesperson

rationales.taskAutomation: Core work is in-person selling, stocking, and customer help on the floor; only inventory lookups and basic paperwork are fully digital and automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI clienteling apps and POS recommendation tools exist and are improving but are not yet a fully mature, universal category.
rationales.adoption: Large retail chains are rolling out AI-assisted clienteling and inventory tools, with adoption still uneven across mid-size and independent retailers.
rationales.agenticExposure: Back-office slices like restocking alerts and basic scheduling are agent-ownable, but the in-person sales interaction itself is outside current agent reach.

tasksGrowing: Personalized styling and product advice
In-store experience and upsell conversations
Handling complex complaints and loyalty relationships
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 41-2031 Retail Salespersons, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Self-checkout and inventory tools automate the paperwork, but the floor interaction that actually closes a sale is still an in-person, physical job AI doesn't reach yet.

roles.title: Product Demonstrator

rationales.taskAutomation: The core work is live, in-person product sampling and demonstration; only pitch-script drafting is a fully digital, automatable task.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generic LLM tools can draft talking points, but no role-specific automation exists for physical in-store or event demonstration.
rationales.adoption: Very few brands use structured AI-scripted demo content today; this remains an early-adopter niche.
rationales.agenticExposure: This is physical, in-person work that current agents cannot reach; nothing in the core role is agent-ownable.

tasksGrowing: Live product demonstration and sampling
In-person Q&A and objection handling
Event setup and brand-experience delivery
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 41-9011 Demonstrators and Product Promoters, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: This is one of the most physically-grounded, in-person roles in marketing, and it stays that way: AI can write the script, but it can't stand at the sample table and make the pitch.

roles.title: Data Entry Clerk

rationales.taskAutomation: Keying, transcribing, and reconciling structured data from forms, invoices, and scans is almost entirely automatable end-to-end; only messy exception cases still need a human.
rationales.toolMaturity: OCR, document AI, and agentic data-extraction pipelines are commodity-grade capabilities deployed at scale across enterprise document systems.
rationales.adoption: Large employers across finance, healthcare admin, and logistics have replaced manual entry queues with automated capture as standard practice.
rationales.agenticExposure: Data entry is close to a pure agent spec: ingest, extract, validate, write to system, and flag exceptions, with almost no multi-step judgment required.

tasksAtRisk: Manual keying of forms, invoices, and scanned documents
Database record creation, deduplication, and updates
Routine data validation against source documents
tasksGrowing: Exception handling and edge-case data quality review
Training and correcting AI extraction pipelines
Light process-improvement and automation configuration
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-9021 (Data Entry Keyers), p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI Document Intelligence & OCR Pipeline Configuration

roles.insight: Document AI and agentic extraction pipelines now handle the full capture-validate-file loop for structured data, leaving almost no defensible core to this role as historically specified. The realistic path is moving into the exception queue and the pipeline-configuration work that keeps the automation running.

roles.title: Administrative Assistant

rationales.taskAutomation: Scheduling, inbox triage, meeting prep, travel booking, and expense reports are now handled end-to-end by agentic assistants at professional reliability; only ad hoc judgment calls and interpersonal gatekeeping resist automation.
rationales.toolMaturity: Microsoft 365 Copilot, Google Workspace Gemini, and dedicated scheduling agents are mature, shipped products embedded directly in the tools admins already use daily.
rationales.adoption: Most mid-size and large employers now deploy AI scheduling and email drafting as a default part of the admin toolchain, not a pilot.
rationales.agenticExposure: Multi-step chains — read the inbox, propose meeting times, draft replies, file expense receipts — are increasingly agent-owned end-to-end for routine cases.

tasksAtRisk: Meeting notes, transcription, and action-item extraction
Travel booking and expense report reconciliation
tasksGrowing: Executive relationship management and judgment calls
Cross-team project coordination
Reviewing and correcting AI agent output
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-6014 (Secretaries and Administrative Assistants, Except Legal, Medical, and Executive), p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Agentic AI Workflow Orchestration (Copilot, Gemini agents)

roles.insight: Agentic assistants now run the full scheduling-to-inbox-to-expense loop with minimal supervision, which is why routine admin work keeps climbing toward Very High risk. The defensible ground is judgment-heavy coordination — reading a room, managing competing executive priorities, catching what an agent gets subtly wrong — not any single software skill.

roles.title: Call Center Agent

rationales.taskAutomation: Most high-volume inbound call flows — account verification, basic troubleshooting, appointment reminders — are scripted enough for AI voice agents to run end-to-end at production quality.
rationales.toolMaturity: Conversational AI voice platforms with real-time speech and telephony integration are mature and already deployed across large call center operations.
rationales.adoption: Call centers were early, aggressive adopters of AI voice deflection given the direct link to headcount cost; adoption is near-mainstream among large operators.
rationales.agenticExposure: A call queue is close to an agent spec: answer, verify, resolve or route, log — an autonomous voice agent can traverse it end-to-end for most routine calls.

tasksAtRisk: High-volume inbound call answering and routing
Scripted account verification and basic troubleshooting
tasksGrowing: Complex or emotionally charged call handling
Cross-selling and relationship-based retention calls
Supervising and correcting AI voice-agent transfers
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-4051 (Customer Service Representatives); range reflects call-center-specific roles at the lower end of the occupation band
roles.insight: AI voice agents now run the bulk of routine call center volume end-to-end, which is why this role carries the steepest agentic exposure in the category. Agents who move into escalation handling, retention calls, and AI-voice-agent supervision are the ones still needed on the floor.

roles.title: Payroll Specialist

rationales.taskAutomation: Payroll runs, tax withholding calculations, and standard reporting are largely automatable end-to-end; multi-jurisdiction edge cases and dispute resolution still need a specialist.
rationales.toolMaturity: Payroll platforms with embedded AI compliance and calculation engines are mature and near-universal in the toolchain.
rationales.adoption: Automated payroll processing is a mainstream expectation across nearly every employer size and sector.
rationales.agenticExposure: Payroll is substantially a pipeline of digital decisions — calculate, withhold, remit, report — that an agent can traverse end-to-end for standard cases.

tasksGrowing: Multi-state and international payroll compliance
Payroll system migration and integration projects
Compensation strategy and analytics support
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-3051 (Payroll and Timekeeping Clerks), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Standard payroll runs are now close to fully automated by platforms with built-in compliance engines, pushing this role toward High risk. The remaining ground is genuinely hard compliance — multi-state, cross-border, system-migration work — where a mistake is expensive enough that employers still want a specialist.

roles.title: Receptionist

rationales.taskAutomation: Call routing, visitor check-in, and basic scheduling are now scripted well enough for AI voice and kiosk systems to handle end-to-end in most settings.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI phone attendants and digital check-in kiosks are mature, commodity products deployed widely across offices and healthcare settings.
rationales.adoption: Mainstream in corporate offices and increasingly common in healthcare and hospitality front desks.
rationales.agenticExposure: Reception is substantially a routing-and-scheduling pipeline an agent can traverse; in-person judgment for security and VIP handling is the remaining human core.

tasksAtRisk: Answering and routing routine phone calls
tasksGrowing: VIP guest experience and hospitality
Security coordination and emergency response
On-site facility and workspace support
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-4171 (Receptionists and Information Clerks), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI voice attendants and check-in kiosks now handle most routine front-desk traffic, which is why this role sits at High risk. The remaining value is in-person: reading a nervous visitor, handling a security issue, making a VIP feel looked after — things a kiosk can't do.

roles.title: Customer Service Representative

rationales.taskAutomation: Routine account questions, order status, and FAQ-style troubleshooting are now handled end-to-end by AI chat and voice agents at professional quality; complex, emotional, or multi-product cases still need a person.
rationales.toolMaturity: LLM-based support chatbots and voice agents are mature, widely shipped products integrated directly into the standard customer-support stack.
rationales.adoption: Customer support deflection is a mainstream mid-2026 expectation — most consumer-facing companies now route a majority of tier-1 volume through AI before a human ever sees it.
rationales.agenticExposure: Tier-1 support is a textbook agent-ownable pipeline: read the ticket, look up the account, resolve or refund, close the loop, with escalation to a human only on failure.

tasksAtRisk: Answering routine account and billing questions
Live chat and email ticket responses
tasksGrowing: De-escalating angry or high-stakes customer situations
Complex multi-product troubleshooting
Reviewing and correcting AI chatbot handoffs
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-4051 (Customer Service Representatives), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI chat and voice agents now close a majority of tier-1 tickets without a human touch, which is why routine customer service sits at High risk. The remaining seats go to reps who handle the escalations an agent bounces back — angry customers, ambiguous multi-product problems, and judgment calls the bot won't make.

roles.title: Order Clerk

rationales.taskAutomation: Order intake, stock checks, and ERP entry are now scripted enough for AI order-management systems to handle end-to-end for standard orders.
rationales.toolMaturity: E-commerce and ERP order-automation tools are mature and near-universal in retail and manufacturing order pipelines.
rationales.adoption: Automated order processing is a mainstream expectation across retail, manufacturing, and distribution.
rationales.agenticExposure: Order management is explicitly the kind of digital pipeline an autonomous agent can traverse end-to-end for the majority of standard, non-custom orders.

tasksAtRisk: Processing incoming purchase and sales orders
Checking stock availability and confirming quantities
Entering orders into ERP or order-management systems
Sending order confirmations and basic status updates
tasksGrowing: Resolving order discrepancies and customer disputes
Managing complex or custom order configurations
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-4151 (Order Clerks), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI order-management systems now take standard orders from intake to confirmation without a human, which is why this role sits at High risk. Clerks handling discrepancies and custom configurations hold the more defensible ground.

roles.title: Executive Assistant

rationales.taskAutomation: Scheduling and correspondence are largely automatable, but confidential judgment calls, stakeholder diplomacy, and ambiguous prioritization for a specific executive still require a person.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI scheduling, email drafting, and briefing-prep tools are mature and widely embedded in executive productivity suites.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is common at large and tech-forward employers but slower where confidentiality and single-principal trust matter most.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can own discrete chains such as briefing assembly and travel logistics, but the trusted, principal-specific judgment core of the role resists autonomous ownership.

tasksAtRisk: Email screening, prioritization, and draft responses
Meeting briefing packets and materials preparation
tasksGrowing: Strategic executive support and decision prep
Confidential project and stakeholder management
Directing and quality-checking AI-assisted work product
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-6011 (Executive Secretaries and Executive Administrative Assistants), p25-p75; upper end reflects senior EA postings at large tech/finance employers
roles.insight: AI now assembles the calendar, drafts the emails, and preps the briefing deck; what it can't do is read a principal's unstated priorities or manage the politics around them. EAs who move toward chief-of-staff-style trusted advising hold their ground better than those who stay purely logistical.

roles.title: Inventory Clerk

rationales.taskAutomation: Stock counting, WMS data entry, and reorder flagging are largely automatable via barcode/RFID scanning and computer-vision counting, though physical handling and discrepancy investigation remain manual.
rationales.toolMaturity: Computer-vision inventory counting and AI-driven WMS reorder logic are credible and improving fast, though not yet as universally deployed as mature warehouse robotics.
rationales.adoption: Common in large retail and distribution operations; adoption is spreading to mid-size warehouses as vision-based counting hardware gets cheaper.
rationales.agenticExposure: Routine reconciliation and reorder-trigger workflows are agent-ownable; physical counting and shrinkage investigation still need a person on the floor.

tasksAtRisk: Manual stock counting and cycle counts
Inventory data entry into WMS systems
Reorder point flagging and restocking requests
tasksGrowing: Investigating inventory discrepancies and shrinkage
Coordinating physical audits across locations
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-5071 (Shipping, Receiving, and Inventory Clerks), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Barcode, RFID, and computer-vision counting have automated most routine stock tracking, pushing this role toward High risk.

roles.title: Records Clerk

rationales.taskAutomation: Filing, classification, and retrieval of digital records is now largely automatable with AI document-management systems; compliance judgment and legal holds still need a person.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI document classification and retrieval tools are credible and maturing quickly inside enterprise content-management platforms.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is common in large organizations with formal records-management functions, spreading to mid-market as document AI gets cheaper.
rationales.agenticExposure: Filing, tagging, and routine retrieval are agent-ownable; retention-schedule judgment and legal-hold compliance stay with a specialist.

tasksGrowing: Records compliance audits and legal holds
Cross-department records governance
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-4071 (File Clerks), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI document-management systems now file, tag, and retrieve most records automatically, pushing this role to High risk. Clerks who move into records compliance and legal-hold work hold a more defensible position than pure filing and retrieval.

roles.title: Billing Specialist

rationales.taskAutomation: Invoice generation, payment posting, and routine accounts-receivable follow-up are largely automatable, but disputed charges and negotiated payment arrangements still need judgment.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-driven billing and AR automation platforms, including automated invoicing and dunning tools, are mature and widely deployed in finance operations.
rationales.adoption: Automated billing and invoice-matching is a mainstream expectation at mid-size and large employers across most industries.
rationales.agenticExposure: Reconciliation and dunning-sequence workflows are meaningfully agent-ownable today; complex disputes and customer-specific negotiation remain human.

tasksGrowing: Complex dispute resolution and negotiated payment plans
Cross-department billing system integration
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-3021 (Billing and Posting Clerks), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI billing platforms now generate invoices and chase routine payments automatically, which pushes this role to High risk. The durable work is disputed charges and negotiated arrangements where a system alone can't close the case.

roles.title: Office Clerk (General)

rationales.taskAutomation: Filing, form processing, record updates, basic correspondence, and data lookup are digital, routinized, and automatable end-to-end; the residual is ad-hoc physical office support.
rationales.toolMaturity: Document AI, workflow automation, and office-suite copilots targeting exactly this task mix are mature and widely shipped.
rationales.adoption: Back-office automation is common in large firms and spreading through mid-market shared-services teams.
rationales.agenticExposure: The role is largely a queue of digital handoffs — routing documents, updating records, chasing status — that autonomous agents can traverse today.

tasksAtRisk: Filing, retrieving, and updating records across office systems
Processing routine forms, invoices, and correspondence
Data entry and cross-system record reconciliation
Answering routine internal status and lookup requests
Scheduling and calendar upkeep for shared resources
tasksGrowing: Exception handling when automated workflows fail or conflict
Overseeing and auditing automated document pipelines
In-person office coordination and vendor reception
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-9061, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI Office-Suite Power Use (Copilot, Gemini for Workspace)

roles.insight: One of America's largest occupations sits squarely in the path of document AI and office agents; clerks who become the person who runs and audits the automation keep the job.

roles.title: Logistics Coordinator

rationales.taskAutomation: Shipment tracking, route optimization, and standard carrier comparison are now largely automated, but multi-modal exception handling and customs judgment calls keep over a third of the role human-driven.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI logistics and visibility platforms are credible, fast-improving, and increasingly the default tooling on top of transportation-management systems.
rationales.adoption: Common among tech-forward retail, manufacturing, and 3PL employers; spreading but not yet universal among smaller shippers.
rationales.agenticExposure: Routine tracking-and-routing chains are agent-ownable today; live-fire disruption response and carrier relationship repair still need a person.

tasksAtRisk: Shipment tracking and automated status updates
Route and carrier optimization for standard lanes
Rate comparison and carrier selection for routine loads
tasksGrowing: Exception handling during disruptions and delays
Carrier and 3PL relationship management
Multi-modal contingency planning
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1081 (Logisticians); range reflects coordinator-level positions within the broader logistician occupation
roles.insight: Tracking, routing, and standard rate comparison are now largely automated by TMS copilots, but real supply chains still break in ways only a person can untangle. Coordinators who specialize in disruption response and carrier relationships are more durable than those doing pure status-update work.

roles.title: Office Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Vendor ordering, policy documentation, and routine scheduling are increasingly automated, but people management, culture-setting, and ad hoc problem-solving keep a substantial human core.
rationales.toolMaturity: Office-operations copilots for procurement and scheduling are mature and commonly bundled into workplace software suites.
rationales.adoption: Common at large and tech-forward employers; adoption at small offices lags since dedicated tooling budgets are smaller.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can plausibly own supply reordering, onboarding paperwork, and routine scheduling chains; team culture and cross-functional leadership stay with a person.

tasksAtRisk: Office budget tracking and expense reconciliation
tasksGrowing: Workplace experience and culture design
Cross-functional operations leadership
Hybrid-work policy and technology management
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-1011 (First-Line Supervisors of Office and Administrative Support Workers), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Ordering supplies and tracking a budget are now largely automatable, but running an office still means reading a room and fixing whatever nobody planned for. Office managers who take on real cross-functional operations leadership are outgrowing the role's clerical reputation.

roles.title: Procurement Specialist

rationales.taskAutomation: Purchase-order creation, catalog management, and routine vendor comparison are largely automatable; strategic sourcing and contract negotiation are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI procurement platforms with spend-analysis and vendor-matching are credible and improving fast, though not yet as embedded as mainstream coding or writing copilots.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is common at large enterprises with mature procurement functions, spreading toward mid-market.
rationales.agenticExposure: Routine PO processing and invoice-matching chains are agent-ownable; complex negotiation and supplier-risk judgment remain human.

tasksAtRisk: Vendor comparison for standard, non-strategic items
tasksGrowing: Strategic supplier relationship management
Complex contract negotiation
Supply chain risk assessment and mitigation
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1023 (Purchasing Agents, Except Wholesale, Retail, and Farm Products), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now handles the transactional half of procurement — POs, catalog pricing, invoice matching — leaving negotiation and supplier risk as the durable core. Specialists who move up into strategic sourcing hold a clearly defensible High-but-not-Very-High position.

roles.title: Mail Clerk

rationales.taskAutomation: Digital mail intake and routing is increasingly automated through scan-and-distribute systems, but physical sorting, package handling, and delivery remain manual tasks.
rationales.toolMaturity: Mailroom digitization tools exist and are improving but are less universally deployed than document AI in other back-office functions.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is uneven — larger corporate campuses have digitized mail intake, but many mid-size and smaller offices still run manual mailrooms.
rationales.agenticExposure: Digital mail intake and routing is agent-ownable; physical sorting and last-mile internal delivery are not.

tasksAtRisk: Sorting and routing incoming physical mail
Digital mail scanning and inbox distribution
tasksGrowing: Secure and sensitive document handling
Mailroom technology administration
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-9051 (Mail Clerks and Mail Machine Operators, Except Postal Service), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Scan-and-distribute systems now handle a growing share of digital mail routing, but someone still has to physically sort packages and hand-deliver sensitive documents. That keeps this role at Moderate risk rather than pushing it higher.

roles.title: Supply Chain Analyst

rationales.taskAutomation: Demand forecasting and inventory-optimization calculations are increasingly automated, but network design and disruption strategy require judgment models can't yet own.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI forecasting and supply-chain optimization tools are credible and improving quickly, approaching the maturity of established analytics platforms.
rationales.adoption: Common at large manufacturing, retail, and tech-forward supply chains; spreading to mid-market operations.
rationales.agenticExposure: Forecast generation and scorecarding are agent-ownable; multi-tier network redesign and crisis response stay with a human analyst.

tasksAtRisk: Demand forecasting from historical and real-time data
tasksGrowing: Supply chain disruption response strategy
Multi-tier supply network design
Sustainability and ESG supply chain planning
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1081 (Logisticians); range reflects analyst-level positions within the broader logistician occupation

defenseSkills.skill: AI Supply Chain Forecasting & Optimization Tools

roles.insight: Forecasting and inventory math are now largely AI-generated starting points, but building a network that survives a real disruption still takes an analyst who understands the business, not just the model. That keeps this role Moderate rather than sliding toward High.

roles.title: Facilities Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Work-order routing, maintenance scheduling, and energy/space reporting are meaningfully automatable, but vendor negotiation, safety judgment, and on-site building operations are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Smart-building IoT platforms and AI-driven maintenance-prediction tools are credible and improving quickly, though not yet universal across building stock.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is growing in large commercial portfolios and tech-forward campuses but remains uneven across older or smaller facilities.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can plausibly own ticket triage, preventive-maintenance scheduling, and energy-anomaly flagging; the physical response and vendor relationship layer stays human.

tasksAtRisk: Work order intake, routing, and status tracking
Energy usage monitoring and anomaly reporting
Space utilization data collection and reporting
tasksGrowing: Workplace experience design for hybrid teams
Sustainability program leadership and ESG reporting
Smart building technology integration and vendor oversight
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-3013 (Facilities Managers); range reflects single-site/mid-portfolio facilities management, below the senior multi-site tail of the occupation

defenseSkills.skill: Smart Building IoT & Predictive Maintenance Platforms

roles.insight: AI now triages work orders and flags maintenance needs before a person notices, but someone still has to stand in the mechanical room, manage the contractor, and own the safety call. Facilities managers who add sustainability and smart-building program leadership to core operations are growing in scope, not shrinking.

roles.title: Operations Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Dashboard compilation, standard scheduling, and SOP documentation are increasingly AI-generated, but the core of the job — leading people, setting priorities, and making judgment calls under ambiguity — resists automation.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI operations dashboards and BI copilots are credible and improving fast, giving managers faster reporting, though not yet a full operating layer.
rationales.adoption: Common at large, tech-forward firms for reporting and scheduling support; the management function itself is not something employers are automating away.
rationales.agenticExposure: Isolated back-office slices — reporting, scheduling, documentation — are agent-ownable, but the core management role of leading a team and owning outcomes is not.

tasksAtRisk: Routine KPI dashboard compilation and status reporting
tasksGrowing: Cross-functional strategy and priority-setting
People leadership, coaching, and performance management
Crisis response and operational judgment calls
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-1021 (General and Operations Managers), p25-p75 rounded; upper end extended toward market postings for senior operations leaders
roles.insight: AI now compiles the reports and drafts the schedules an operations manager used to build by hand, but leading a team through an ambiguous, high-stakes day still needs a person in the room. That is why this role stays firmly Low risk even as the reporting work underneath it automates.

roles.title: Warehouse Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Shift scheduling and throughput reporting are increasingly AI-generated, but running a physical warehouse floor — safety, labor management, equipment issues — requires on-site human judgment.
rationales.toolMaturity: Warehouse management system (WMS) analytics and AI forecasting tools are credible and improving, but do not replace floor-level operational management.
rationales.adoption: Common in large logistics and retail distribution operations for planning support; the management function itself is not being automated away.
rationales.agenticExposure: Scheduling and reporting are agent-ownable slices; core physical-floor leadership and safety accountability are not.

tasksAtRisk: Routine shift scheduling and labor allocation
tasksGrowing: Frontline team leadership and safety culture
Vendor, carrier, and 3PL relationship management
Crisis response to equipment failure or labor shortages
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-3071 (Transportation, Storage, and Distribution Managers), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now handles the scheduling math and throughput reporting, but running a physical floor safely still takes a person who can read the room and react to a forklift breakdown. That keeps this role solidly Low risk.

roles.title: Medical Transcriptionist

rationales.taskAutomation: Ambient AI speech-to-text now drafts the large majority of clinical notes end to end at production quality; only edge-case dictation and non-standard formatting need a human editor.
rationales.toolMaturity: Ambient clinical documentation tools (Nuance DAX, Abridge and similar) are commodity-grade and embedded directly into EHR workflows.
rationales.adoption: Ambient AI scribing is now mainstream across large health systems and is spreading fast into smaller practices.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agentic pipeline can capture, draft, format and route a note end to end; a human still reviews before it's signed into the chart.

tasksGrowing: AI transcription quality assurance
Clinical documentation improvement
Medical language specialist consulting
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 31-9094 (Medical Transcriptionists), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Ambient AI scribes now draft the majority of clinical notes directly from the patient encounter, collapsing the separate transcription step almost entirely. The remaining career path runs through documentation-quality oversight, not typing.

roles.title: Medical Coder

rationales.taskAutomation: AI coding engines now assign codes for routine, single-diagnosis encounters end to end at production-grade accuracy; complex multi-comorbidity and denial-appeal cases still need a coder.
rationales.toolMaturity: NLP-based coding engines (3M 360, Nuance, EHR-embedded coders) are mature, widely shipped and standard in most hospital systems.
rationales.adoption: Automated coding is now mainstream in large health systems and hospital networks, expected wherever claim volume justifies the license cost.
rationales.agenticExposure: Code-assign-verify-submit loops are meaningfully agent-owned today, with a human coder auditing outputs and handling exceptions.

tasksAtRisk: Assigning ICD-10 and CPT codes from notes
tasksGrowing: Complex surgical and specialty coding
Coding compliance and audit preparation
Agentic coding-pipeline oversight and exception handling
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-2072 (Medical Records Specialists), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI coding engines now assign codes for routine single-diagnosis visits with production-grade accuracy, and agentic pipelines are starting to run the full code-verify-submit loop with a human auditing exceptions. Coders who specialize in complex multi-comorbidity cases and own that audit layer stay essential.

roles.title: Pharmacy Technician

rationales.taskAutomation: Prescription data entry, insurance processing and routine pill counting/filling are largely automatable at production quality; complex compounding and patient-facing exceptions are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Robotic dispensing and pharmacy-workflow automation are mature and widely deployed across large chain and hospital pharmacies.
rationales.adoption: Automated filling and insurance-adjudication systems are standard in most large retail and hospital pharmacy operations.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agentic pipelines can run data entry through dispensing end to end, with a technician or pharmacist handling exceptions and compounding.

tasksAtRisk: Prescription data entry and insurance processing
tasksGrowing: Sterile and non-sterile compounding
Complex insurance and prior-authorization exceptions
Pharmacy automation system oversight
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-2052 (Pharmacy Technicians), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Robotic dispensing and automated insurance adjudication now run routine fills close to end to end, which is why this is the most exposed role in the healthcare category. Technicians who move into compounding or automation-system oversight have the clearer path forward.

roles.title: Pharmacist

rationales.taskAutomation: Prescription verification, interaction screening, claims adjudication and inventory ordering are substantially automatable; clinical consultation and complex therapy management are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Robotic dispensing and AI interaction-checking are mature and standard in most large pharmacy operations.
rationales.adoption: AI-driven dispensing and adjudication tools are common across large retail and hospital pharmacy chains.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agentic workflows can run verification-to-dispensing end to end with a pharmacist checking exceptions, though the final clinical check-off stays licensed and human.

tasksGrowing: Clinical pharmacy and patient consultation
Complex medication therapy management
Pharmacogenomics and personalized medicine
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1051 (Pharmacists), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Robotic dispensing and AI interaction-checking now run routine fills close to end to end, with a pharmacist reviewing exceptions. Pharmacists moving into clinical consultation and medication-therapy management are seeing their scope grow, not shrink.

roles.title: Radiologist

rationales.taskAutomation: AI now screens and pre-measures the large majority of normal-study volume end to end; complex, ambiguous and interventional cases still require a radiologist.
rationales.toolMaturity: FDA-cleared imaging AI for triage, measurement and normal-study flagging is mature and embedded directly into PACS workflows.
rationales.adoption: AI triage and flagging tools are now common across large and tech-forward hospital systems, spreading into community radiology groups.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agentic pipelines can pre-screen studies, flag urgent findings and draft normal-study reports, with the radiologist signing off and handling complex reads.

tasksAtRisk: Screening standard imaging for common findings
Measurements and quantification on routine scans
tasksGrowing: Complex and ambiguous case interpretation
Interventional radiology procedures
AI model validation and quality oversight
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1224 (Radiologists), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now triages and pre-screens most normal-study volume and drafts routine reports, compressing read time on straightforward cases. The complex, ambiguous and interventional work that defines the specialty still sits with the radiologist.
roles.restatement: v2's realization ceiling caps the score at task-automation potential rather than a flat composite; imaging AI's maturity and adoption have both climbed sharply in 2026, but complex and interventional work still bounds what's automatable, pulling the score down from v1's higher composite.

roles.title: Healthcare Administrator

rationales.taskAutomation: Scheduling optimization, billing oversight and routine compliance reporting are largely automatable, but service-line strategy, regulatory judgment and staff leadership are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Healthcare-specific revenue-cycle, staffing-optimization and compliance-analytics products are credible and improving fast, short of the embedded maturity of coding copilots.
rationales.adoption: AI-driven scheduling and revenue-cycle tools are now common in large health systems and spreading into mid-size practices.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agentic workflows can already run scheduling, claims triage and standard compliance reporting end to end, with escalations still routed to a human administrator.

tasksAtRisk: Agentic claims triage and billing oversight
tasksGrowing: Healthcare strategy and service line development
Regulatory change management (AI-specific rules)
Patient experience and quality improvement leadership
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-9111 (Medical and Health Services Managers), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Agentic tools now run scheduling, billing triage and standard compliance reports end to end with light oversight, cutting the back-office headcount these functions once required. Administrators who lead AI implementation and own regulatory and quality strategy are taking on more scope, not less.
roles.restatement: v2's realization ceiling (task automation as a hard cap, agentic factor weighted at only 10%) scores this role lower than v1's flatter composite; back-office administration is more agent-automatable than ever, but core management judgment still caps the ceiling well below where v1 placed it.

roles.title: Lab Technician

rationales.taskAutomation: Routine sample processing, QC logging and standard result interpretation are largely automatable, but specimen handling and equipment troubleshooting remain physical, hands-on work.
rationales.toolMaturity: Lab automation platforms and AI-assisted QC/anomaly flagging are credible and improving fast, short of full embedding across most clinical labs.
rationales.adoption: Automated analyzers and AI flagging are common in large hospital and reference labs, spreading into smaller clinical labs.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can already own QC logging, result flagging and calibration scheduling, but physical specimen handling keeps the core role out of reach.

tasksGrowing: Complex specimen troubleshooting
New assay validation and development
Lab automation system management
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-2012 (Medical and Clinical Laboratory Technicians), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Automated analyzers and AI-based QC flagging now handle the bulk of routine testing volume end to end, but someone still has to load, prep and troubleshoot the physical specimen path. Techs who manage lab automation systems and validate new assays keep growing scope.
roles.restatement: Under v2's realization ceiling, hands-on specimen handling caps automation well below where v1's flatter composite placed this role; instrument and QC automation are real but don't reach the physical core of the job.

roles.title: Dietitian

rationales.taskAutomation: Meal-plan generation, nutrient analysis and standard dietary-tracking are substantially automatable; personalized counseling and behavior-change work are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Consumer and clinical nutrition-AI apps that generate meal plans and analyze intake are common and improving quickly.
rationales.adoption: AI nutrition apps are widely used by consumers and increasingly integrated into clinical dietetics workflows.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can generate meal plans and track macros end to end, leaving the dietitian to handle counseling, adherence and medical nutrition therapy.

tasksAtRisk: Standard dietary intake tracking and reporting
tasksGrowing: Medical nutrition therapy for complex conditions
Behavior-change counseling and adherence support
Clinical collaboration on complex care plans
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1031 (Dietitians and Nutritionists), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Consumer apps now generate meal plans and track macros about as well as a routine consult would, pushing dietitians toward medical nutrition therapy and behavior-change counseling for complex conditions. The generic meal-planning end of the field is the most exposed.

roles.title: Medical Assistant

rationales.taskAutomation: Rooming documentation, vitals entry, scheduling and basic charting are substantially automatable; hands-on patient prep and clinical support tasks are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI scribing and intake-automation tools aimed at ambulatory care are credible and spreading through EHR vendors.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is common in larger clinics and health-system-owned practices, spreading into independent offices.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can own intake documentation, scheduling and routine follow-up messaging, but vitals-taking and direct patient prep stay physical.

tasksAtRisk: Basic chart updates and intake forms
tasksGrowing: Hands-on clinical support (injections, specimen collection)
Patient triage and communication
Cross-training into higher-scope clinical roles
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 31-9092 (Medical Assistants), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI intake and scheduling tools are absorbing a real share of front-desk and documentation work in clinics. Medical assistants who move toward hands-on clinical support and cross-train into higher-scope roles have the clearest path forward.

roles.title: Medical Lab Scientist

rationales.taskAutomation: Standard assay result flagging and QC documentation are automatable; complex specimen troubleshooting, method validation and abnormal-result interpretation are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Lab automation and AI-based result-flagging systems are credible and improving, increasingly standard in larger reference and hospital labs.
rationales.adoption: Automated analyzers and AI flagging are common in large reference labs and hospital systems, spreading into smaller labs.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can own routine result flagging and QC documentation, but complex troubleshooting and method development stay with the scientist.

tasksGrowing: Complex specimen troubleshooting and abnormal-result interpretation
New assay and method validation
Lab automation system oversight and quality management
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-2011 (Medical and Clinical Laboratory Technologists), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI-based result flagging now catches most routine abnormalities automatically, but validating new assays and troubleshooting complex specimens still requires a trained scientist's judgment. Labs are shifting scientists toward automation oversight rather than routine testing.

roles.title: Physician

rationales.taskAutomation: Reviewing standard labs/imaging, drafting clinical notes and checking drug interactions are automatable; complex diagnostic reasoning and procedures are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Ambient documentation and diagnostic-support tools are credible and improving quickly across major EHR platforms.
rationales.adoption: Large health systems increasingly deploy ambient scribing and decision-support tools; adoption varies widely by specialty and setting.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can pre-draft notes and flag abnormal results for review, but diagnosis, procedures and patient communication remain physician-led.

tasksAtRisk: Reviewing standard lab results and imaging
tasksGrowing: Complex diagnostic reasoning and differential diagnosis
Patient communication and shared decision-making
Procedural and surgical interventions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1229 (Physicians, All Other), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Ambient AI scribes and decision-support tools are cutting real documentation burden, and physicians who pair clinical judgment with these tools are becoming more effective, not less needed. The complex reasoning and procedures at the core of the job stay out of reach for now.

roles.title: Nurse Practitioner

rationales.taskAutomation: Documentation, standard order sets and routine prescription refills are automatable; independent diagnosis and treatment planning are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Ambient documentation and decision-support tools are credible and improving quickly, similar to what's shipping for physicians.
rationales.adoption: Adoption tracks large health-system deployment of ambient scribing and decision support, now common in bigger practices.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can pre-draft visit notes and flag abnormal results, but diagnosis and prescribing authority remain with the NP.

tasksAtRisk: Standard lab and imaging result review
tasksGrowing: Independent diagnosis and treatment planning
Complex case management
Patient counseling and shared decision-making
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1171 (Nurse Practitioners), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Ambient scribing is cutting real documentation time for NPs the same way it is for physicians, and NPs are increasingly filling the primary-care access gap AI can't close. Scope-of-practice expansion is a bigger factor than AI displacement here.

roles.title: Optometrist

rationales.taskAutomation: Routine refraction and standard screening are increasingly automatable via autorefractors and AI retinal-screening tools; complex diagnosis and eye disease management are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Autonomous AI retinal-screening systems (e.g., FDA-cleared diabetic retinopathy screening) are a mature, credible product category.
rationales.adoption: Adoption of AI screening tools is spreading through larger optometry chains and primary-care partnerships; independent practices lag.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run routine refraction workflows and flag screening abnormalities, but diagnosis and treatment of eye disease stay with the optometrist.

tasksGrowing: Complex eye disease diagnosis and management
Specialty contact lens fitting
Referral coordination for surgical care
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1041 (Optometrists), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Autonomous AI systems already screen for diabetic retinopathy at close to specialist accuracy, and autorefractors handle routine prescriptions. Optometrists managing eye disease and complex fittings hold the ground AI hasn't reached.

roles.title: Medical Sonographer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI-assisted measurement and biometry tools automate a real slice of image analysis, but probe placement and image acquisition remain a manual, operator-dependent skill.
rationales.toolMaturity: Automated biometry and AI image-quality tools are credible and improving, increasingly embedded in newer ultrasound systems.
rationales.adoption: Larger imaging centers and hospital systems are adopting AI-assisted measurement tools; broader adoption is still building.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can pre-populate standard measurements and flag image-quality issues, but acquiring a diagnostic-quality scan stays with the sonographer.

tasksGrowing: Complex and non-standard scan acquisition
Real-time image interpretation during exams
Patient positioning and comfort management
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-2032 (Diagnostic Medical Sonographers), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now auto-populates standard measurements on many scans, but getting a diagnostic-quality image out of a moving probe against a moving patient is still a hands-on skill no model performs. Demand for sonographers keeps growing with imaging volume.

roles.title: Physician Assistant

rationales.taskAutomation: Documentation, standard order sets and routine follow-up visits carry a meaningful digital share; diagnosis and procedures under supervision are not automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: Ambient documentation and decision-support tools targeting this role are credible and maturing quickly across major EHR platforms.
rationales.adoption: Large health systems are deploying ambient scribing and decision support broadly; smaller practices adopt more slowly.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can pre-draft notes and flag abnormal results for physician/PA review, but exams and procedures stay hands-on.

tasksGrowing: Physical exams and minor procedures
Complex case co-management with physicians
Patient education and care coordination
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1071 (Physician Assistants), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: PAs are absorbing the same ambient documentation gains as physicians and NPs, freeing time for exams and procedures rather than replacing them. Demand keeps climbing as health systems lean on PAs to extend physician capacity.

roles.title: Speech-Language Pathologist

rationales.taskAutomation: Standard screening-assessment scoring and session documentation carry a meaningful digital share; therapy delivery and clinical judgment do not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI voice-analysis and speech-therapy apps are credible and improving, increasingly used alongside in-person and teletherapy sessions.
rationales.adoption: School systems and telehealth-enabled practices are early-to-mid adopters of AI speech-therapy tools; broader adoption is still building.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can score standardized assessments and generate home-practice materials, but therapy delivery stays with the clinician.

tasksGrowing: Direct therapy delivery and technique adjustment
Complex swallowing and cognitive-communication assessment
Caregiver training and coordination with other providers
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1127 (Speech-Language Pathologists), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI apps now support standardized scoring and generate practice materials between sessions, but delivering therapy still requires a clinician adjusting technique in real time. School and healthcare demand for SLPs continues to outpace the training pipeline.

roles.title: Veterinary Technician

rationales.taskAutomation: Charting, standard lab processing and client-reminder communication carry a meaningful digital share; animal handling and hands-on clinical support are not automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI tools targeting veterinary tech workflows are early and mostly generic, borrowed from human lab-automation products.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is concentrated in larger corporate veterinary chains; most independent clinics use little beyond practice-management software.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can handle appointment reminders and routine chart entries, but restraint, sample collection and hands-on animal care stay physical.

tasksGrowing: Animal restraint and hands-on clinical support
Anesthesia monitoring during procedures
Client education on treatment plans
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-2056 (Veterinary Technologists and Technicians), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Veterinary tech work is hands-on animal care with a modest paperwork layer around it, and AI tooling for this specific role is still immature. Corporate consolidation and staffing shortages are bigger near-term forces than automation.

roles.title: Psychiatrist

rationales.taskAutomation: Session documentation and standard medication-management notes are automatable; diagnostic interviewing and the therapeutic relationship are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted symptom tracking and ambient documentation tools exist and are improving, short of the embedding seen in general primary care.
rationales.adoption: Adoption concentrates in larger academic and telepsychiatry practices; broader adoption is still early.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can draft visit notes and track symptom-scale scores, but diagnosis, medication decisions and the patient relationship stay physician-led.

tasksGrowing: Diagnostic interviewing and complex case formulation
Medication management for treatment-resistant cases
Crisis intervention and risk assessment
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1223 (Psychiatrists), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI drafts notes and tracks symptom scales, but diagnosing and treating mental illness still requires a licensed physician building trust with a patient over time. Demand for psychiatric care continues to outstrip the supply of psychiatrists.

roles.title: Radiologic Technologist

rationales.taskAutomation: Image-quality QC and standard positioning-protocol documentation carry a modest digital share; operating imaging equipment and positioning patients remain hands-on.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI image-quality and dose-optimization tools are credible and improving, increasingly embedded in newer imaging systems.
rationales.adoption: Larger hospital imaging departments are adopting AI dose-optimization and QC tools; broader adoption is still building.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can flag image-quality issues and manage worklist prioritization, but positioning and operating imaging equipment stay with the technologist.

tasksGrowing: Complex imaging protocols and modality specialization
Patient positioning for non-standard cases
Radiation-safety and dose management
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-2034 (Radiologic Technologists and Technicians), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI dose-optimization and image-QC tools are improving fast, but positioning a patient correctly for a diagnostic-quality scan is still a manual skill performed one patient at a time. Cross-training into CT and MRI modalities offers the clearest advancement path.

roles.title: Veterinarian

rationales.taskAutomation: Exam documentation, standard lab-result review and routine treatment-plan notes carry a meaningful digital share, but physical exams, surgery and animal handling are not automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI diagnostic-imaging and telehealth triage tools for veterinary medicine are credible and improving, short of the maturity seen in human radiology.
rationales.adoption: Larger corporate veterinary chains are adopting AI triage and imaging tools; independent practices lag.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can pre-screen intake symptoms and draft visit notes, but exams, surgery and hands-on animal care stay with the veterinarian.

tasksGrowing: Surgical and emergency procedures
Complex diagnostic workups
Client communication around difficult decisions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1131 (Veterinarians), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI triage chatbots and imaging tools are starting to handle routine intake and screening in veterinary practices, but surgery and hands-on animal care remain squarely the veterinarian's. Corporate consolidation is a bigger near-term force on the job than AI is.

roles.title: Mental Health Counselor

rationales.taskAutomation: Session-note drafting, treatment-plan templates and screening-assessment scoring are automatable; the therapeutic relationship itself is not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI scribes and screening tools for behavioral health are credible and improving, but nothing role-specific yet matches the maturity of general clinical documentation tools.
rationales.adoption: Tech-forward practices and telehealth platforms use AI note-taking and intake tools; adoption elsewhere remains early.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can own intake scheduling, screening-tool scoring and note drafting, but crisis intervention and the counseling relationship stay squarely human.

tasksGrowing: Therapeutic relationship building
Complex case conceptualization
Crisis intervention and safety planning
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 21-1014 (Mental Health Counselors), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now drafts session notes and scores standard screening tools, cutting real administrative load. What it can't do is sit with a person in crisis, and demand for that keeps outpacing supply.

roles.title: Physical Therapist

rationales.taskAutomation: Exercise-program documentation and outcome-measure tracking are automatable; hands-on manual therapy and movement assessment are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI motion-analysis and documentation tools are credible and improving but not yet standard practice equipment.
rationales.adoption: Larger outpatient and telehealth-enabled PT practices are adopting AI documentation and motion-tracking tools; broader adoption is still early.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can generate home-exercise plans and track outcome measures, but manual therapy and in-person motivation stay human.

tasksGrowing: Hands-on manual therapy and treatment
Patient motivation and behavioral change
Complex movement assessment and planning
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1123 (Physical Therapists), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI handles documentation and outcome tracking, freeing PTs for the hands-on treatment that actually drives recovery. Demand keeps growing faster than the pipeline of new therapists.

roles.title: Nurse (RN)

rationales.taskAutomation: Vitals charting, medication-administration documentation and standard patient education are automatable; direct assessment, hands-on care and advocacy are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: EHR-integrated documentation assistants and clinical decision support are credible and maturing, well short of coding-copilot-level embedding.
rationales.adoption: Large hospital systems increasingly deploy AI charting and monitoring tools; adoption is uneven outside big systems.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can own routine charting and monitoring alerts, but bedside care and rapid clinical judgment stay out of reach.

tasksGrowing: Direct patient care and clinical assessment
Patient advocacy and family communication
Care coordination across complex cases
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1141 (Registered Nurses), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI documentation assistants and monitoring alerts cut real charting time, but nursing's core, hands-on assessment, advocacy and rapid judgment at the bedside, sits outside what agents can own. The supply-demand gap keeps widening.

roles.title: Dentist

rationales.taskAutomation: Routine restorative and preventive procedures require licensed hands-on dexterity; only imaging review and treatment-plan documentation carry a meaningful digital share.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI caries-detection and imaging-analysis tools (Overjet, Pearl) are credible and improving, not yet standard equipment in most practices.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is concentrated in larger dental service organizations; independent practices are mostly early or non-adopters.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can pre-screen bitewing X-rays and draft treatment-plan documentation, but the procedures themselves stay physically hands-on.

tasksAtRisk: AI-assisted caries and pathology detection on X-rays
tasksGrowing: Restorative and surgical procedures
Complex case diagnosis and treatment sequencing
Patient trust-building and chairside communication
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1021 (Dentists, General), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now flags caries and bone loss on X-rays before the dentist even looks, tightening diagnosis but not touching the drill. The procedures that define the job stay licensed, physical, and human for the foreseeable future.

roles.title: Occupational Therapist

rationales.taskAutomation: Progress documentation and standard outcome-measure tracking are automatable; hands-on functional assessment and treatment are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI documentation and motion-tracking tools targeting rehab therapy are credible and improving, not yet standard equipment.
rationales.adoption: Larger outpatient and telehealth-enabled practices are adopting AI documentation tools; broader adoption remains early.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can generate home-program plans and track outcome measures, but hands-on functional treatment stays human.

tasksGrowing: Hands-on functional and adaptive-equipment assessment
Complex rehabilitation treatment planning
Patient and caregiver training
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1122 (Occupational Therapists), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now handles documentation and tracks recovery metrics, but functional rehabilitation is inherently hands-on work built around a specific patient's body and environment. Demand keeps climbing alongside an aging population and rising chronic-condition rates.

roles.title: EMT/Paramedic

rationales.taskAutomation: Patient assessment, treatment and transport in the field are physical, licensed acts; only dispatch routing and post-call documentation carry a digital share.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted dispatch triage and route optimization tools exist but are not yet standard across most EMS agencies.
rationales.adoption: Larger urban EMS systems are piloting AI dispatch triage; most agencies still rely on traditional protocols.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can optimize dispatch routing and pre-fill run reports, but on-scene assessment and treatment stay entirely human.

tasksGrowing: On-scene patient assessment and treatment
Emergency procedures and transport decisions
Crisis communication with patients and families
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-2042 (Emergency Medical Technicians), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Emergency response is in-person, time-critical physical work that AI can support at the margins, dispatch routing, report drafting, but not perform. Paramedic-level certification keeps opening more career ceiling than AI closes.

roles.title: Surgeon

rationales.taskAutomation: The core of the job is manual operative skill under direct liability; only pre/post-op documentation and standard order sets carry a digital share.
rationales.toolMaturity: Surgical robotics and AI-assisted planning tools are improving but remain assistive, not autonomous, in actual operating rooms.
rationales.adoption: Robotic-assisted surgery and AI planning tools are adopted mainly at larger academic and tertiary care centers.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can draft operative notes and pre-op checklists, but the operation itself stays entirely surgeon-led.

tasksAtRisk: Standard pre-op order sets and checklists
tasksGrowing: Operative technique and complex procedures
Intra-operative judgment under uncertainty
Patient and family surgical counseling
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1249 (Surgeons, All Other), p25-p75 rounded, extended toward market postings for high-demand specialties
roles.insight: AI assists with pre-op planning and drafts the paperwork around a case, but the operation itself is manual, liability-bearing work no model performs. Surgical robotics extend a surgeon's hands; they don't replace the surgeon.

roles.title: Dental Hygienist

rationales.taskAutomation: Cleanings, scaling and patient education are licensed physical acts performed chairside; almost none of the core task is digital.
rationales.toolMaturity: Little purpose-built AI targets hygienist tasks directly; generic charting software is the extent of current tooling.
rationales.adoption: AI has essentially no adoption footprint in the hygienist's actual task list beyond scheduling software.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can handle recall scheduling and note templating, but the in-mouth procedure itself is untouched by agentic tools.

tasksGrowing: Periodontal scaling and root planing
Patient oral-health education and motivation
Early disease screening during cleanings
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1292 (Dental Hygienists), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Hygiene work is a licensed physical act performed in a patient's mouth, one of the least AI-exposed jobs in health care. The only automatable slice is scheduling and note templating around the appointment.

roles.title: Phlebotomist

rationales.taskAutomation: Venipuncture is a licensed manual procedure; only labeling, logging and scheduling carry any digital share.
rationales.toolMaturity: Robotic phlebotomy devices exist only in early trials; no mature AI tooling targets this role's core task.
rationales.adoption: Adoption of any automation beyond basic lab information systems is rare.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can manage draw scheduling and specimen logging, but the needle stick itself stays entirely manual.

tasksGrowing: Venipuncture and specimen collection technique
Difficult-draw and pediatric patient handling
Patient comfort and anxiety management
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 31-9097 (Phlebotomists), p25-p75 rounded
