roles.insight: Robotic venipuncture devices remain experimental, so the needle stick stays a manual, licensed act for the foreseeable future. The digital slice of this job, scheduling and logging, is small and already partly automated.

roles.title: Home Health Aide

rationales.taskAutomation: The job is in-person personal care, bathing, mobility assistance, companionship, that has essentially no digital surface for AI to automate.
rationales.toolMaturity: Almost no purpose-built AI tooling targets this role's actual task list; scheduling apps are the extent of it.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is rare; home-care agencies use scheduling and dispatch software, not AI for the care itself.
rationales.agenticExposure: Only scheduling and visit-logging slices are agent-ownable; the in-person caregiving core is untouched.

tasksGrowing: Hands-on personal care and mobility assistance
Companionship and emotional support
Family communication and care coordination
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 31-1121 (Home Health Aides), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: This is about as AI-resistant as work gets: in-person personal care with no digital surface to automate. Demand is rising fast with an aging population, and the constraint is workforce supply, not AI displacement.

roles.title: Contract Reviewer

rationales.taskAutomation: Clause identification, template comparison, obligation extraction, and risk scoring on standard commercial contracts are now routine digital production AI clears end-to-end; only non-standard terms and negotiation strategy stay human.
rationales.toolMaturity: Contract-review AI (Ironclad, Ivo, Spellbook, Kira) is mature and embedded directly in the redlining workflow, not a bolt-on tool.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted contract review is now a mainstream expectation in corporate legal departments and law firms handling volume commercial paper.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agentic pipelines already ingest, redline, and flag standard agreements end-to-end, routing only exceptions to a human reviewer.

tasksAtRisk: Contract comparison against templates and playbooks
Risk scoring on standard contract types
tasksGrowing: Negotiation strategy for non-standard terms
Complex multi-party agreement analysis
AI contract tool training, playbook design, and oversight
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 23-2011, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI Contract Analysis Tools (Kira, Ironclad, Ivo, Spellbook)

roles.insight: AI contract-review tools now clear standard commercial paper in minutes and flag exceptions on their own. Reviewers who remain valuable are the ones who own the playbook, tune the AI, and handle the non-standard deal terms it can't yet judge.

roles.title: Legal Secretary

rationales.taskAutomation: Document formatting, calendar and deadline tracking, template correspondence, billing entry, and intake processing are routine digital production AI now performs directly, leaving little of the traditional role untouched.
rationales.toolMaturity: Scheduling, dictation, e-filing, and drafting assistants built for legal offices are mature and widely shipped, on par with generic office AI copilots.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted scheduling, drafting, and filing tools are now a mainstream expectation in law-firm administrative operations.
rationales.agenticExposure: Calendar management, correspondence, billing, and intake form a scripted pipeline of handoffs an autonomous agent can traverse end-to-end.

tasksAtRisk: Calendar management and court deadline tracking
tasksGrowing: Complex case coordination and management
Court relationship management
Legal technology administration
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-6012, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now handles document formatting, scheduling, and routine correspondence directly, cutting deep into the traditional job. Legal secretaries should move toward paralegal certification and legal-technology administration to stay ahead of it.

roles.title: E-Discovery Specialist

rationales.taskAutomation: Predictive-coding review, deduplication and culling, privilege-log drafting, and production formatting are routine digital production; complex privilege calls and defensibility strategy still need a human specialist.
rationales.toolMaturity: Technology-assisted review and generative-AI review platforms (Relativity aiR, Everlaw, DISCO) are mature and embedded directly in the standard e-discovery toolchain.
rationales.adoption: Predictive coding and AI-assisted review are now a mainstream expectation in large-scale litigation and corporate legal departments.
rationales.agenticExposure: Ingest-cull-tag-privilege-log-produce is already an agent-traversable pipeline in most modern e-discovery platforms, with exceptions routed to a human reviewer.

tasksAtRisk: Predictive-coding/technology-assisted document review and relevance ranking
Data ingestion, deduplication, and culling across custodians
tasksGrowing: Complex privilege and work-product disputes requiring attorney judgment
E-discovery strategy design and defensibility arguments for courts
Cross-border data-transfer and privacy-regulation navigation
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 23-2099 (Legal Support Workers, All Other; closest published SOC for e-discovery specialists), p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: TAR & Generative-AI Review Platform Mastery (Relativity aiR, Everlaw, DISCO)

roles.insight: Technology-assisted review already does most first-pass document culling at a fraction of linear-review cost. Specialists who remain are defending review methodology in court and making the privilege calls AI still gets wrong.

roles.title: Court Reporter

rationales.taskAutomation: Real-time verbatim transcription, captioning, and transcript formatting are routine digital production AI performs directly; the remaining share is producing a legally certified record and handling complex, multi-speaker proceedings.
rationales.toolMaturity: Speech-to-text has closed most of the accuracy gap for routine proceedings; real-time ASR and captioning products are mature, widely shipped, and near-commodity for standard audio conditions.
rationales.adoption: Digital audio/video recording is spreading through state and municipal courts and private depositions, though many jurisdictions still statutorily require a certified stenographer for the official record.
rationales.agenticExposure: Ingest-transcribe-format-index pipelines are largely agent-traversable for asynchronous depositions and virtual hearings, but live courtroom certification still needs a human in the loop.

tasksAtRisk: Real-time verbatim transcription of routine depositions and hearings
Automated closed-captioning and CART-style speech-to-text support
Transcript formatting, indexing, and word-search tagging
tasksGrowing: Certified legal-record accuracy review and challenged-testimony correction
Complex multi-speaker, technical, or high-stakes trial transcription requiring human judgment
Court technology administration and ASR quality assurance
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-3092, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Speech-to-text has closed most of the accuracy gap for routine depositions and hearings, and cost-pressured courts are piloting digital recording over stenographers. The remaining moat is the legally certified verbatim record: someone accountable for accuracy in complex, multi-speaker, or high-stakes proceedings.

roles.title: Title Examiner

rationales.taskAutomation: Public-record search, lien and encumbrance matching, and standard residential title-chain compilation are routine digital production; complex, contested, or non-digitized-county titles are what still need a human examiner.
rationales.toolMaturity: Automated title-search and title-plant software is a credible, fast-improving product category among large underwriters, but capability is uneven where county records are not yet fully digitized.
rationales.adoption: Large title insurers in digitized markets run automated search in production; adoption is spreading but far from universal given inconsistent county record digitization nationally.
rationales.agenticExposure: In modernized jurisdictions, the search-match-flag-exception workflow is largely a scripted digital pipeline an agent can traverse end-to-end for standard residential titles.

tasksAtRisk: Public record search and lien/encumbrance matching
Title chain-of-ownership compilation from digitized county records
Basic title-exception flagging against underwriting rules
tasksGrowing: Complex or contested title-defect resolution (boundary disputes, heirship, easements)
Rural and non-digitized county record investigation
Underwriting judgment on non-standard commercial and mixed-use properties
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 23-2093, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Automated Title-Search Platform Fluency (Qualia, Endpoint, title-plant AI)

roles.insight: Automated title search now clears most standard residential resales in digitized counties end-to-end; examiners increasingly specialize in the contested, rural, or commercial titles automation still can't clear.

roles.title: Paralegal

rationales.taskAutomation: Document organization, standard legal research, filing prep, and discovery processing are more than half the role's core output, but client contact, case strategy, and litigation judgment still gate the rest.
rationales.toolMaturity: Legal AI research and document-review tools are credible and improving fast, embedded in most modern practice-management stacks, short of fully commoditized.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted research and document review is common at large firms and corporate legal departments, and spreading into mid-market practices.
rationales.agenticExposure: Research-summarize-draft and discovery-triage loops are meaningfully agent-ownable today, though case strategy and client-facing judgment are not.

tasksGrowing: Complex litigation support and case strategy
Client communication and relationship management
E-discovery management and technology oversight
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 23-2011, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now handles document review and standard research at scale on its own. Paralegals who master legal AI tools, specialize in complex practice areas, and manage technology-driven workflows will remain valuable.
roles.restatement: Methodology v2's realization model plus a role-specific reassessment of tool maturity and adoption produce a materially lower composite than the v1 weighted average; core paralegal output remains substantial, but a large share (client contact, case strategy support) is judgment-gated work AI does not yet own end-to-end.

roles.title: Compliance Analyst

rationales.taskAutomation: Regulatory-change tracking, policy-gap analysis, and standard compliance reporting are now largely digital-document tasks AI can run end-to-end, but interpreting ambiguous violations and building an investigation record still needs a human analyst.
rationales.toolMaturity: RegTech platforms (Compliance.ai, Ncontracts, Diligent) plus generic LLM research assistants now cover regulatory-change monitoring and gap analysis with production reliability, though niche and cross-border jurisdictions still lag.
rationales.adoption: Large banks, tech firms, and healthcare systems run AI regulatory monitoring in production; mid-market and public-sector compliance teams are still mostly piloting.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agentic tools already run continuous regulatory-change monitoring and draft gap analyses and reports end-to-end, leaving analysts an exception queue instead of a blank page.

tasksGrowing: AI regulatory compliance (EU AI Act, state AI laws)
Investigation and incident management
Cross-border compliance strategy
AI governance program design and model-risk oversight
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1041, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now tracks regulatory changes and drafts compliance reports on its own, compressing the routine half of the job. Analysts who specialize in AI-specific regulation (EU AI Act, state AI laws) and cross-border complexity are moving into a high-growth niche instead.

roles.title: Judicial Law Clerk

rationales.taskAutomation: Case-law research, first-draft bench memoranda, and cite-checking are more than half the clerkship's core output, but framing the actual judgment call for the judge still requires human synthesis.
rationales.toolMaturity: Legal research and memo-drafting copilots (CoCounsel, Harvey, Westlaw AI) are credible and improving fast, and are increasingly present in chambers workflows.
rationales.adoption: Federal appellate chambers and large state courts are adopting AI research tools at a growing but still uneven rate across the judiciary.
rationales.agenticExposure: The research-summarize-draft loop that defines a clerkship is meaningfully agent-ownable today, short of the in-chambers deliberation and judgment framing a clerk provides.

tasksAtRisk: Case-law and statutory research for pending motions and appeals
First-draft bench memoranda summarizing party arguments and precedent
tasksGrowing: Judge-facing legal analysis on novel or high-stakes questions
In-chambers deliberation support and judgment-call framing
Complex multi-issue opinion drafting requiring judicial voice
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 23-1012, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI Legal Research Co-Piloting (CoCounsel, Harvey, Westlaw AI)

roles.insight: AI research tools now produce a credible first-pass bench memo in minutes; clerks who remain valuable are the ones a judge trusts to catch what the memo got wrong and frame the actual judgment call.

roles.title: Lawyer

rationales.taskAutomation: Legal research, template contract drafting, and due-diligence document review are automatable, but courtroom advocacy, client counseling, and liability-bearing judgment gate most of a lawyer's core work.
rationales.toolMaturity: Legal-research and drafting copilots (CoCounsel, Harvey, Westlaw AI) are credible and improving fast, though not yet the fully mature, unquestioned default across the profession.
rationales.adoption: Large firms and corporate legal departments have moved AI research and drafting into daily use; solo and small-firm practice adoption is still catching up.
rationales.agenticExposure: Multi-step research-draft-cite loops are agent-ownable today for routine matters, but representation and strategic judgment remain outside current agentic reach.

tasksAtRisk: Legal research and case law review
Brief section drafting for routine motions
tasksGrowing: Courtroom advocacy and negotiation
Client counseling and strategic advice
Complex deal structuring and novel legal questions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 23-1011, p25-p75 rounded (extended toward market postings for senior/big-law roles)

defenseSkills.skill: AI Legal Research Tools (Westlaw AI, CoCounsel)

roles.insight: AI accelerates research and drafting, making lawyers more productive rather than obsolete. Lawyers who master AI tools and specialize in AI-related law (IP, liability, regulation) are positioned for growth.

roles.title: Administrative Law Judge

rationales.taskAutomation: Case-file and medical/vocational record summarization and standardized eligibility checklisting are a modest digital-paperwork share; the credibility findings and binding eligibility decisions that define the role remain judgment-gated.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generic AI tools plus some agency-specific record-summarization aids apply to hearing prep, but nothing role-specific has broad traction across the administrative bench.
rationales.adoption: Some high-volume dockets (disability, unemployment) are piloting AI-assisted case-file summarization, but use in the decision itself is still an early-adopter phenomenon.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can digest medical and vocational records and pre-populate standardized checklists, but the statutorily required credibility and eligibility determination is not currently agent-ownable.

tasksAtRisk: Case-file and medical/vocational record summarization for hearing prep
Standardized eligibility-criteria checklisting (e.g., disability grid rules)
Draft decision boilerplate and procedural-order generation
tasksGrowing: Credibility determinations and live-testimony evaluation
Novel or borderline eligibility determinations requiring discretion
Appeals-resistant, legally defensible written decisions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 23-1021, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Case-prep and record summarization are increasingly AI-assisted, especially in high-volume dockets like disability and unemployment hearings, but statute still requires a human officer to weigh credibility and issue the binding decision.

roles.title: Mediator

rationales.taskAutomation: Case intake, scheduling, and standard settlement paperwork are a modest digital-paperwork share; the core work of building trust, reading emotional dynamics, and crafting creative settlements is interpersonal and not reliably automatable today.
rationales.toolMaturity: Online dispute resolution (ODR) platforms are credible for narrow, standardized disputes like e-commerce and small claims, but nothing broadly covers general commercial or family mediation.
rationales.adoption: ODR is used by early-adopter courts and e-commerce platforms for simple claims; broader family, labor, and commercial mediation practice has barely touched AI tools.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can handle intake, scheduling, and document exchange, but the core facilitation act of building trust between adversarial parties is outside current agentic reach.

tasksAtRisk: Case intake, scheduling, and standard settlement-agreement drafting
Simple online dispute resolution for e-commerce and small-claims matters
tasksGrowing: Complex multi-party negotiation requiring trust-building and creative deal design
Family, labor, and high-conflict mediation requiring emotional intelligence
Cross-cultural and high-stakes commercial dispute facilitation
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 23-1022, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: ODR platforms now clear routine e-commerce and small-claims disputes without a human in the loop, but complex mediation still runs on trust and reading a room, territory current agents don't credibly occupy.

roles.title: Judge

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can draft bench-memo summaries and check sentencing math, but the constitutional and statutory requirement for a human decision-maker keeps the core act of adjudication essentially non-delegable regardless of AI capability.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generic legal-research and drafting tools apply in chambers, but no widely deployed product targets judicial decision-making itself; adoption for the bench remains niche and unproven.
rationales.adoption: Use of AI directly in judicial decision-making is rare and actively discouraged by judicial ethics guidance; courts remain among the most conservative institutional adopters.
rationales.agenticExposure: Autonomous agents can prep case files and flag precedent, but binding rulings, sentencing, and courtroom credibility findings sit outside current agentic reach by law, not just capability.

tasksAtRisk: Case-law research and precedent summarization for pending motions
First-draft procedural order and boilerplate ruling generation
tasksGrowing: Courtroom fact-finding and witness credibility assessment
Novel constitutional and first-impression legal questions
Sentencing and equitable discretion in ambiguous, high-stakes cases
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 23-1023, p25-p75 rounded (state/local judiciary; federal Article III judicial pay is set by statute, not OES)

defenseSkills.skill: AI-Assisted Judicial Research Oversight (CoCounsel, Westlaw AI)
Judicial Ethics & AI Governance in Court Technology

roles.insight: AI now drafts bench memos and flags precedent for chambers staff, but the requirement for a human judicial officer to weigh credibility and issue binding rulings structurally insulates the bench regardless of AI capability. The real disruption lands on the clerks and researchers who prepare the record a judge reviews, not the judge's own docket.

roles.title: Tutor

rationales.taskAutomation: Concept explanation, practice-problem generation and correction, and standardized test-prep drills are now handled end-to-end by AI tutoring products at high reliability.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI tutoring apps (Khanmigo, Duolingo-style adaptive practice) are mature, widely-shipped consumer products, not just pilots.
rationales.adoption: Students and families now routinely use free or low-cost AI tutors directly, and tutoring companies increasingly build them into service delivery.
rationales.agenticExposure: Adaptive-practice agents already run full explain-practice-assess loops autonomously; human tutors add the most value in accountability and emotional coaching an agent can't replicate.

tasksGrowing: Motivational coaching and accountability
Identifying and addressing learning gaps
Executive function and study skills coaching
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 25-3041 (Tutors), p25-p75 rounded; wide spread reflects gig/part-time market
roles.insight: Free AI tutors now explain concepts and generate practice problems around the clock at high reliability. Human tutors who survive are the ones providing accountability, emotional support, and executive-function coaching AI can't replicate.

roles.title: Instructional Designer

rationales.taskAutomation: Storyboarding, assessment-question generation, and format conversion for standard e-learning courses are largely AI-automatable; learning-experience strategy and needs analysis are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI course-creation platforms (Synthesia, Articulate AI features) are mature, production-deployed tools already standard in many L&D toolchains.
rationales.adoption: Mid-2026 adoption of AI-assisted course authoring is common across corporate and higher-ed learning teams.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can run the outline-to-storyboard-to-assessment pipeline for a standard course largely unsupervised; immersive/simulation design and performance consulting still need a human designer.

tasksGrowing: Learning experience strategy and architecture
Immersive learning design (VR/AR/simulation)
Performance consulting and needs analysis
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 25-9031 (Instructional Coordinators), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now storyboards and drafts assessments for standard e-learning courses in minutes. Designers who apply learning science, build immersive/simulation experiences, and tie training to business outcomes keep the strategic seat.

roles.title: Corporate Trainer

rationales.taskAutomation: Slide decks, handouts, knowledge-check quizzes, and completion tracking are now routinely AI-generated end-to-end, leaving live facilitation and change management as the differentiated work.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generative-AI course-authoring tools (Synthesia, generative slide/quiz builders) are mature and widely embedded in corporate L&D toolchains.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted content creation is now a mainstream expectation in corporate learning and development functions at large employers.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agent pipelines can already draft a full training module -- outline, slides, quiz, and completion report -- end to end, though live delivery and org change work stay human.

tasksAtRisk: Creating training slide decks and handouts
tasksGrowing: Live facilitation and workshop leadership
Organizational change management
AI skill-building program design
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1151 (Training and Development Specialists), p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI Training-Content Pipelines (Synthesia, generative authoring tools)

roles.insight: AI now drafts training decks, quizzes, and completion reports end to end. Trainers who lead live facilitation, drive organizational change, and design AI-upskilling programs are the ones employers still pay for.

roles.title: Academic Advisor

rationales.taskAutomation: Degree audits, prerequisite checks, scheduling conflicts, and routine FAQ responses are now largely digital matching tasks AI handles end-to-end, though career counseling and at-risk intervention still require human judgment.
rationales.toolMaturity: Predictive-analytics advising platforms (EAB Navigate, Starfish) and general-purpose chatbots are credible, improving products now embedded in many advising offices.
rationales.adoption: Large universities and systems increasingly deploy AI degree-audit and chat-based advising tools, though adoption in smaller colleges lags.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can plan and execute multi-step advising workflows -- pulling transcripts, flagging requirement gaps, drafting outreach -- but cannot own the trust-building conversations at the center of advising.

tasksAtRisk: AI chatbot handling of routine FAQ and scheduling conflicts
First-pass drafting of outreach emails to at-risk students
tasksGrowing: Career counseling and goal alignment
Holistic student-success coaching and case management
Complex at-risk intervention requiring trust and judgment
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 21-1012 (Educational, Guidance, and Career Counselors and Advisors), p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI-Assisted Degree Audit & Advising Platforms (EAB Navigate, Starfish)

roles.insight: AI-assisted degree audits and chatbots now field most routine scheduling and FAQ traffic. Advisors who shift toward career coaching, case management for at-risk students, and the trust-based conversations AI can't fake stay essential.

roles.title: ESL Teacher

rationales.taskAutomation: Vocabulary drills, pronunciation practice, and basic grammar exercises are now handled well by language-learning AI, but assessing real communicative competence and cultural context is not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI language tools (adaptive practice apps, real-time translation, conversational AI partners) are mature and widely shipped.
rationales.adoption: Language-learning apps are now commonly used to supplement formal ESL instruction in schools and adult-education programs.
rationales.agenticExposure: Adaptive-practice agents can already run drill-and-assess loops autonomously, though placement testing and classroom-level communicative teaching remain human-led.

tasksGrowing: Communicative competence assessment
Cultural context and idiom instruction
Individualized language-acquisition coaching
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 25-3011 (Adult Basic Education, Adult Secondary Education, and English as a Second Language Instructors), p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI Language-Learning Platforms (adaptive practice, real-time translation)

roles.insight: AI language apps now handle vocabulary drills and pronunciation practice well. ESL teachers who assess real communicative competence and teach cultural context remain the harder-to-automate part of the job.

roles.title: Curriculum Coordinator

rationales.taskAutomation: Standards-alignment documentation, curriculum mapping, and resource compilation are largely automatable, but coaching teachers and building cross-stakeholder buy-in are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI curriculum-alignment and content-tagging tools are credible and improving fast, though not yet a single dominant platform.
rationales.adoption: Districts increasingly use AI to speed up standards-alignment and resource-mapping work.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can map curriculum to standards and compile aligned resources largely unsupervised, but coaching teachers through adoption stays human.

tasksAtRisk: Generating pacing guides and unit templates
tasksGrowing: Teacher coaching and professional development
Cross-stakeholder curriculum adoption and buy-in
Program evaluation and needs assessment
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 25-9031 (Instructional Coordinators), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now handles standards-alignment mapping and pacing-guide drafting quickly. Coordinators who coach teachers through real classroom adoption keep the job's most valuable part.

roles.title: Librarian

rationales.taskAutomation: Cataloging, metadata tagging, and routine reference lookups are increasingly AI-handled, while research consultations, community programming, and collection-development judgment are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-powered discovery layers and reference chatbots are credible, fast-improving products now piloted in academic and large public library systems.
rationales.adoption: Large academic and public library systems are adopting AI search and chat-reference tools, but budget-constrained smaller libraries lag well behind.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can competently run cataloging and metadata-tagging pipelines and handle first-line reference triage, but community programming and instruction stay human.

tasksAtRisk: Digital resource curation and licensing renewals
tasksGrowing: Information literacy instruction
Community programming and outreach
Complex research consultation and collection-development judgment
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 25-4022 (Librarians and Media Collections Specialists), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI reference chatbots and discovery tools now field routine lookups and cataloging work. Librarians who teach information literacy, run community programming, and make judgment calls on collections and complex research stay firmly in demand.

roles.title: College Professor

rationales.taskAutomation: Lecture-slide generation, first-pass grading of structured assignments, and syllabus/reading-list assembly are automatable, but original research, seminar facilitation, and mentorship are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI writing detectors, grading assistants, and generative slide/content tools are credible and improving but not yet a unified professor toolchain.
rationales.adoption: AI use for course-material drafting and grading support is spreading across universities, though research and tenure-track work remain largely unaffected.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can competently run literature-summary and first-draft loops for course content, but cannot conduct original research or graduate mentorship.

tasksAtRisk: Drafting standard lecture slides and materials
First-pass grading of essays against a rubric
tasksGrowing: Original research and knowledge creation
Seminar facilitation and Socratic discussion
Graduate mentorship and career advising
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 25-1199 (Postsecondary Teachers, All Other), p25-p75 with upward allowance for high-paying fields (business, law, medicine)
roles.insight: Course prep and first-pass grading increasingly run through AI, but professors who produce original research, run genuine seminar discussion, and mentor graduate students remain hard to displace. The bigger near-term shift is defending academic integrity against AI-written student work.

roles.title: Teacher (K-12)

rationales.taskAutomation: Lesson-plan drafting, worksheet generation, and grading of objective assessments are now largely automatable, but classroom management, mentorship, and differentiated instruction are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Classroom-facing AI tools (MagicSchool AI, Khanmigo, Diffit) are credible and improving fast, approaching mature adoption in many districts.
rationales.adoption: A growing majority of districts now permit or actively deploy AI lesson-planning and grading-assist tools, though policy and funding vary widely by state.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can draft lesson plans and grade structured work in a supervised loop, but classroom presence, behavior management, and real-time adaptation to students remain squarely human.

tasksAtRisk: Lesson plan drafting and resource gathering
tasksGrowing: Student mentorship and emotional support
Differentiated instruction for diverse learners
Classroom management and culture building
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 25-2021 (Elementary School Teachers), representative anchor for K-12 classroom teachers; middle/secondary levels run similar, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI Teaching Tools (MagicSchool AI, Khanmigo, Diffit)

roles.insight: AI now drafts lesson plans and grades routine quizzes, freeing time -- but classroom management, mentoring, and differentiated instruction for diverse learners are the parts of teaching AI still can't do.

roles.title: School Counselor

rationales.taskAutomation: Scheduling, resource referral, and college-application document review are partly automatable, but crisis intervention, mental-health support, and relationship-building are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generic LLM tools and emerging school-specific advising bots exist, but nothing purpose-built for K-12 counseling has broad traction.
rationales.adoption: Districts are slowly adding AI tools for scheduling and college-matching, but core counseling caseloads are still handled manually.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can handle scheduling and resource-lookup slices of the job, but crisis response and trust-based student relationships are squarely human.

tasksAtRisk: Standard FAQ responses about programs and requirements
tasksGrowing: Crisis intervention and mental-health support
Individualized college and career counseling
Building trust with at-risk students and families
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 21-1012 (Educational, Guidance, and Career Counselors and Advisors), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI can handle scheduling and routine resource lookups, but the crisis intervention, mental-health support, and trust-building at the core of school counseling remain firmly human work.

roles.title: Teaching Assistant

rationales.taskAutomation: Grading support, material prep, and basic supervision have a real digital-automatable share, but direct student support and classroom presence do not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generic AI tools apply to grading and material prep, but no dedicated teaching-assistant product has broad traction yet.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is uneven and mostly limited to individual teachers sharing AI-generated materials with their aides.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can pre-grade objective work and draft practice materials, but one-on-one and small-group student support in the classroom stays human.

tasksGrowing: One-on-one and small-group student support
Classroom behavior management assistance
Individualized accommodation support
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 25-9042 (Teaching Assistants, Preschool, Elementary, Middle, and Secondary School, Except Special Education), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI can pre-grade quizzes and draft practice worksheets, but the one-on-one support and accommodation help teaching assistants provide inside the classroom is what schools still pay for.

roles.title: School Principal

rationales.taskAutomation: Compliance reporting, scheduling, and budget administration are increasingly digital and assistable, but personnel decisions, school culture, and stakeholder relationships are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: General administrative and reporting AI tools are mature, but nothing purpose-built for school leadership dominates yet.
rationales.adoption: AI use for compliance reporting and scheduling is spreading among districts seeking administrative efficiency.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can draft reports and manage scheduling logistics, but personnel management, discipline, and community relations require an accountable human leader.

tasksGrowing: Personnel management and teacher coaching
School culture and climate leadership
Crisis response and community relations
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-9032 (Education Administrators, Kindergarten through Secondary), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now drafts compliance reports and helps with scheduling logistics, but the accountable, relationship-heavy work of leading a school -- coaching teachers, shaping culture, and handling crises -- stays firmly with the principal.

roles.title: School Psychologist

rationales.taskAutomation: Test-scoring support and report-drafting boilerplate are digitizable, but assessment interpretation, therapy, and crisis response are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generic AI drafting tools and some assessment-scoring software exist, but nothing purpose-built dominates school psychology practice.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is limited mostly to report-drafting assistance in well-resourced districts.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can help draft evaluation reports, but clinical judgment, IEP-meeting participation, and crisis intervention require a licensed human.

tasksAtRisk: Scheduling and administrative tracking of evaluations
tasksGrowing: Clinical assessment interpretation and diagnosis
Crisis intervention and safety planning
IEP meeting participation and family consultation
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 19-3034 (School Psychologists), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI can help draft evaluation-report boilerplate, but clinical interpretation, crisis response, and licensed judgment in IEP meetings keep school psychologists firmly needed.

roles.title: Special Education Teacher

rationales.taskAutomation: IEP paperwork drafting and progress-report generation are digitizable, but behavior support, physical accommodations, and legally-mandated case management dominate the role and resist automation.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generic LLM tools can draft IEP goal language, but no purpose-built special-education product has real classroom traction yet.
rationales.adoption: Only early-adopter districts use AI for IEP-drafting assistance; most special education staff still write documentation manually.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent could pre-fill IEP paperwork and progress notes, but individualized behavior intervention and compliance meetings require a certified human in the room.

tasksAtRisk: Drafting IEP goal language and progress-report boilerplate
tasksGrowing: Individualized behavior intervention and support
IEP meeting facilitation with families
Hands-on adaptive-equipment and physical support
Crisis de-escalation
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 25-2058 (Special Education Teachers, Secondary School), representative anchor across special-education teacher levels, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI can draft IEP paperwork and generate differentiated worksheets, cutting real administrative load. But behavior intervention, physical support, and the legally-mandated human judgment in IEP meetings keep this one of the lowest-displacement roles in education.

roles.title: Preschool Teacher

rationales.taskAutomation: The job is dominated by hands-on care, safety supervision, and play-based social-emotional development that current AI cannot perform.
rationales.toolMaturity: Little purpose-built AI tooling exists for early-childhood classrooms beyond generic content-generation apps for take-home materials.
rationales.adoption: AI adoption in early-childhood settings is rare, mostly limited to occasional lesson-idea generation.
rationales.agenticExposure: Physical supervision and safety of very young children put this work almost entirely out of reach of any autonomous agent.

tasksAtRisk: Generating take-home activity sheets and craft ideas
tasksGrowing: Play-based social-emotional development
Hands-on safety supervision and care
Early literacy and motor-skill development
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 25-2011 (Preschool Teachers, Except Special Education), p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: This is among the most AI-resistant jobs in the index -- hands-on care and safety supervision of very young children sit almost entirely outside current AI capability.

roles.title: Technical Writer

rationales.taskAutomation: API references, user guides, release notes, and FAQ content can be generated end-to-end from code, specs, and commit history at professional quality today.
rationales.toolMaturity: Docs-as-code AI tools and coding-copilot-adjacent generators are mature and integrated directly into engineering toolchains.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted documentation is now a mainstream expectation at software companies, tracking the broader adoption of AI coding tools.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can read a codebase, draft and update docs, and open pull requests autonomously for routine documentation — a genuine pipeline of digital handoffs.

tasksAtRisk: API reference generation from code comments and schemas
User guide and how-to drafts from feature specs
tasksGrowing: Documentation strategy for complex, multi-product systems
Developer experience (DX) and information architecture design
Technical content for developer marketing and thought leadership
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-3042 Technical Writers, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now drafts most reference and how-to documentation directly from code and specs, and agent pipelines can keep docs current without a writer opening the file. Technical writers who own information architecture and developer experience across a whole product line are the ones still driving headcount decisions.

roles.title: Translator

rationales.taskAutomation: Machine translation now handles routine document, website, and business translation at near-professional quality across major language pairs end-to-end; live interpretation, legal/literary nuance, and low-resource languages remain harder to fully automate.
rationales.toolMaturity: DeepL and frontier LLM translation are mature, commodity-grade tools embedded directly into localization and CAT-tool workflows.
rationales.adoption: Machine-translation-plus-post-editing is now the default production model across the localization industry, not a pilot.
rationales.agenticExposure: Detect-translate-QA-format pipelines can run end-to-end for high-volume content; only certified legal, medical, and literary translation reliably route to a human.

tasksAtRisk: First-pass translation of business documents and websites
Localization of software strings and UI text
Subtitle and caption translation for video content
tasksGrowing: Certified legal and medical translation with liability
Literary translation preserving voice and style
Live simultaneous interpretation in high-stakes settings
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-3091 Interpreters and Translators, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Machine translation has closed the quality gap for routine document and localization work to the point that most high-volume translation is now MT-plus-light-editing rather than translate-from-scratch. Translators without certified legal, medical, or literary specialization, or live interpretation skills, are competing directly with a free browser tab.

roles.title: Copy Editor

rationales.taskAutomation: Grammar, punctuation, consistency, and house-style enforcement are now reliably automated end-to-end; developmental judgment about structure, tone, and argument still needs a human editor.
rationales.toolMaturity: Grammarly and LLM-based editing tools are mature, commodity-grade, and embedded directly in most writing and publishing platforms.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted copyediting is now standard across publishing, content, and marketing teams, not an early-adopter behavior.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run a full manuscript through style-guide compliance, consistency, and proofreading passes with minimal human review for routine text.

tasksAtRisk: Grammar, punctuation, and mechanical error correction
Consistency checks (names, terms, formatting) across long documents
tasksGrowing: Developmental editing for structure and argument
Tone and voice calibration for brand or author identity
High-stakes legal, medical, and academic copyediting requiring liability sign-off
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-3041 Editors, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI-Assisted Editing Tools (Grammarly, LLM Copyedit Passes)

roles.insight: Mechanical copyediting is now handled reliably by AI at commodity cost, making this one of the more exposed roles in the Creative category. Editors doing developmental and high-liability work are the ones still needed.

roles.title: Graphic Designer

rationales.taskAutomation: Template-driven production work like social graphics, icon sets, and layout variations is now generated end-to-end; brand strategy and creative direction still require human judgment.
rationales.toolMaturity: Midjourney, Adobe Firefly, and Figma AI generate production-ready assets natively inside standard design toolchains.
rationales.adoption: Most marketing teams and agencies now default to AI-assisted design for first-pass concepts and routine production work.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run brief-to-asset pipelines end-to-end for routine formats, though brand-critical work still routes through a human.

tasksAtRisk: Generating social media graphics and ad variants from a brief
Producing logo concepts and iteration sets
Batch photo retouching and background removal
Resizing and reformatting creative across channels
Icon, pattern, and stock illustration generation
tasksGrowing: Brand identity systems and visual strategy
Creative direction across multi-channel campaigns
Motion design and interactive brand experiences
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-1024 Graphic Designers, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now produces most first-pass design work in seconds, and agentic pipelines can take a brief through export without a human touch for template-driven formats. Designers who own brand systems, creative direction, and motion work are pulling further ahead of production-only roles.

roles.title: Illustrator

rationales.taskAutomation: Commercial and concept illustration for stock, editorial, and game-adjacent use is now generated end-to-end from a prompt at usable quality; gallery-grade fine art and signature-style commissioned work remain human-differentiated.
rationales.toolMaturity: Midjourney, DALL-E, and Adobe Firefly are mature, widely-shipped image generators embedded in publishing and marketing production pipelines.
rationales.adoption: Publishers, ad agencies, and game studios routinely use AI generation for concept art and stock illustration in production, not just pilots.
rationales.agenticExposure: Prompt-iterate-deliver pipelines can produce and revise commercial illustration sets with minimal human input for routine briefs.

tasksAtRisk: Stock and editorial illustration for common subjects
Concept art variations and mood-board generation
Children's book and educational illustration drafts
Pattern, texture, and background art production
tasksGrowing: Signature-style commissioned and gallery fine art
Original character and world-building IP development
Art direction for licensed and brand-owned illustration
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-1013 Fine Artists, Including Painters, Sculptors, and Illustrators, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Generative image tools now cover most commercial illustration briefs at production quality, collapsing day-rate stock and concept work. Illustrators building recognizable, ownable style and original IP are the ones still landing licensing and commission deals.

roles.title: Video Editor

rationales.taskAutomation: Rough-cut assembly, auto-captioning, and preset-based color grading are now reliably automatable end-to-end; narrative pacing and complex compositing still need an editor's judgment.
rationales.toolMaturity: Runway, Descript, and CapCut's AI features are mature and standard in most content-team editing workflows.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted editing is mainstream for marketing and social content teams; high-end film and broadcast post still lean heavily on manual craft.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can ingest raw footage, auto-cut to a template, caption, and export for routine social and marketing formats without an editor in the loop.

tasksAtRisk: Rough cut assembly from raw footage
tasksGrowing: Narrative storytelling and pacing
Complex multi-cam and effects compositing
Creative direction and client collaboration
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-4032 Film and Video Editors, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now assembles rough cuts, captions, and color grades footage without an editor's involvement for routine formats. Editors who shape narrative pacing, run complex composites, and sit with clients through creative decisions are commanding the premium end of the rate card.

roles.title: Journalist

rationales.taskAutomation: AI reliably drafts commodity stories today — earnings recaps, sports summaries, weather, and aggregation — but original reporting, sourcing, and verification remain human-gated.
rationales.toolMaturity: LLM-based drafting, transcription, and summarization tools are mature and embedded in most newsroom CMS workflows.
rationales.adoption: AI drafting and research assistance is now standard at large digital newsrooms and wire services, spreading to mid-market outlets.
rationales.agenticExposure: Research-summarize-draft agent loops can own routine beat coverage and news aggregation, but original sourcing and editorial judgment stay outside agent reach.

tasksAtRisk: Earnings, market, and data-driven story drafts
Aggregating and summarizing wire and multi-source coverage
SEO headline and social copy variants for published stories
tasksGrowing: Original investigative reporting and human sourcing
Long-form feature writing with narrative voice
Analysis and opinion built on verified, on-the-record reporting
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-3023 News Analysts, Reporters, and Journalists, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now drafts most commodity news competently, and journalists increasingly edit AI output rather than write it from scratch. In an AI-saturated information environment, original reporting and verified sourcing carry a trust premium algorithms can't replicate.

roles.title: Voice Actor

rationales.taskAutomation: AI voice cloning and TTS now produce broadcast-usable narration, IVR prompts, and background character voices end-to-end; lead character work, emotional range, and union-protected game/animation roles are harder to fully automate.
rationales.toolMaturity: Voice-cloning and text-to-speech tools (ElevenLabs and peers) are mature and shipped directly in audiobook, e-learning, and IVR production platforms.
rationales.adoption: E-learning, corporate narration, and IVR production have largely adopted synthetic voice as the default; premium game and animation casting still leans human.
rationales.agenticExposure: Script-to-voice agent pipelines can generate and iterate full narration tracks for routine formats without a voice actor in the loop.

tasksAtRisk: IVR, phone-tree, and app notification voice prompts
Background and crowd character voices in games
tasksGrowing: Lead character voice work in premium games and animation
Union-protected performance capture and dubbing
Live and interactive voice performance (events, improv)
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-2011 Actors, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Synthetic voice is now broadcast-quality for narration, IVR, and background game voices, and studios default to it for cost reasons. Voice actors who hold lead roles, union protections, and irreplaceable performance range are the ones still getting booked.

roles.title: Animator

rationales.taskAutomation: Templated motion graphics, lower-thirds, and simple 2D animation are largely automatable today; character animation, rigging, and complex VFX sequences still require skilled human animators.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generative video tools (Runway, Pika) are improving fast and credible for short-form and pre-visualization work, but aren't yet reliable for production-grade character animation.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is concentrated in marketing and indie production; major studio animation pipelines remain largely manual and proprietary.
rationales.agenticExposure: Storyboard-to-rough-animation agent pipelines can produce pre-vis and short-form drafts, but final character and VFX work stays human-directed.

tasksAtRisk: Templated motion graphics and lower-thirds animation
In-between frame generation for basic 2D sequences
tasksGrowing: Character rigging and performance animation
Complex VFX sequence direction and compositing
Original visual style development for franchises
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-1014 Special Effects Artists and Animators, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Generative Video Tools for Pre-Vis (Runway, Pika)

roles.insight: Generative video tools now handle rough pre-visualization and templated motion graphics, compressing early-stage production time. Animators who own character performance, rigging, and full VFX pipelines remain the load-bearing talent studios build schedules around.

roles.title: Music Producer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI now generates full instrumental beats, loops, and passable mixes end-to-end, but shaping an artist's sonic identity and directing a full album remain outside reliable automation.
rationales.toolMaturity: Suno, Udio, Soundraw, and AI mastering tools (LANDR) are mature and widely used for track generation and finishing.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is concentrated among independent and bedroom producers; major-label studios still lean on human engineers for signature sound.
rationales.agenticExposure: Generate-mix-master pipelines are agent-ownable for demos and background tracks, but live session direction and artist collaboration are not.

tasksAtRisk: Generating beats, loops, and instrumental backing tracks
AI mastering passes for streaming release
Royalty-free background music and jingle production
tasksGrowing: Artist development and creative vision-setting
Live session direction and performance engineering
Full album production and cohesive sonic identity
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-2012 Producers and Directors, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Generative Music Tools (Suno, Udio, AI Mastering)

roles.insight: Generative tools now produce usable beats and masters in minutes, pulling revenue from commodity production work. Producers who build an artist's sound over a full body of work, and who can run a live session, are the ones labels still pay for.

roles.title: Author

rationales.taskAutomation: LLMs draft competent formulaic prose, outlines, and genre fiction at speed, but original literary voice, narrative innovation, and brand-name authorship remain outside reliable automation.
rationales.toolMaturity: Frontier LLMs are mature, general-purpose drafting tools; no author-specific product has displaced general writing assistants.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is concentrated among self-published and genre-fiction authors using AI for drafting and editing; traditional publishing adoption is far more cautious and often contractually restricted.
rationales.agenticExposure: Outline-draft-revise agent loops can produce full manuscript drafts in genre categories, though publishable literary quality still needs heavy human authorship.

tasksAtRisk: Formulaic genre fiction drafting (romance, thriller tropes)
Book description, blurb, and marketing copy
tasksGrowing: Original literary voice and narrative innovation
Brand-name authorship and reader trust
Ghostwriting requiring deep subject-matter collaboration
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-3043 Writers and Authors, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI can now produce a passable formulaic draft in any popular genre in minutes, flooding self-publishing categories with volume. Authors whose voice, originality, and platform readers actively seek out are the ones still commanding advances and backlist sales.

roles.title: Photographer

rationales.taskAutomation: Stock and product photography, plus batch retouching, are largely reproducible by generative image tools; event, portrait, and photojournalism work still require physical presence and human rapport.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generative image tools (Midjourney, Firefly) and AI editing suites (Lightroom AI, Luminar) are mature and standard in the workflow.
rationales.adoption: E-commerce and stock-photo buyers have shifted much of their volume to AI generation; event and portrait segments show far less adoption.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agent pipelines can shoot-list, batch-edit, and tag product catalogs end-to-end; they cannot replace a photographer's physical presence at a shoot.

tasksAtRisk: Stock photography production for common subjects
Batch photo editing, retouching, and background removal
Photo metadata, tagging, and catalog organization
tasksGrowing: Event and portrait photography requiring physical presence
Creative direction and art direction for shoots
Specialized photography (architecture, food, fashion editorial)
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-4021 Photographers, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Generative image tools have largely displaced commodity stock and product photography. Photographers whose work depends on being physically present — weddings, events, editorial shoots — remain far more insulated than catalog and stock work.

roles.title: Sound Designer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can generate stock sound effects, basic foley, and ambient beds from a text prompt; complex spatial mixing, foley performance, and final dub-stage work for film and premium games still need a human sound designer.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generative audio and sound-effect tools are credible and improving fast, but aren't yet a substitute for a trained ear on a dub stage.
rationales.adoption: Indie game and podcast production have adopted generative sound effects meaningfully faster than film and AAA game post houses.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can generate and layer stock sound-effect libraries against a scene description, but final spatial mix and creative sound decisions stay human.

tasksAtRisk: Stock sound-effect and ambience generation from text prompts
Basic foley layering for routine scenes
tasksGrowing: Final spatial mix and dub-stage work for film and premium games
Original sonic identity design for franchises
Live foley performance and creative sound direction
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-4014 Sound Engineering Technicians, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Generative audio tools now cover stock sound effects and basic foley, but the final mix that makes a scene actually land still needs a trained ear. This is a role where AI eats the library work, not the craft.

roles.title: Fashion Designer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can generate print, pattern, and trend-forecast concepts quickly, but garment construction, fit engineering, and brand-defining silhouette decisions remain designer-led.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generative print, textile, and trend-forecasting tools are credible and improving, though no product yet handles pattern-to-garment execution end-to-end.
rationales.adoption: Fast-fashion and print-heavy segments are adopting AI concept generation faster than luxury and made-to-measure segments.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can generate and iterate print and trend concepts against a brief, but pattern-making, fit sessions, and production oversight stay human.

tasksAtRisk: Print, pattern, and textile concept generation
Trend forecasting and mood board assembly
Basic tech-pack drafting from a design brief
tasksGrowing: Garment construction and fit engineering
Brand-defining silhouette and collection direction
Sustainable materials sourcing and production oversight
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-1022 Fashion Designers, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI speeds up print and trend concept work, but a collection still lives or dies on fit, construction, and a designer's point of view. Concept-stage work is commoditizing faster than the craft of making clothes that fit.

roles.title: Screenwriter

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can draft scene outlines, coverage, and rough dialogue passes quickly, but structuring an original produced screenplay with a distinct authorial voice remains largely human work.
rationales.toolMaturity: General-purpose LLMs draft usable prose and dialogue, but no screenwriting-specific product has displaced the writer's own process.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is constrained by WGA contract guardrails limiting AI use in credited screenwriting; usage is higher in unscripted, content-mill, and indie-web writing.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can generate script coverage and first-pass outlines quickly, but a produced screenplay still requires a credited human author under current guild rules.

tasksAtRisk: Dialogue pass suggestions for minor characters
tasksGrowing: Original produced screenplay structure and voice
Showrunner-level story and character arc development
Collaborative rewrites requiring credited authorship
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-3043 Writers and Authors, p25-p75 rounded (screenwriting skews above the general-writer median per WGA minimums)
roles.insight: AI drafts serviceable coverage and rough outlines fast, but guild rules and the need for a distinct authorial voice keep credited screenwriting largely human for now. That guardrail is a contract fact, not a permanent technical one.

roles.title: Art Director

rationales.taskAutomation: Art directors' core work — evaluating concepts, aligning stakeholders, and making final creative calls — is judgment- and relationship-driven and resists full automation, even though the assets they review are increasingly AI-generated.
rationales.toolMaturity: The generative tools art directors direct (Firefly, Midjourney) are mature and standard, even though no tool automates the director's own judgment.
rationales.adoption: Nearly every creative team now uses AI generation somewhere in its pipeline, which art directors are expected to evaluate and direct.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can produce and pre-filter concept variations for review, but final creative sign-off and stakeholder alignment stay with the director.

tasksAtRisk: First-pass concept generation for creative review
Producing multiple visual directions for client pitches
Routine asset quality-checking against brand guidelines
tasksGrowing: Final creative sign-off and stakeholder alignment
Cross-discipline team direction and mentorship
Original campaign concept development
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-1011 Art Directors, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now generates most of the raw creative options an art director reviews, but the judgment call of what actually ships still sits with a human. The job is shifting from making things to curating and directing what AI makes.

roles.title: Interior Designer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can generate mood boards, rendering concepts, and layout options quickly, but site measurement, code compliance, vendor coordination, and client relationship management remain human-gated.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI rendering and visualization tools are credible and improving fast, but no product yet handles the full designer workflow from concept to procurement.
rationales.adoption: A growing share of designers use AI for early-concept renders and mood boards, but most of the specification and procurement workflow stays manual.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can produce and iterate visualization concepts quickly, but on-site work, code compliance, and vendor negotiation stay outside agent reach.

tasksAtRisk: Mood board and concept rendering generation
Furniture and material option curation from a brief
tasksGrowing: On-site space planning and code-compliance review
Client relationship management and vendor negotiation
Custom millwork and bespoke design detailing
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-1025 Interior Designers, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now speeds up mood boards and concept renders, but the physical realities of a space — code compliance, site conditions, procurement — still require a human designer on-site. Concept visualization is commoditizing faster than the execution work around it.

roles.title: Set Designer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can generate concept renders and mood boards for a set quickly, but physical build feasibility, materials sourcing, and on-site construction oversight remain hands-on work.
rationales.toolMaturity: Visualization and rendering tools are moderately mature, but no product manages the physical build process a set designer runs.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is limited to early concept and pitch-deck visualization; production and build phases remain manual.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can generate and iterate concept visuals, but they cannot walk a soundstage floor or supervise a build crew.

tasksAtRisk: Concept rendering and mood-board generation for pitches
tasksGrowing: Physical build feasibility and materials engineering
On-site construction supervision and crew coordination
Original set concepts for signature productions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-1027 Set and Exhibit Designers, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI speeds up concept pitches and mood boards, but a set designer's real value is running a physical build a director can actually shoot on. That work stays firmly in the physical world.

roles.title: Choreographer

rationales.taskAutomation: Creating and directing original dance and movement is an embodied, in-person craft with almost no automatable digital component.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI motion-generation exists for game and film pre-visualization but has no real product targeting live choreography or dance direction.
rationales.adoption: Adoption in professional dance and stage choreography is essentially nonexistent outside novelty digital-avatar projects.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents have no meaningful path into directing live performers' physical movement.

tasksAtRisk: Reference-clip research and archival movement documentation
Music-cue timing sheets and rehearsal-note transcription
tasksGrowing: Original movement creation and performance direction
Live rehearsal coaching and performer feedback
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-2032 Choreographers, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Choreography is one of the most physically embodied creative professions, and current AI has essentially no path into directing a live performer's movement. Administrative and documentation tasks around the craft are the only exposed slice.

roles.title: Craft Artist

rationales.taskAutomation: Making handmade objects — pottery, weaving, glasswork, jewelry — by hand is physical craft work with almost no automatable digital component.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generic AI design tools can suggest patterns or inspiration images, but no tool performs the physical making itself.
rationales.adoption: AI adoption among working craft artists is essentially limited to inspiration-gathering and online shop listing copy.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents have no path into physically forming, firing, or assembling handmade objects.

tasksAtRisk: Online shop listing copy and product photography prep
Social media caption and marketing copy for a maker's shop
tasksGrowing: Original handmade object creation and technique mastery
Teaching workshops and building a maker community
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-1012 Craft Artists, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Craft art is about as physically embodied as creative work gets, and current AI has no path into the actual making. The only exposed slice is the online-shop marketing layer around the craft.

roles.title: Talent Sourcer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI sourcing agents now build boolean-searched candidate lists, enrich profiles, and send first-touch outreach end-to-end; sourcers increasingly step in only when a candidate replies.
rationales.toolMaturity: Sourcing platforms (SeekOut, hireEZ, LinkedIn Recruiter, agentic outbound tools) are mature, standard products in the sourcing stack.
rationales.adoption: AI-driven sourcing is now the mainstream default for high-volume and tech hiring pipelines.
rationales.agenticExposure: Autonomous sourcing agents can run the full search-enrich-outreach loop across dozens of roles simultaneously without a human touching each candidate.

tasksAtRisk: Boolean and semantic candidate searches across job boards and LinkedIn
Candidate profile enrichment and contact-data lookup
First-touch outreach messaging and sequenced follow-ups
tasksGrowing: Niche and passive-talent market research for hard-to-fill roles
Sourcing strategy design for new AI-sourcing tool rollouts
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1071, p25-p75 rounded (entry tier within HR specialists)
roles.insight: Agentic sourcing tools now run the full search-enrich-outreach loop unattended, which is why this is one of the most exposed jobs in recruiting. Sourcers who survive move into niche market research or into designing the sourcing stack instead of running it by hand.

roles.title: Recruiter

rationales.taskAutomation: AI now screens resumes, drafts outreach sequences, schedules interviews, and writes job descriptions end-to-end, leaving relationship-building and closing as the core human task.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI sourcing and screening platforms (SeekOut, hireEZ, LinkedIn Recruiter AI) are mature, widely-deployed tools embedded in the standard ATS stack.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted sourcing and screening is now a mainstream expectation across tech, finance, and retail hiring functions.
rationales.agenticExposure: Autonomous agents can run full sourcing-to-scheduling pipelines for high-volume roles; recruiters increasingly own only candidate persuasion and executive-level searches.

tasksAtRisk: Resume screening and shortlisting against role criteria
Initial candidate outreach and sequenced follow-up messages
Job description drafting from role templates
Candidate pipeline status updates and reporting
tasksGrowing: Candidate relationship-building and offer negotiation for competitive talent
Executive and specialized-skill search strategy
Employer branding and talent-market intelligence
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1071, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now runs the sourcing-to-scheduling pipeline end-to-end for most volume hiring, and agentic tools are starting to run full outreach sequences unattended. Recruiters who survive the cut are the ones selling hard-to-close candidates and running searches AI can't source its way into.

roles.title: Compensation Analyst

rationales.taskAutomation: AI now benchmarks market pay data, runs job-evaluation matrices, and drafts pay-equity reports end-to-end, but executive comp design and total-rewards strategy still require human judgment.
rationales.toolMaturity: Comp platforms like Payscale, Pave, and Mercer's AI tools are mature, widely-shipped products for market pricing and pay-equity modeling.
rationales.adoption: Most large employers and a growing share of the mid-market now run AI-assisted compensation benchmarking as standard practice.
rationales.agenticExposure: Multi-step agents can pull survey data, run equity audits, and draft benchmarking reports unattended, though sign-off on pay decisions stays human.

tasksAtRisk: Market salary benchmarking pulled and normalized by AI across survey sources
Pay equity audits run end-to-end by compliance-analytics agents
Job evaluation and leveling scored against AI-built architecture models
Compensation survey submission and participation handled by automated data feeds
tasksGrowing: Executive and equity compensation design for retention-critical roles
Total rewards strategy tied to AI-driven workforce planning
Pay transparency law interpretation and multi-state compliance strategy
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1141, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI Compensation Analytics Platforms (Payscale, Pave, Mercer AI)

roles.insight: AI now runs most market benchmarking and pay-equity audits directly from survey data, collapsing the analyst hours once spent compiling comp studies. Analysts who move into executive compensation design, equity strategy, and multi-state pay-transparency compliance are absorbing the higher-value work employers still won't hand to a model.

roles.title: Benefits Administrator

rationales.taskAutomation: AI now processes open enrollment, plan-change life events, and COBRA administration end-to-end; plan design and vendor negotiation still need a person.
rationales.toolMaturity: Benefits platforms (Workday, ADP, Businessolver) ship mature AI enrollment assistants and eligibility engines.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted benefits enrollment and eligibility processing is standard practice at most mid-size and large employers.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run the full enrollment-eligibility-compliance-filing loop for standard plan changes without a human touching each case.

tasksAtRisk: Open enrollment processing and eligibility verification
Life-event benefit changes and COBRA administration
Benefits plan documentation and summary plan description updates
Vendor invoice reconciliation and compliance filing
tasksGrowing: Benefits plan design and vendor contract negotiation
Employee benefits education and total-rewards communication strategy
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1141, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now runs enrollment, eligibility, and COBRA administration end-to-end. Administrators who move into plan design and vendor negotiation keep the work AI can't yet own.

roles.title: Training Coordinator

rationales.taskAutomation: AI now handles session scheduling, attendance certificates, catalog upkeep, and survey distribution end-to-end, but designing learning programs that actually move performance still needs a person.
rationales.toolMaturity: LMS platforms with built-in AI scheduling, content tagging, and reporting are mature and standard in most L&D stacks.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted LMS administration is common practice across mid-size and large employers' training functions.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run the full scheduling-tracking-reporting loop for a training calendar; program design and vendor strategy stay human-led.

tasksAtRisk: Training session scheduling and room or platform booking
Course catalog updates and content tagging
Post-training survey distribution and basic results summarization
tasksGrowing: Learning program strategy and ROI measurement tied to business outcomes
AI upskilling curriculum design and rollout
Vendor evaluation and learning-technology partnership management
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1151, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: LMS Administration with AI Scheduling & Reporting

roles.insight: AI now runs the scheduling, tracking, and survey mechanics of a training calendar without a coordinator touching it. The job is narrowing to program design and proving learning ROI, work that determines whether the AI upskilling push actually changes performance.

roles.title: HR Generalist

rationales.taskAutomation: AI chatbots now resolve routine policy questions, onboarding checklists, and leave-tracking end to end, but complex employee-relations cases and culture work still need a human generalist.
rationales.toolMaturity: General-purpose HR chatbots and HRIS AI add-ons (Workday Assistant, Lattice AI) are mature and widely deployed for FAQ and case intake.
rationales.adoption: Most mid-size and large employers now run an AI layer in front of first-line HR inquiries and onboarding.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can triage tickets, populate onboarding steps, and draft standard policy responses, but employee-relations judgment calls stay with the generalist.

tasksAtRisk: Employee FAQ and policy-lookup responses handled by an HR chatbot
New-hire onboarding checklist creation and tracking automated end-to-end
Leave and time-off case intake triaged by AI before human review
Standard employment verification letters generated automatically
tasksGrowing: Complex employee-relations investigations and conflict resolution
Employee experience and engagement program design
HR technology rollout and change management across teams
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1071, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI HR Chatbot & Case Management Tools (Workday Assistant, Lattice AI)

roles.insight: Chatbots and HRIS assistants now close out most routine FAQ, onboarding, and leave-tracking work that used to fill a generalist's week. The role is consolidating around employee relations, culture, and rolling out the HR tech itself, work that still needs a person in the room.

roles.title: HRIS Analyst

rationales.taskAutomation: AI now builds standard HRIS reports, configures routine workflow rules, and flags data-quality errors, but system architecture decisions and vendor integration design still need an analyst.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI copilots inside Workday, SAP SuccessFactors, and UKG are mature for report-building and basic configuration.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted HRIS reporting and configuration is common at large employers running modern HR tech stacks.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run recurring report generation, data audits, and standard configuration change requests end-to-end.

tasksAtRisk: Standard HRIS report and dashboard generation
User access provisioning and basic troubleshooting tickets
tasksGrowing: HR system architecture and multi-system integration design
Vendor evaluation and HR tech roadmap planning
AI-agent governance within the HRIS itself
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1071, p25-p75 rounded with allowance toward systems-analyst market postings

defenseSkills.skill: HRIS Platform Configuration (Workday, SAP SuccessFactors, UKG)
AI Agent Governance in HR Systems

roles.insight: AI copilots now build the routine reports and configuration changes that filled an HRIS analyst's queue. The role is shifting toward system architecture and governing the AI agents now embedded in the platform itself.

roles.title: HR Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: AI drafts policy documentation, schedules compliance training, and processes routine benefits questions, but building culture and resolving complex people issues remains squarely managerial judgment.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted HR platforms (Workday AI, Lattice, LLM-based policy drafting) are mature and standard across most HRIS stacks.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted policy drafting and reporting workflows are now mainstream in mid-size and large HR departments.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can handle policy drafting and scheduling workflows, but org design, culture strategy, and workforce planning stay with the manager.

tasksAtRisk: Policy documentation and handbook drafting from templates
Standard employee inquiry and benefits-question responses
Compliance training scheduling and completion tracking
Routine HR reporting and headcount dashboards
tasksGrowing: Employee relations and complex conflict resolution
Organizational culture and engagement strategy
AI workforce-planning and internal AI-use policy development
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-3121, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI HR Platform Administration (Workday AI, Lattice)
