roles.insight: AI now drafts the policy documents and runs the routine reporting that used to eat an HR manager's calendar. What's left, and growing, is the part that scales with judgment: culture strategy, thorny employee-relations calls, and writing the rules for how the org itself uses AI.

roles.title: Executive Recruiter

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can generate long-lists and map talent markets, but courting confidential C-suite candidates, closing negotiations, and board-level trust-building resist automation.
rationales.toolMaturity: General AI market-mapping and sourcing tools apply, but no dedicated executive-search product has real traction beyond assisting research.
rationales.adoption: Executive search remains a relationship-first, low-adoption niche; most firms use AI only for background research, not candidate engagement.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can build longlists and compile market-intelligence briefs, but the confidential, high-trust engagement work stays entirely human.

tasksAtRisk: Market-mapping and long-list generation from AI-searched public data
Background research and compensation benchmarking on shortlisted candidates
Search-status reporting and pipeline documentation for the client
tasksGrowing: Confidential C-suite candidate engagement and trust-building
Board-level search strategy and succession-planning advisory
Offer negotiation and counter-offer management for senior hires
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1071 (Human Resources Specialists), p75 extended toward executive-search market postings
roles.insight: AI compiles the market maps and background research that used to take a search associate weeks, but persuading a sitting CEO to consider a move is not a prompt-able task. Executive recruiters who lean into board relationships and succession advisory are among the least exposed roles in recruiting.

roles.title: Labor Relations Specialist

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can search contract language and flag grievance precedents, but negotiating collective bargaining terms and representing the company in arbitration remain human, legally consequential work.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generic contract-analysis and legal-research AI applies to CBA lookup, but no dedicated labor-relations negotiation tool has real traction.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is low: unionized and heavily regulated employers move cautiously on AI in labor relations.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can retrieve contract clauses and past-grievance precedent, but the negotiation and arbitration representation itself is out of reach.

tasksAtRisk: Collective bargaining agreement clause lookup and precedent research
Routine labor-law compliance monitoring and reporting
tasksGrowing: Collective bargaining negotiation strategy
Arbitration and mediation representation
Union relationship management during organizing campaigns
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1075, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI can pull contract clauses and grievance precedent in seconds, but it can't sit across the table in a bargaining session or represent the company in arbitration. This keeps labor relations one of the lowest-exposure specialist roles in HR.

roles.title: Program Coordinator

rationales.taskAutomation: Scheduling, tracker updates, meeting-minute distribution, and status-deck compilation make up most of a program coordinator's week, and AI now handles that reliably; only escalation judgment and stakeholder follow-through resist full automation.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI scheduling assistants, meeting-note tools, and project-tracker automations (Asana AI, Notion AI, Calendly AI) are mature, widely shipped, and already the default in offices that use these platforms.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is spreading fast beyond tech into professional services, healthcare administration, and nonprofits wherever a PMO already runs on Asana, Monday, or Smartsheet.
rationales.agenticExposure: A program coordinator's core loop, track a task, chase a status, update a tracker, remind a stakeholder, is close to an agent spec; what remains is judgment about which chases actually matter.

tasksAtRisk: Document and logistics coordination for program events
tasksGrowing: Escalation judgment on which follow-ups matter
Stakeholder relationship-building across teams
Onboarding and process documentation for new programs
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1082 Project Management Specialists (junior/coordinator-level), anchored toward the lower end of the p25-p75 range

defenseSkills.skill: AI Scheduling & Tracker Automation (Asana AI, Notion AI)
Agentic AI Literacy for Program Operations

roles.insight: Most of what fills a program coordinator's day, scheduling, tracker updates, minute-taking, is now a background agent task, not a job. Coordinators who move into judgment work, deciding which fire actually needs a human, are the ones who keep a seat.

roles.title: Scrum Master

rationales.taskAutomation: Sprint metrics, burndown and velocity charts, and retro data aggregation are now fully AI-generated from tool telemetry, but facilitating a team through conflict and building trust in the room is not something AI performs.
rationales.toolMaturity: Native AI reporting is now standard in Jira, Azure DevOps, and Zoom/Teams meeting tools, giving Scrum teams auto-generated retros and velocity trends out of the box.
rationales.adoption: Nearly every tech organization running Scrum now uses AI-generated sprint reporting by default; adoption is close to universal in software teams.
rationales.agenticExposure: Metrics rollups and meeting-note summarization are fully agent-ownable today; team coaching, impediment removal requiring political capital, and building psychological safety are not.

tasksGrowing: Team coaching and psychological safety building
Organizational agility transformation
Impediment resolution requiring political skill
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1082 Project Management Specialists, p25-p75 rounded (no dedicated Scrum Master SOC code)
roles.insight: Burndown charts and retro summaries are now fully automated from tool telemetry, and no one misses making them by hand. That leaves the Scrum Master's real job exposed: can they actually build a team's trust, mediate a conflict, and clear a political impediment? Those who only ran ceremonies are the most at risk.

roles.title: Project Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Status reports, scheduling updates, meeting notes, and resource allocation for standard projects are now largely AI-generated, but scope negotiation, stakeholder conflict resolution, and judgment calls under uncertainty remain human-led.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI features are now default in mainstream PM software (Asana, Monday, MS Project, Notion) with auto-summarized meetings and predictive scheduling shipped, not experimental.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted project tracking is now the norm across tech, finance, and increasingly construction and healthcare IT projects; only the most bespoke or compliance-heavy shops still run manual boards.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can already own the reporting-scheduling-tracking loop end to end for straightforward projects, but escalations, vendor negotiation, and team motivation still route to the human project manager.

tasksAtRisk: Meeting notes and action item tracking
tasksGrowing: Stakeholder management and conflict resolution
Risk management for complex, ambiguous programs
Cross-functional team leadership and motivation
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1082 Project Management Specialists, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI Project Copilots (Asana AI, Monday AI, MS Project Copilot)

roles.insight: Status decks, timeline updates, and action-item chasing are now a few prompts, not a Friday afternoon. Project managers whose entire job was reporting are being squeezed hardest, while those who negotiate scope, resolve conflict, and keep a team moving through ambiguity are seeing their role expand into program-level trust-building.

roles.title: Product Owner

rationales.taskAutomation: Backlog grooming, acceptance-criteria drafting, and user-story writing from templates are now heavily AI-assisted, but prioritizing conflicting stakeholder demands against a fixed budget of engineering time still requires human judgment and accountability.
rationales.toolMaturity: Jira, Azure DevOps, and Productboard now ship AI backlog-refinement and story-drafting features that are genuinely useful day to day, though they still need a human to approve trade-offs.
rationales.adoption: Most software teams running Scrum or Kanban with a dedicated Product Owner now use AI to draft stories and groom backlogs; adoption is close to mainstream in tech but thinner in traditional industries running agile-in-name-only.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can already draft a sprint's worth of user stories and acceptance criteria from a rough feature brief; deciding which of those stories actually ships this quarter stays with the Product Owner.

tasksGrowing: Stakeholder prioritization trade-offs
Value-based roadmap decisions
Cross-team dependency negotiation
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1082 Project Management Specialists, p25-p75 rounded (no dedicated Product Owner SOC code; closest to Product Manager duties)

defenseSkills.skill: AI Backlog & Story Drafting Tools (Jira AI, Productboard)
Agentic AI Literacy for Product Teams

roles.insight: AI now drafts most of the backlog: user stories, acceptance criteria, even release notes, from a short feature brief. That collapses the writing half of the job but sharpens the real one: which ten stories out of a hundred actually get built this sprint. Product Owners who can defend a prioritization call to five stakeholders at once are becoming harder to replace, not easier.

roles.title: Program Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Status aggregation, dependency tracking, and capacity spreadsheets are already handled well by AI, but arbitrating trade-offs across competing programs and owning delivery risk is not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Portfolio tools like Jira Align, Smartsheet, and Monday.com now ship credible AI status-rollup and forecasting features, though they still require human sign-off on program health calls.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is strong in tech and finance program offices but lags in government and regulated programs where reporting cadences are still manually gated.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can already draft cross-project status rollups and flag schedule slippage, but executive relationship management and cross-program risk trade-offs stay with the human program manager.

tasksGrowing: Multi-program strategic alignment
Executive and sponsor relationship management
Cross-program risk arbitration and trade-off decisions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1082 Project Management Specialists (program-level roles cross-walk to this detailed occupation), p25-p75 with upward allowance for senior tech/finance program offices

defenseSkills.skill: AI Portfolio & Program Dashboards (Jira Align, Smartsheet AI)
Agentic Workflow Governance for Multi-Program Delivery

roles.insight: AI now auto-generates the status rollups and dependency maps that used to eat a program manager's Friday afternoons. What it can't do is decide which of three conflicting program priorities the VP actually cares about, or hold a vendor accountable after a missed milestone. Program managers who move from status-reporting to risk-arbitration are becoming more valuable, not less.

roles.title: Product Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Research synthesis, competitive analysis pulls, and first-draft PRDs are now largely AI-assisted, but setting product vision, making prioritization trade-offs, and owning outcomes are not automatable end-to-end.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI copilots inside Productboard, Notion, and Jira plus generalist LLMs handle drafting and synthesis well, but purpose-built PM agents are still maturing rather than fully embedded the way coding copilots are.
rationales.adoption: Most tech-forward companies now expect PMs to use AI for research synthesis and doc drafting, and the practice is spreading into mid-market SaaS, though it is not yet universal outside tech.
rationales.agenticExposure: Multi-step agents can already run research-synthesize-draft loops for specs and market scans, but cannot own prioritization calls or cross-team negotiation.

tasksAtRisk: Competitive analysis and market research synthesis
tasksGrowing: Product vision and strategic bets
Cross-functional prioritization and trade-off decisions
Customer discovery and stakeholder trust-building
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1082 Project Management Specialists (closest detailed occupation; no dedicated Product Manager SOC code), p25-p75 with upward allowance for senior tech PM roles per market postings

defenseSkills.skill: AI Agent Orchestration for Product Workflows
Prompt-Driven PRD and Roadmap Tooling (Productboard AI, Notion AI)

roles.insight: AI now drafts specs and synthesizes user feedback in minutes, collapsing work that used to take a day. That is compressing junior PM headcount at some startups, but it is raising the bar for PMs who can translate ambiguous business goals into hard prioritization calls. The scarcest skill in 2026 isn't writing PRDs, it's deciding which ten features not to build.

roles.title: Technical Program Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Cross-team dependency tracking and technical status rollups are increasingly AI-assisted, but arbitrating architecture trade-offs between engineering teams and driving technical risk decisions for a launch require judgment AI can't yet own.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted dependency mapping and technical status tooling (GitHub Projects, Jira with Copilot integrations) are improving quickly but aren't yet a mature, purpose-built category the way coding copilots are.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is concentrated in the large tech companies where TPM roles are most common; the role and its tooling haven't spread broadly into mid-market or non-tech firms.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can already draft cross-team dependency maps and flag technical risk from ticket and repo data, but negotiating an architecture trade-off between two engineering leads still needs a human in the room.

tasksAtRisk: Technical status rollups from repos and ticket systems
tasksGrowing: Architecture trade-off arbitration across teams
Technical risk escalation and mitigation strategy
Cross-org launch alignment and negotiation
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1082 Project Management Specialists as base anchor; high end extended toward market postings for big-tech TPM roles, which run well above the OES median for the closest detailed occupation

defenseSkills.skill: AI-Assisted Dependency & Repo Analytics (GitHub Projects, Jira+Copilot)
Agentic AI Literacy for Engineering Programs

roles.insight: AI can already map dependencies across a dozen engineering teams and flag a slipping timeline before a human notices, but it can't sit in a room and get two staff engineers to agree on an architecture trade-off. TPMs whose value is that negotiation, not the status deck, are largely insulated.

roles.title: Engagement Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Status reporting, SOW and deliverable tracking, and standard client update decks are now largely AI-drafted, but de-escalating an at-risk client relationship or renegotiating scope mid-engagement stays firmly human.
rationales.toolMaturity: Contract and SOW drafting assistants (Ironclad AI and similar) plus generalist LLMs cover the paperwork well, but there is no mature, purpose-built 'engagement management' product category yet.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted reporting and contract drafting is common in consulting and tech engagement teams, but adoption is uneven outside those sectors.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can already draft status updates and flag deliverable slippage from project data, but they can't read a client's frustration in a room and adjust the relationship in real time.

tasksAtRisk: Client status reporting and update decks
tasksGrowing: At-risk client relationship management
Scope renegotiation and expansion selling
Cross-functional delivery escalation ownership
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1111 Management Analysts (closest detailed occupation for client-facing engagement/delivery leadership), p25-p75 rounded, upper end extended for tech/consulting market postings

defenseSkills.skill: AI Contract & SOW Drafting Tools (Ironclad AI)
Agentic AI Literacy for Delivery Operations

roles.insight: AI now drafts the status decks and flags slipping deliverables before an engagement manager even opens the project tracker. What still requires a human is reading a client's mood in a steering committee meeting and knowing when to eat a scope change instead of billing for it.

roles.title: Construction Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Scheduling, budgeting, RFI tracking and progress reporting are substantially digital and automatable, but on-site coordination, safety judgment and subcontractor negotiation are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-enabled project-management and BIM-coordination platforms (scheduling optimization, cost forecasting, clash detection) are credible and improving fast, short of full maturity.
rationales.adoption: Mid-size and large general contractors have adopted AI-assisted scheduling and cost-forecasting tools; small residential builders mostly have not.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can draft schedules, flag budget overruns and route RFIs, a real workflow slice, while site safety calls and subcontractor disputes stay human.

tasksGrowing: On-site safety judgment and incident response
Subcontractor negotiation and dispute resolution
Owner and stakeholder relationship management
Adapting schedules to real-world site surprises (weather, supply delays)
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 11-9021, p25-p75 rounded, upper end extended toward large commercial project postings

defenseSkills.skill: AI-assisted scheduling and cost-forecasting platform fluency

roles.insight: Construction management is the highest-exposure role in this category because the job is half digital pipeline: schedules, budgets, RFIs and progress reports are exactly what AI project-management tools now handle, and mid-size and large contractors are already running them. What still requires a human is the part no software owns: reading a job site, negotiating with subcontractors, and making the safety call when the plan and reality diverge.

roles.title: General Contractor

rationales.taskAutomation: Bidding, scheduling and subcontractor-payment paperwork are substantially digital and automatable; on-site coordination and client relationships are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted bidding, scheduling and project-management tools are credible and improving, similar to those used by larger construction managers but at smaller-project scale.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is growing among small-to-mid-size general contractors using AI-assisted estimating and scheduling software; adoption still trails large commercial firms.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can draft a bid package and track subcontractor payments; it cannot walk the site or resolve a client dispute.

tasksGrowing: On-site quality control and subcontractor coordination
Client relationship management and change-order negotiation
Adapting scope to unforeseen site conditions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 11-9021, p25-p75 rounded (residential/small-commercial general-contractor scale)

defenseSkills.skill: AI-assisted bidding and scheduling software fluency

roles.insight: General contractors carry meaningful exposure on the paperwork side, bidding, scheduling and permitting are increasingly software-driven, while site coordination and client relationships remain a human function.

roles.title: Surveyor

rationales.taskAutomation: Drone photogrammetry and AI point-cloud processing automate a real share of data collection and mapping, but legal boundary determination and site judgment remain professional, liability-bearing work.
rationales.toolMaturity: LiDAR, drone photogrammetry and AI-assisted point-cloud processing software are mature and widely deployed in the surveying industry.
rationales.adoption: Mainstream among surveying firms serving construction and infrastructure clients; nearly universal for larger firms.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can process drone and LiDAR data into a preliminary map; it cannot certify a legal boundary or resolve a title dispute.

tasksAtRisk: Drone and LiDAR data capture and point-cloud processing
tasksGrowing: Legal boundary determination and title-dispute resolution
Certified professional judgment on ambiguous or historic boundary evidence
Client and legal-counsel consultation on survey findings
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 17-1022, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: LiDAR/drone photogrammetry and AI point-cloud processing fluency
Professional land surveyor (PLS) licensure and certification
Legal boundary and title-dispute consulting skills

roles.insight: Surveying carries the highest exposure among the new trades here: drone and LiDAR data capture with AI point-cloud processing is mature and mainstream, compressing field-crew time, while licensed boundary determination remains a professional, liability-bearing function AI tools only support.

roles.title: CNC Operator

rationales.taskAutomation: Program loading, cycle monitoring and basic quality checks are largely automatable now that CAM-generated programs run with minimal operator input.
rationales.toolMaturity: CNC automation and AI-assisted monitoring tools are mature and standard in modern manufacturing.
rationales.adoption: Widely adopted in manufacturing and machine shops running current-generation CNC equipment.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent-adjacent automated cell can run a program and flag deviations end-to-end; a human is still needed for setup, tool changes and exceptions.

tasksAtRisk: Basic tool-wear alerts and replacement scheduling
tasksGrowing: Multi-machine cell setup and changeover
Exception handling when automated checks flag anomalies
Cross-training toward CNC programming and CAM software
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 51-9161, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: CNC programming and CAM software cross-training
Multi-machine cell setup and changeover skills

roles.insight: CNC operating is one of the higher-exposure trades in this category: modern machines already run programs and quality checks with minimal input, and adoption is mainstream in manufacturing. The mixed outlook reflects reshoring-driven demand for machine time colliding with fewer operators needed per machine.

roles.title: Home Inspector

rationales.taskAutomation: Photo-based defect detection and report generation are increasingly automatable, but crawlspace, roof and mechanical-system judgment calls in the field are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted image analysis for defect flagging and automated report-writing tools are credible and shipping in the inspection software market.
rationales.adoption: A growing share of home-inspection companies use AI-assisted reporting software; adoption is not yet universal.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can flag likely defects from inspection photos and draft the report, not physically test a system or access a tight crawlspace.

tasksAtRisk: Photo-based defect flagging and report drafting
tasksGrowing: Structural and mechanical-system judgment calls in the field
Client walkthrough consultation and Q&A
Specialty inspections (radon, mold, pool) requiring certified testing
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 47-4011, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Client consultation and walkthrough communication skills

roles.insight: Home inspection carries above-average trade exposure because AI image analysis and automated report generation are real, shipped capabilities gaining adoption, while the physical field judgment behind a defect call still needs a person on site.

roles.title: Machinist

rationales.taskAutomation: CAM software and AI-assisted toolpath generation automate a meaningful share of programming, but precision setup, tolerance troubleshooting and quality judgment remain human.
rationales.toolMaturity: CAM and AI toolpath-optimization software is credible and improving fast, short of fully mature autonomous machining.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is concentrated in larger manufacturing shops running modern CAM software; small job shops still program manually.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can generate and optimize a toolpath from a CAD file, not diagnose a tolerance drift on the shop floor.

tasksGrowing: Precision setup and first-article inspection judgment
Troubleshooting tolerance drift and tool wear on the floor
Cross-training into multi-axis and additive manufacturing
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 51-4041, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Machinists face moderate, rising exposure as CAM and AI toolpath tools take over programming, but precision setup and tolerance troubleshooting on the actual floor still require a trained eye AI inspection cameras don't fully replace yet.

roles.title: Auto Mechanic

rationales.taskAutomation: OBD-II diagnostics and AI-assisted repair-guide lookup automate a real share of diagnosis, but hands-on teardown, part replacement and road-testing stay manual.
rationales.toolMaturity: Dealer and franchise diagnostic platforms with AI fault-pattern matching are mature and standard equipment in most shops.
rationales.adoption: Diagnostic AI is mainstream in dealership and chain service centers; independent shops have adopted more unevenly.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can read codes, order parts and schedule the bay, but cannot pull an engine or verify a repair with a road test.

tasksAtRisk: OBD-II fault-code interpretation and repair-guide lookup
tasksGrowing: EV and hybrid drivetrain diagnosis and high-voltage safety work
Complex intermittent-fault troubleshooting AI code readers miss
Customer trust-building around AI-flagged repair recommendations
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 49-3023, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Auto mechanics carry more exposure than most trades because AI-assisted diagnostic platforms are mature, mainstream in dealership service bays, and genuinely automate fault-code interpretation. The mixed trend reflects two forces pulling apart: EV and ADAS complexity is creating higher-skill demand even as routine ICE maintenance keeps shrinking with vehicle reliability and diagnostic automation.

roles.title: Appliance Repair Technician

rationales.taskAutomation: Fault-code lookup and diagnostic-app guidance automate a real share of triage on smart appliances, but disassembly and part replacement stay manual.
rationales.toolMaturity: Manufacturer diagnostic apps and AI-assisted troubleshooting guides for smart appliances are mature and widely deployed.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is common among larger appliance-service companies using manufacturer diagnostic platforms; independent techs vary.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can interpret a smart appliance's fault code and order the part, not open the unit and make the repair.

tasksGrowing: Smart-appliance and IoT-connected system troubleshooting
Diagnosing failures diagnostic apps misclassify
Customer consultation on repair-vs-replace decisions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 49-9031, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Smart-appliance and IoT diagnostic platform fluency

roles.insight: Appliance repair shows moderate exposure as manufacturer diagnostic apps mature, but the trend is mixed: smart appliances create more diagnostic complexity even as consumers increasingly replace rather than repair low-cost units.

roles.title: Solar Installer

rationales.taskAutomation: System design, permitting paperwork and site-assessment modeling are increasingly AI-automated, but panel mounting, electrical tie-in and roof-safety work remain manual.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted solar design and permitting platforms (automated shade analysis, permit-package generation) are credible, shipped products used across the industry.
rationales.adoption: Most solar installation companies now use automated design software for proposals and permitting; it's close to standard practice in the residential segment.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can generate a system design, permit package and proposal from a satellite image; it cannot climb the roof and land the conduit.

tasksAtRisk: System design and shade-analysis modeling from site imagery
Permit-package generation and utility interconnection paperwork
Proposal and financing-option generation for customers
tasksGrowing: Rooftop mounting, wiring and electrical tie-in work
Battery-storage and microgrid system commissioning
Troubleshooting non-standard roof and structural conditions AI design tools flag incorrectly
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 47-2231, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI solar-design and permitting software fluency
Electrification and energy-consulting skills for customers

roles.insight: Solar installation shows one of the fastest tool-maturity curves in the trades: AI-driven design and permitting software is now close to standard practice, automating the proposal-to-permit pipeline. The physical install, especially battery and microgrid commissioning as storage attaches to more systems, is where the job's growth and its floor against automation both sit.

roles.title: Diesel Mechanic

rationales.taskAutomation: Telematics-based fault codes and fleet-diagnostic software automate a real share of triage, but teardown, part replacement and DOT-compliance inspection stay manual.
rationales.toolMaturity: Fleet telematics and AI-assisted diagnostic platforms are mature and widely deployed across trucking and bus fleets.
rationales.adoption: Large fleet operators have adopted predictive-maintenance and diagnostic AI broadly; owner-operator shops lag.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can flag a fault from telematics data and schedule a bay, not replace a turbo or pass a DOT inspection.

tasksAtRisk: Fault-code triage from fleet telematics data
DOT inspection paperwork and compliance logging
tasksGrowing: Complex engine and drivetrain teardown and repair
Electric and hybrid truck drivetrain servicing
Diagnosing intermittent faults telematics data misses
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 49-3031, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Fleet telematics and predictive-maintenance platform fluency

roles.insight: Diesel mechanics see moderate exposure because fleet telematics and AI-assisted diagnostics are mature and mainstream among large carriers, automating first-level fault triage, while the physical repair work and DOT-compliance judgment stay firmly manual.

roles.title: HVAC Technician

rationales.taskAutomation: Diagnostic app guidance and smart-thermostat data pull real automatable share into the job, but refrigerant handling, physical repair and code-driven installation remain manual.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted diagnostic tools tied to smart thermostats and connected HVAC systems are shipping and improving fast across major manufacturers.
rationales.adoption: Larger HVAC service franchises now route dispatch and first-level diagnosis through AI tools; independent shops lag.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can triage a smart-thermostat fault code and schedule the visit; it cannot recover refrigerant, braze a line or commission a system.

tasksAtRisk: First-level fault-code triage from connected thermostats
Service scheduling and technician dispatch routing
tasksGrowing: Heat-pump and electrification system commissioning
Refrigerant handling and EPA-compliant repair
Diagnosing multi-system faults AI triage tools misclassify
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 49-9021, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: EPA refrigerant handling and compliance expertise

roles.insight: HVAC sits slightly above other trades because connected thermostats and AI-assisted diagnostics genuinely automate first-level fault triage today, and adoption is real at scale among larger service franchises. The heat-pump and electrification wave is the growth lane: commissioning new all-electric systems isn't something diagnostic software can do end-to-end.

roles.title: Landscaper

rationales.taskAutomation: Planting, mowing and hardscape installation are physical work; route scheduling and basic design proposals are the automatable share.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted landscape design and route-optimization apps are credible and gaining traction among larger commercial landscaping companies.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is growing among mid-size and larger commercial landscaping firms for scheduling and design proposals; small residential crews mostly are not.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can optimize a mowing route and generate a planting-plan proposal, not plant, prune or grade a yard.

tasksGrowing: Hardscape and drainage installation requiring site judgment
Plant health diagnosis and pest management
Robotic-mower fleet oversight
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 37-3011, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI landscape-design and route-optimization software fluency

roles.insight: Landscaping's risk is modest and concentrated in scheduling and design proposals, where AI route-optimization and rendering tools are gaining real traction, while planting and hardscape installation stay physical work.

roles.title: Locksmith

rationales.taskAutomation: Mechanical lockout and key-cutting work is manual and physical; access-control system programming is the growing automatable/digital slice.
rationales.toolMaturity: Smart-lock and access-control management software is credible and improving, shipped by major security vendors.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is growing among commercial locksmiths managing electronic access-control systems; residential lockout work remains manual.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can provision and audit access-control credentials remotely; it cannot pick a lock or rekey a physical cylinder on site.

tasksAtRisk: Access-control credential provisioning and audit logs
Scheduling and dispatch of service calls
tasksGrowing: Smart-lock and electronic access-control system installation
Commercial security-system integration consulting
Complex mechanical lockout and safe work
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 49-9094, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Locksmithing's risk is modest and shifting: mechanical lockout work stays entirely manual, while growth in smart-lock and access-control system installation is reshaping the job's digital half.

roles.title: Wind Turbine Technician

rationales.taskAutomation: Climbing, mechanical repair and blade inspection at height are manual, safety-critical work; remote condition-monitoring is the automatable slice.
rationales.toolMaturity: SCADA-based remote condition-monitoring and predictive-maintenance software is mature and standard across utility-scale wind operators.
rationales.adoption: Nearly universal among utility-scale wind operators, who rely on remote monitoring to schedule maintenance visits.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can flag a predicted gearbox or blade fault from sensor telemetry, not climb the tower and make the repair.

tasksAtRisk: Maintenance scheduling based on predictive-analytics output
tasksGrowing: Height-safety mechanical repair and component replacement
Blade inspection and drone-assisted damage assessment
Cross-training on newer, larger turbine platforms
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 49-9081, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Advanced turbine platform and gearbox certification

roles.insight: Wind turbine technician remains one of the fastest-growing trades: remote condition-monitoring is mature and near-universal among operators, but the physical climb-and-repair work keeps risk low even as demand rises with fleet growth.

roles.title: Electrician

rationales.taskAutomation: Wiring, panel work, troubleshooting and code-driven installation remain physical, licensed acts; only estimating, permit paperwork and scheduling are AI-automatable today.
rationales.toolMaturity: Estimating and takeoff software and AI-assisted code-lookup tools are shipping, but nothing automates the trade itself.
rationales.adoption: Early-adopter electrical contractors use AI for quoting and dispatch; most small shops still run on paper tickets and phone calls.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can draft quotes, schedule callbacks and file permit paperwork, but cannot pull wire, torque a lug or pass an inspection.

tasksAtRisk: Permit application paperwork and code-reference lookup
Scheduling and dispatch of service calls
Invoice generation and customer follow-up emails
tasksGrowing: Smart-panel and EV-charger integration diagnostics
Reviewing AI-flagged code-compliance issues before sign-off
Customer consultation on electrification and solar-ready wiring
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 47-2111, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: EV charger and smart-panel installation certification
AI-assisted estimating and takeoff software fluency

roles.insight: Electricians sit near the floor of AI displacement risk because the licensed physical work of pulling wire and passing inspection has no automatable substitute, but the back office is changing fast: AI-assisted estimating and permit-paperwork tools are already trimming hours from bidding. The growth lane runs through electrification work AI can't yet spec: EV chargers, smart panels and solar-ready service upgrades.

roles.title: Heavy Equipment Operator

rationales.taskAutomation: Operating excavators, graders and loaders requires real-time site judgment; only fleet-tracking and basic telemetry logging are automatable today.
rationales.toolMaturity: Semi-autonomous grading and GPS machine-control systems are credible and improving, but full autonomy is not yet production-standard on most sites.
rationales.adoption: GPS machine-control adoption is real among larger earthwork and grading contractors; most smaller operations remain manually operated.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can log machine hours and flag maintenance from telemetry; it cannot grade a pad to spec on an active site.

tasksAtRisk: Fuel and utilization tracking across a fleet
tasksGrowing: Operating GPS machine-control and semi-autonomous grading systems
Site judgment on unstable or unmapped ground conditions
Cross-training across multiple equipment classes
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 47-2073, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: GPS machine-control and semi-autonomous grading certification
Fleet telemetry and maintenance-tracking software literacy

roles.insight: Heavy-equipment operation trends toward rising demand as infrastructure spending grows, and semi-autonomous GPS grading systems are shifting the job toward oversight of assisted equipment rather than replacing operators outright.

roles.title: Carpenter

rationales.taskAutomation: Framing, finish work and on-site problem-solving are manual craft; only cut-list generation and material ordering are reliably automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: CAD-driven cut-list and takeoff software is credible and improving, but nothing automates the physical build.
rationales.adoption: Larger framing crews and production builders increasingly use digital takeoff tools; custom and finish carpenters mostly do not.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can generate cut lists and order lumber from a plan set, not frame a wall or fit trim to an out-of-square corner.

tasksGrowing: Custom finish work and on-site design problem-solving
Coordinating trade sequencing on multi-crew jobs
Reading and adapting to as-built site conditions AI plans miss
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 47-2031, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Digital takeoff and cut-list software fluency
Finish carpentry and custom fabrication specialization

roles.insight: Carpentry's risk is capped by the physical craft: framing, trim and site-adapted problem-solving resist automation, while digital cut-list and takeoff tools quietly speed up the estimating side of the job. Carpenters who can run that software and lead multi-trade sequencing are the best-positioned in the trade.

roles.title: Elevator Technician

rationales.taskAutomation: Installation, code-compliant repair and safety-testing of elevator systems are manual, licensed acts; predictive-maintenance alerts are the automatable slice.
rationales.toolMaturity: IoT-based predictive-maintenance platforms from major elevator manufacturers are credible and shipping, monitoring equipment remotely.
rationales.adoption: Major elevator service companies have adopted remote-monitoring and predictive-maintenance platforms at real scale.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can flag a predicted component failure from sensor data and schedule the technician visit, not perform the repair.

tasksAtRisk: Predictive-maintenance alert triage from equipment sensors
tasksGrowing: Modernization and code-upgrade installation on aging building stock
Diagnosing faults predictive systems misclassify
Safety-critical repair requiring certified judgment
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 47-4021, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Elevator technicians see rising demand as aging buildings require modernization, and remote predictive-maintenance sensors now handle real monitoring work, but code-compliant repair itself remains licensed, hands-on work.

roles.title: Painter

rationales.taskAutomation: Surface prep and application are manual craft; only estimating from photos/measurements and scheduling are automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted paint-estimating apps (photo-to-square-footage, color visualization) are credible and improving.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is growing among larger residential and commercial painting companies for bidding; small crews remain manual.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can generate a quote from a photo and floor plan, not prep a wall or cut a clean line.

tasksAtRisk: Estimate generation from photos and floor plans
tasksGrowing: Surface prep and repair judgment on damaged substrates
Specialty finishes and faux/decorative work
Customer color consultation
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 47-2141, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Painting's exposure is modest and concentrated in bidding: AI photo-to-estimate tools are gaining real traction with larger companies, while surface prep and application stay a hands-on craft.

roles.title: Construction Laborer

rationales.taskAutomation: Site cleanup, material staging and basic tool support are physical tasks that resist automation; only material-tracking logs are reliably AI-automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: No purpose-built tooling targets the laborer role directly; generic scheduling and logistics apps apply at the margins.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is limited to project-level logistics software used by general contractors, not the laborer's own tasks.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can track material deliveries against a schedule; it cannot move, stage or install materials on site.

tasksAtRisk: Material delivery tracking and inventory logging
Basic scheduling coordination with crew leads
tasksGrowing: Cross-training into skilled-trade apprenticeships
Operating semi-automated site equipment safely
Site safety compliance monitoring
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 47-2061, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Apprenticeship pathway into a licensed trade
Site logistics and material-tracking software literacy

roles.insight: Construction laborer is near the floor of this index: the job is site presence and physical handling, and the only digital slice, delivery and inventory tracking, is a small share of the role.

roles.title: Crane Operator

rationales.taskAutomation: Precision rigging and load control on active sites are safety-critical manual skills; scheduling and load-plan documentation are the automatable slice.
rationales.toolMaturity: Load-monitoring and anti-collision sensor systems are mature and increasingly standard on modern cranes, but full autonomous operation is not deployed.
rationales.adoption: Large commercial and high-rise contractors have adopted sensor-based load-monitoring systems broadly; smaller operations lag.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can generate a lift plan and log load data; it cannot operate the crane or make a live judgment call on wind and load sway.

tasksAtRisk: Equipment scheduling across a job site
tasksGrowing: Live load and rigging judgment under changing site conditions
Certified operation of larger and more complex crane classes
Coordinating multi-crane lifts on active sites
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 53-7021, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Advanced crane certification (NCCCO, larger load classes)
Load-monitoring and anti-collision sensor system fluency

roles.insight: Crane operation stays low-risk: sensor-based load monitoring is mature and mainstream on large sites, but the safety-critical judgment of an actual lift has no autonomous substitute in production use.

roles.title: Drywall Installer

rationales.taskAutomation: Hanging, taping and finishing are manual craft; only material takeoff and scheduling are reliably automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: Digital takeoff software for drywall quantities is credible but not role-specific beyond generic construction estimating tools.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is limited to larger commercial drywall contractors using digital takeoff for bidding.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can calculate sheet counts and mud quantities from a floor plan, not hang or finish a wall.

tasksAtRisk: Material takeoff (sheet count, mud, tape quantities)
tasksGrowing: Level-5 finish and specialty texture work requiring craft judgment
Fire-rated and sound-rated assembly compliance
Adapting to non-standard framing conditions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 47-2081, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Level-5 finish and specialty texture specialization

roles.insight: Drywall installation carries low risk: hanging, taping and finishing remain manual craft, with automation limited to the material takeoff and bidding side of larger commercial jobs.

roles.title: Flooring Installer

rationales.taskAutomation: Installing hardwood, tile and resilient flooring is manual craft; material takeoff and scheduling are the automatable slice.
rationales.toolMaturity: Digital takeoff and room-measurement apps are credible and improving, similar to other trade-estimating tools.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is growing among larger flooring retailers and installation companies for measurement and quoting.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can calculate square footage and material waste factors from a floor plan, not lay a plank or set a tile to level.

tasksGrowing: Subfloor repair and moisture-remediation judgment
Custom pattern and specialty flooring installation
Adapting to out-of-level or damaged subfloor conditions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 47-2042, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Digital measurement and takeoff software fluency

roles.insight: Flooring installation carries low risk: the craft itself resists automation, with AI tools reaching only the room-measurement and quoting step ahead of the job.

roles.title: Insulation Worker

rationales.taskAutomation: Installing batt, spray-foam and blown insulation is manual physical work; only material-quantity estimating is reliably automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generic energy-modeling software estimates insulation needs, but no AI tool automates the installation itself.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is limited to energy-auditing software used by larger insulation and weatherization contractors.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can calculate R-value requirements and material quantities from a building model; it cannot spray foam into a wall cavity.

tasksGrowing: Spray-foam and blown-insulation installation judgment
Air-sealing and building-envelope diagnostics
Energy-retrofit consulting for building owners
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 47-2131, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Insulation work stays low-risk on the install side while energy-code tightening and retrofit incentives drive rising demand, with AI energy-modeling tools automating only the estimating step.

roles.title: Roofer

rationales.taskAutomation: Tear-off, deck repair and shingle/membrane installation are physical work at height with no automatable substitute; drone-based inspection reports are the main automatable slice.
rationales.toolMaturity: Drone and AI-assisted roof-inspection imaging tools are credible and gaining traction for estimating, but not for the install itself.
rationales.adoption: A minority of larger roofing companies use drone/AI inspection tools for estimating; most crews still measure and inspect manually.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can process a drone image into a measurement report; it cannot tear off a roof or seal a membrane.

tasksAtRisk: Drone-based roof measurement and damage assessment
Estimate and proposal generation from inspection imagery
Material ordering based on measured square footage
tasksGrowing: Tear-off, decking repair and installation at height
Reading and adapting to structural surprises found mid-tear-off
Storm-damage assessment requiring in-person judgment
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 47-2181, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Drone/AI roof-inspection and measurement software fluency

roles.insight: Roofing stays low-risk because the physical work at height has no substitute, though drone-based inspection and AI measurement tools are starting to automate the estimating step for larger companies.

roles.title: Welder

rationales.taskAutomation: Field and structural welding demands manual dexterity and real-time visual judgment that current robotic and AI systems can't replicate outside fixed factory cells.
rationales.toolMaturity: Robotic welding is mature in high-volume manufacturing, but that tooling doesn't reach field and structural welders working one-off joints on site.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is concentrated in automotive and heavy-manufacturing plants; construction and field welding remain almost entirely manual.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can track weld logs, certifications and inspection paperwork, but cannot run a bead or pass a destructive weld test.

tasksAtRisk: Weld procedure documentation and certification tracking
tasksGrowing: Structural and pressure-vessel welding requiring certified judgment
Reading and adapting to non-standard field joints and repairs
Cross-training on new alloys and welding processes as materials evolve
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 51-4121, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Field welding stays low-risk because robotic welding, though mature, is confined to fixed factory cells and hasn't reached one-off structural and site work. The exposure that exists is administrative: certification tracking and inspection logs, not the weld itself.

roles.title: Glazier

rationales.taskAutomation: Cutting, fitting and installing glass and curtain-wall systems is manual, precision physical work with no automatable substitute at job-site scale.
rationales.toolMaturity: No purpose-built AI tooling targets glazing installation; generic estimating software applies only at the margins.
rationales.adoption: Adoption of any AI tooling in glazing is rare, limited to generic takeoff software used by larger commercial glazing contractors.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can generate a glass-quantity takeoff from architectural drawings; it cannot set a curtain-wall panel plumb and square.

tasksGrowing: Curtain-wall and structural-glazing installation judgment
Safety-glass and code-compliance expertise
Cross-training into commercial high-rise glazing
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 47-2121, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Glazing is a low-risk trade: precision glass installation has no automated substitute, and AI tooling in the field is essentially absent beyond generic estimating software.

roles.title: Mason

rationales.taskAutomation: Bricklaying and block-setting are manual craft requiring physical precision that has no automated substitute at typical job-site scale.
rationales.toolMaturity: Robotic bricklaying exists only in limited pilot deployments, not shipped production tools.
rationales.adoption: Adoption of any AI or robotic tooling in masonry is rare, concentrated in a handful of large commercial contractors piloting robotic layers.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can generate a material takeoff from a wall plan; it cannot lay a course of brick to line and level.

tasksGrowing: Custom and historic masonry restoration requiring craft judgment
Structural masonry requiring code-compliance expertise
Cross-training toward larger commercial masonry projects
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 47-2021, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Digital takeoff and estimating software literacy

roles.insight: Masonry is among the lowest-risk trades: robotic bricklaying remains a pilot-stage novelty, and the craft's precision work stays entirely manual.

roles.title: Ironworker

rationales.taskAutomation: Erecting and connecting structural steel at height is manual, safety-critical physical work with no automated substitute in field conditions.
rationales.toolMaturity: No purpose-built AI tooling targets structural ironwork; robotic steel erection remains research-stage, not deployed on active job sites.
rationales.adoption: Adoption of any AI or robotic tooling in ironwork is essentially nonexistent on active commercial job sites.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can track steel-delivery schedules against an erection plan; it cannot connect a beam at height.

tasksGrowing: Structural connection and high-rise erection judgment
Rigging and crane-coordination expertise
Cross-training into rebar and reinforcing work
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 47-2221, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Advanced rigging and high-rise erection certification

roles.insight: Ironwork sits at the floor of this index: structural steel erection at height is inherently manual and safety-critical, and demand is rising with the data-center and infrastructure construction boom.

roles.title: Plumber

rationales.taskAutomation: Pipe fitting, leak diagnosis in tight physical spaces, and code-compliant installation are almost entirely manual, licensed acts AI cannot perform end-to-end.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generic scheduling and invoicing apps exist, but no shipped tool does the trade's physical diagnostic or installation work.
rationales.adoption: Most plumbing shops remain phone-and-paper operations; AI use is confined to a minority of larger service franchises' back offices.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can route service calls and draft invoices, a sliver of the job, while diagnosis and repair stay entirely human.

tasksGrowing: Complex leak and pipe-failure diagnosis in older buildings
Backflow and code-compliance judgment calls
Client consultation on repipe vs. repair decisions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 47-2152, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI-assisted scheduling and dispatch software fluency

roles.insight: Plumbing is one of the lowest-risk trades in the index: licensed, hands-in-the-wall work has no automatable substitute, and even the back office runs mostly on phone calls rather than software. What is changing is dispatch and invoicing, where AI scheduling tools are starting to cut administrative overhead for larger service franchises.

roles.title: Shipping Clerk

rationales.taskAutomation: Manifest generation, label printing, and inventory reconciliation are digital-record work that makes up half or more of this role's core output.
rationales.toolMaturity: Warehouse and inventory management AI tools are mature and standard across mid-size and large operations.
rationales.adoption: Common among large and mid-market shippers and spreading further as WMS platforms become standard.
rationales.agenticExposure: Order processing, manifest generation, and inventory reconciliation form a digital pipeline an agent can largely traverse end-to-end.

tasksGrowing: Exception handling for damaged, missing, or misrouted shipments
Vendor and carrier coordination on discrepancies
Overseeing and correcting automated inventory systems
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-5071, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Vendor/carrier coordination for damaged or lost freight

roles.insight: This is one of the more exposed roles in the category: manifest generation, labeling, and inventory reconciliation are exactly the kind of digital record-processing work agentic tools already own end to end. Clerks who move toward exception handling and vendor coordination are furthest from the automated core.

roles.title: Freight Broker

rationales.taskAutomation: Quoting, load posting, carrier matching, and booking are largely digital and already automatable; carrier relationship management and rate negotiation on hard freight still need a person.
rationales.toolMaturity: Digital freight marketplaces and AI pricing/matching tools (Uber Freight, DAT, Convoy-style platforms) are mature, shipped products.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted brokerage is common at large 3PLs and spreading into smaller brokerages as digital marketplaces become standard.
rationales.agenticExposure: Freight brokering is substantially a pipeline of digital decisions, quoting, matching, booking, tracking, that an autonomous agent can traverse end-to-end for standard loads.

tasksGrowing: Relationship-based carrier negotiation for hard-to-cover freight
Exception handling for disrupted or specialized shipments
Overseeing and correcting AI-matched loads
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-5011, p25-p75 rounded, high end reflects commission-heavy brokerage pay

defenseSkills.skill: Digital freight marketplace and AI pricing-tool fluency

roles.insight: Freight brokerage is one of the most exposed roles in this category because it's already a digital-marketplace business: standard load matching, quoting, and booking are agent-ownable today. Brokers who survive are shifting toward the relationship-heavy, hard-to-cover freight that automated marketplaces still struggle to fill.

roles.title: Dispatcher

rationales.taskAutomation: Load-driver matching, scheduling, and routing are largely digital decisions AI can already make; live exception handling and driver relationships still need a person for the harder calls.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-based load-matching and dispatch platforms (transportation management systems, digital freight marketplaces) are mature and commercially embedded.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted dispatch is common at large fleets and freight brokerages and spreading into mid-market operations.
rationales.agenticExposure: Dispatch is fundamentally a pipeline of digital decisions and handoffs, matching, scheduling, tracking, rerouting, that autonomous agents can traverse end-to-end.

tasksAtRisk: Standard status updates to customers and drivers
Basic rebooking when a route falls through
tasksGrowing: Complex multi-party exception resolution (breakdowns, weather, disputes)
Driver relationship management and retention
Overseeing and correcting AI dispatch-system decisions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-5032, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI dispatch and TMS platform oversight

roles.insight: Dispatch is one of the more exposed roles in this category precisely because it's a digital coordination job, not a physical one: routine matching, scheduling, and status updates are already agent-handled at many large fleets. The dispatchers who remain valuable are the ones running exception queues AI escalates to them, not the ones doing standard matching by hand.

roles.title: Customs Broker

rationales.taskAutomation: Tariff classification, entry filing, and documentation are largely rules-based digital work AI can do well, but regulatory judgment calls, audits, and liability for misclassification still require a licensed person.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted HTS classification and trade-compliance tools are credible products improving quickly, though full automated filing without human review isn't standard yet.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is growing among mid-size and large brokerages and importers, but is not yet universal, especially among smaller import operations.
rationales.agenticExposure: Entry filing, documentation, and compliance checks form a digital pipeline an agent can largely traverse, subject to human sign-off on liability.

tasksGrowing: Regulatory judgment on ambiguous or novel classifications
Audit defense and penalty mitigation
Advising importers on evolving trade policy
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1041 (Compliance Officers, closest detailed occupation), p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI-assisted trade-compliance and HTS classification tools
Regulatory audit defense and penalty mitigation

roles.insight: Classification and filing are exactly the kind of rules-based digital work AI already handles well, which is pushing routine entry work toward automation. The licensed judgment and liability side of the job, defending an audit, resolving an ambiguous classification, is what keeps this role from moving further, faster.

roles.title: Logistics Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Reporting, scheduling, and route/network optimization are largely digital, but vendor relationships, people management, and exception judgment still gate most of the role's value.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-enabled transportation and warehouse management systems are mature and widely embedded in the standard logistics toolchain.
rationales.adoption: Common at large logistics and retail employers, spreading into mid-market operations.
rationales.agenticExposure: Routing optimization, reporting, and exception triage are meaningfully agent-ownable slices, but the managerial and vendor-facing core is not.

tasksAtRisk: Standard KPI dashboards and performance tracking
tasksGrowing: Vendor and carrier contract negotiation
Team management and cross-functional coordination
Strategic network redesign and crisis response
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-3071, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI has taken over much of the reporting and route-optimization work that used to fill a logistics manager's week, but the vendor negotiation, team leadership, and judgment calls during disruptions remain squarely human, keeping overall risk moderate rather than high.

roles.title: Transportation Planner

rationales.taskAutomation: Traffic modeling, data analysis, and draft reporting are substantially digital, but stakeholder engagement, political judgment, and policy trade-offs gate most of the role's value.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted traffic simulation and modeling tools are credible and improving quickly, though not yet a full planning-workflow replacement.
rationales.adoption: Early-to-common among larger metro planning agencies and consultancies; smaller municipal planning offices lag.
rationales.agenticExposure: Data analysis and scenario modeling are agent-ownable slices; public engagement and policy judgment are not.

tasksAtRisk: GIS mapping and spatial data processing
tasksGrowing: Public stakeholder engagement and community input synthesis
Policy trade-off analysis and political navigation
Cross-agency coordination on major infrastructure decisions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 19-3051, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI-assisted traffic simulation and modeling tools

roles.insight: AI modeling tools speed up the data-analysis side of transportation planning, but the public engagement and political trade-off work, deciding whose neighborhood gets the new transit line, remains a human judgment call agents don't reach.

roles.title: Package Handler

rationales.taskAutomation: Automated sortation and scanning handle a large share of hub throughput, but loading, unloading, and irregular-package handling still need people at most facilities.
rationales.toolMaturity: Automated sortation systems are mature and widely deployed at major parcel hubs (UPS, FedEx, Amazon).
rationales.adoption: Common at large national parcel hubs; adoption is lower at smaller regional facilities that still handle much manually.
rationales.agenticExposure: Sorting-decision and routing logic is agent-owned already; physical loading/unloading is not.

tasksGrowing: Loading/unloading trailers and containers
Handling oversized, fragile, or misrouted packages
Operating and maintaining sortation equipment
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 53-7064, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Major parcel hubs already run mature automated sortation, which has shifted headcount toward loading, unloading, and exception handling rather than eliminating it outright; smaller regional facilities lag well behind.

roles.title: Forklift Operator

rationales.taskAutomation: Autonomous forklifts and AGVs handle repetitive pallet moves in modern facilities, but irregular loads, tight spaces, and mixed human-equipment traffic still need a licensed operator in most warehouses.
rationales.toolMaturity: Autonomous forklift/AMR products (Seegrid, Fetch and similar) are mature and commercially deployed in large distribution centers.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is real but concentrated in large, newer, automation-forward facilities; most of the broader warehouse base still runs manually operated forklifts.
rationales.agenticExposure: Load-routing and task-queue assignment are agent-ownable; direct physical vehicle operation in unstructured floor conditions is not.

tasksAtRisk: Repetitive pallet moves on fixed routes
Standard put-away and retrieval in mapped racking
tasksGrowing: Irregular, oversized, or damaged-load handling
Operating in mixed human/AGV traffic zones safely
Equipment maintenance and troubleshooting
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 53-7051, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AGV/AMR fleet coexistence and safety operation

roles.insight: Autonomous forklifts are a mature product, but they are best at repetitive, mapped-route moves; the irregular loads and mixed human/robot floors of most warehouses still need a licensed human operator, so adoption is real but concentrated in a minority of large, newer facilities.

roles.title: Warehouse Worker

rationales.taskAutomation: Robotic picking, sorting, and shelf-moving are real in modern fulfillment centers, but most physical pick-pack-load work across the broader warehouse base is still done by hand.
rationales.toolMaturity: Autonomous mobile robots and pick-assist systems (Amazon robotics, Locus, 6 River) are mature, widely-shipped products in large modern facilities.
rationales.adoption: Robotics adoption is common among large e-commerce and 3PL operators and spreading, though most of the smaller warehouse base still runs largely manual operations.
rationales.agenticExposure: Inventory tracking, slotting, and order-routing decisions are agent-ownable; the physical handling of goods is not.

tasksGrowing: Operating and troubleshooting robotic pick/pack systems
Exception handling for damaged, mislabeled, or irregular items
Quality control and safety oversight on automated floors
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 53-7062, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Quality control and safety inspection on automated lines

roles.insight: Large e-commerce and 3PL fulfillment centers already run substantial robotics, but that has mostly shifted the job toward robot-adjacent work (loading, exception handling, machine oversight) rather than eliminating headcount outright. The smaller, non-automated warehouse base, still the majority of facilities, faces materially less near-term pressure.

roles.title: Rideshare Driver

rationales.taskAutomation: Waymo and peers run driverless rides commercially in several geofenced cities, but reliable operation across arbitrary routes, weather, and unmapped areas is still not there.
rationales.toolMaturity: Robotaxi service is a mature, paying-customer product in its operating zones (Waymo in SF, Phoenix, LA, Austin), well past the demo stage where it runs.
rationales.adoption: Robotaxis are a small fraction of total rideshare trips nationally; almost all rideshare miles are still driven by humans, including in robotaxi cities.
rationales.agenticExposure: Trip matching, pricing, and routing are already agent-run by the platforms; the human driving task itself is the part agents can't yet reach at scale.

tasksGrowing: Rides outside geofenced/mapped robotaxi zones
Rider assistance requiring judgment (accessibility, safety, disputes)
Multi-passenger, luggage, or event-surge trips robotaxis don't yet serve
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 53-3054, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Operating in non-geofenced, unmapped service areas
Platform and gig-app fluency across multiple services

roles.insight: Robotaxis are commercially live today, not a future prospect, but confined to specific mapped cities and conditions. Drivers in those metro robotaxi zones face real and growing displacement over the next few years; drivers everywhere else face materially less pressure until geofences expand, which has historically happened city by city, slowly.

roles.title: Delivery Driver

rationales.taskAutomation: Sidewalk robots and delivery drones handle a growing slice of last-mile drops in pilot markets, but most stops still require a human to navigate traffic, buildings, and handoffs.
rationales.toolMaturity: Nuro, Amazon Scout-successors, and drone delivery (Zipline, Wing) are shipped products, not demos, though coverage is limited to specific metro pilot zones.
rationales.adoption: Only a small number of large retailers and carriers run robotic/drone last-mile pilots; the vast majority of delivery volume still moves by human driver.
rationales.agenticExposure: Route sequencing, proof-of-delivery, and dispatch handoffs are agent-ownable digital workflows layered on top of a still-human physical drop.

tasksGrowing: Complex multi-unit and access-restricted deliveries
Customer service recovery for failed or disputed drops
Supervising robot/drone delivery fleets in pilot zones
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 53-3033, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Customer-facing problem resolution for failed deliveries

roles.insight: Drone and sidewalk-robot delivery are commercially operating today, but only in select metro pilot zones covering a small fraction of last-mile volume nationally. The realistic medium-term shift is fewer stops per driver as robots absorb easy, short-hop drops, with humans increasingly routed to the access-restricted and exception-heavy deliveries robots can't yet handle.

roles.title: Courier

rationales.taskAutomation: Same-day and specialty courier runs require physical pickup, handling, and delivery across arbitrary addresses; sidewalk-robot and drone pilots cover only a narrow slice of this today.
rationales.toolMaturity: Sidewalk delivery robots and drones are shipped products in specific metro pilots, not a general courier replacement.
rationales.adoption: A handful of markets run robot/drone courier pilots; nearly all courier volume nationally still moves by human driver, biker, or walker.
rationales.agenticExposure: Route sequencing and delivery-status tracking are agent-ownable; the physical pickup-and-handoff core is not.

tasksAtRisk: Standard document/parcel drop-off in accessible locations
tasksGrowing: Time-sensitive medical/legal document handling
Complex or access-restricted delivery locations
Customer service recovery for failed handoffs
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-5021, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Robot and drone courier pilots exist in a handful of metro markets but cover a narrow slice of courier volume; the specialized, time-sensitive document runs (medical, legal) that make up much of this role's value are not close to automatable.

roles.title: Truck Driver

rationales.taskAutomation: Highway driving in structured lanes is the closest thing to automatable, but docking, weather, detours, and city streets still require a human at the wheel almost everywhere in the country.
rationales.toolMaturity: Driverless freight companies (Aurora, Kodiak, Waabi) run real revenue lanes on mapped interstate corridors, but the tech is geofenced and route-specific, not general-purpose.
rationales.adoption: Driverless freight lanes number in the dozens nationally out of millions of truck routes; almost every fleet still runs human drivers exclusively.
rationales.agenticExposure: ELD logging, routing, and load-matching paperwork are agent-ownable side tasks, but the physical driving core of the job is not.

tasksGrowing: Supervising and handing off to autonomous convoy systems on mapped lanes
Exception handling for weather, detours, and last-mile delivery
Specialized and oversize/hazmat hauling requiring certified judgment
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 53-3032, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Fleet telematics and AI dispatch-system fluency

roles.insight: Autonomous trucking is real but narrow: driverless freight runs on a handful of mapped interstate lanes today, not on the docks, city streets, and weather a driver actually navigates. The near-term threat is on the paperwork side (logs, routing, load matching), already largely automated, while the medium-term pressure on long-haul lanes will build as mapped corridors expand over the next several years.

roles.title: Postal Worker

rationales.taskAutomation: Mail sorting is heavily automated at the facility level, but the delivery route itself, navigating streets, mailboxes, and packages door to door, is still physical, human work.
rationales.toolMaturity: Automated sorting machines are mature and near-universal at processing facilities; route delivery automation remains experimental.
rationales.adoption: USPS and major carriers use automated sortation widely; carrier-route automation has no meaningful deployment.
rationales.agenticExposure: Route sequencing is agent-assistable; the physical delivery route is not.

tasksAtRisk: Mail and package sorting at processing facilities
tasksGrowing: Physical door-to-door delivery in varied conditions
Customer-facing service at retail counters
Handling special/certified mail and exceptions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-5052, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Facility-level sorting has been automated for years without eliminating the carrier role, because the door-to-door delivery route itself remains physical work automation hasn't reached; near-term risk stays low.

roles.title: Train Conductor

rationales.taskAutomation: Positive Train Control and some driverless freight/transit trials exist, but most conductor duties, safety checks, coupling, passenger/crew coordination, remain hands-on and human-gated.
rationales.toolMaturity: PTC and automated rail-signaling systems are mature; fully autonomous freight or passenger rail is still in limited trial, not a shipped general product.
rationales.adoption: Automation is common in signaling and braking systems but rare for replacing conductor duties themselves.
rationales.agenticExposure: Scheduling and manifest paperwork are agent-assistable; physical safety checks and crew coordination are not.

tasksAtRisk: Standard signal and speed compliance logging
tasksGrowing: Physical safety inspections and coupling operations
Emergency response and derailment/incident management
Crew and yard coordination during disruptions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 53-4031, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Rail safety inspection and incident response
Automated signaling and PTC system fluency
Yard and crew coordination during disruptions

roles.insight: Rail automation has focused on signaling and braking systems, not on replacing the conductor's physical safety and coordination duties; driverless freight rail trials exist but remain narrow pilots years from broad deployment.

roles.title: Aircraft Mechanic

rationales.taskAutomation: This is hands-on, licensed physical repair work in unstructured conditions; AI diagnostic tools assist but cannot perform the physical inspection and repair itself.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted predictive-maintenance and diagnostic tools are credible and improving fast, but they inform mechanics rather than replace physical work.
rationales.adoption: Predictive-maintenance software is spreading among larger carriers and MROs, still uneven across the smaller operator base.
rationales.agenticExposure: Parts ordering and maintenance-log documentation are agent-assistable; the licensed physical repair is not.

tasksGrowing: Complex diagnostic and structural repair work
Emergency/AOG (aircraft-on-ground) troubleshooting
Certifying airworthiness sign-off
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 49-3011, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Complex diagnostic and structural repair certification

roles.insight: AI predictive-maintenance tools are shipping and genuinely useful for flagging what needs attention, but the licensed, hands-on repair and airworthiness certification at the center of this role remain firmly human work.

roles.title: Bus Driver

rationales.taskAutomation: Autonomous transit shuttles run on a handful of fixed, low-speed routes, but mixed-traffic city and highway bus routes with passenger safety obligations remain human-driven.
rationales.toolMaturity: Autonomous shuttle products (May Mobility and similar) exist and are improving, but remain low-speed, fixed-route pilots rather than general bus replacements.
rationales.adoption: A small number of transit agencies run autonomous shuttle pilots; the overwhelming majority of transit and intercity bus service is entirely human-driven.
rationales.agenticExposure: Scheduling and route-timing optimization are agent-assistable; the driving and passenger-safety core is not.

tasksAtRisk: Standard fixed-route driving on low-speed campus/shuttle lines
tasksGrowing: Passenger safety response and de-escalation
Mixed-traffic and adverse-weather driving
Accessibility assistance for riders
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 53-3052, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Autonomous shuttle pilots are real but confined to fixed, low-speed routes; mixed-traffic transit and intercity bus driving, with the safety and liability obligations of carrying passengers, is not close to automatable at production reliability.

roles.title: Air Traffic Controller

rationales.taskAutomation: AI conflict-detection and decision-support tools assist controllers, but final separation and clearance authority is a licensed human responsibility with zero tolerance for error.
rationales.toolMaturity: Automation aids (NextGen tools, conflict alerts) are shipped and used, but full autonomous air-traffic management is not a deployed or certified product.
