rationales.adoption: FAA facilities use automation aids widely, but as decision support only; staffing has not been reduced by AI to date.
rationales.agenticExposure: The role's safety-critical, split-second liability keeps it outside where autonomous agents currently operate.

tasksAtRisk: Routine traffic-flow monitoring in low-density airspace
tasksGrowing: High-density and emergency separation management
Cross-sector coordination during disruptions
Overseeing AI decision-support tool outputs
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 53-2021, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI decision-support tool oversight and override judgment

roles.insight: Controllers already work alongside mature automation aids, but the FAA has not reduced staffing because final separation authority stays with a certified human; near-term risk is low, concentrated instead in tool-assisted efficiency gains rather than headcount reduction.

roles.title: Airline Pilot

rationales.taskAutomation: Autopilot and autoland already fly most of cruise and a share of approach, but takeoff, taxi, weather diversions, and emergencies remain a licensed human responsibility by regulation.
rationales.toolMaturity: Autopilot/autoland systems are decades-mature and near-universal on commercial aircraft; single-pilot or pilotless commercial passenger flight is not a certified or shipped product.
rationales.adoption: Every major carrier uses mature autopilot systems, but that has not reduced flight-deck crew counts; reduced-crew concepts remain in study, not deployment.
rationales.agenticExposure: The role is licensed, safety-critical, and physically present by regulatory mandate; agentic systems don't reach cockpit command authority today.

tasksGrowing: Takeoff, landing, and taxi in degraded or non-standard conditions
In-flight emergency and weather-diversion decision-making
Crew leadership and passenger-safety command authority
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 53-2011, p25-p75 rounded, high end reflects major-carrier captain pay

defenseSkills.skill: Crew resource management and safety leadership

roles.insight: Autopilot already handles most of the flying hours in a commercial flight, and that has been true for years without displacing pilots, because certification, insurance, and passenger trust require a licensed human in command. Reduced-crew and single-pilot concepts are in FAA-monitored study, not deployment; a real regulatory shift is possible over a decade-plus horizon but is not the near-term picture.

roles.title: Flight Attendant

rationales.taskAutomation: The role is dominated by in-person passenger service, physical safety demonstrations, and emergency response, none of which current AI can perform end-to-end.
rationales.toolMaturity: No shipped product automates cabin safety or service duties; AI is limited to back-office scheduling and booking tools that don't touch the in-cabin role.
rationales.adoption: Airlines use AI for crew scheduling and booking systems, not for any part of the physical in-flight role.
rationales.agenticExposure: In-person safety command and physical presence keep this role outside current agentic reach.

tasksAtRisk: Routine in-app passenger notifications and rebooking prompts
tasksGrowing: Emergency response and evacuation command
Passenger conflict de-escalation
Accessibility and special-needs passenger assistance
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 53-2031, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Emergency response and evacuation command certification

roles.insight: This is an in-person safety role first and a service role second; current AI has no path to performing either at the required reliability, so displacement risk stays low regardless of how much AI improves in adjacent booking and scheduling systems.

roles.title: Cashier

rationales.taskAutomation: Scanning, payment processing, and basic transaction handling are scripted, repetitive digital-physical work that self-checkout already performs end-to-end.
rationales.toolMaturity: Self-checkout kiosks and frictionless-checkout computer-vision systems are commodity technology deployed at massive scale.
rationales.adoption: Self-checkout is now the default option in most grocery, big-box, and drugstore chains, a mainstream expectation for the retail transaction function.
rationales.agenticExposure: Item scanning, payment, and loss-prevention flagging form a full agent-traversable transaction pipeline; only exception handling and customer assistance stay human.

tasksGrowing: Self-checkout exception handling and theft prevention
Customer assistance and complex return judgment calls
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 41-2011, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Self-checkout exception handling and loss-prevention judgment
Customer-service recovery on the retail floor
Cross-training into inventory or fulfillment tech roles

roles.insight: Self-checkout has already automated the core transaction, and most large retailers now treat it as the default lane, not the exception. What remains for cashiers is what the machines can't handle: age-verification disputes, theft prevention, and the returns a kiosk kicks back to a human.

roles.title: Travel Agent

rationales.taskAutomation: Flight and hotel research, price comparison, and basic itinerary building are exactly the tasks AI trip-planning tools now perform end-to-end for standard trips.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI trip-planning assistants and booking-platform AI features are mature and widely available to consumers directly.
rationales.adoption: Consumer self-service booking via AI tools is now the default path for most travelers; agents retain a smaller, more complex client base.
rationales.agenticExposure: Research, comparison, and itinerary assembly form a classic agent-ownable research-and-book workflow for standard trips.

tasksAtRisk: Standard itinerary building for common destinations
tasksGrowing: Complex multi-destination and group itinerary design
Handling disruptions, cancellations, and rebooking under pressure
High-touch advisory for luxury and corporate travel
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 41-3041, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI trip-planning tool integration into client service

roles.insight: AI trip planners already out-research most human agents on a standard beach vacation, comparing hundreds of fares and itineraries in seconds. The agents still working are the ones handling what breaks: a canceled flight mid-trip, a ten-stop corporate itinerary, or a client who wants judgment, not just options.

roles.title: Hotel Front Desk Agent

rationales.taskAutomation: Check-in, check-out, and basic reservation and FAQ handling are highly standardized information tasks that mobile check-in and kiosks already perform end-to-end.
rationales.toolMaturity: Mobile check-in, keyless entry, and AI chatbot concierge tools are mature and deployed across major hotel brands.
rationales.adoption: Mobile check-in and self-service kiosks are now mainstream at mid-size and large chains, with adoption still spreading to independents.
rationales.agenticExposure: Reservation lookup, room assignment, and check-in/out confirmation form a complete agent-traversable booking pipeline.

tasksAtRisk: Basic reservation changes and FAQ responses
tasksGrowing: Service recovery for guest complaints
Local recommendations tailored to individual guests
Handling groups, VIPs, and non-standard requests
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-4081, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Hotel PMS and AI check-in system administration
Cross-training toward guest-relations or revenue roles

roles.insight: Mobile check-in and keyless entry already handle the routine arrival for most guests. The desk agent's value has shifted to the guests self-service can't handle: the VIP request, the overbooking problem, the complaint that needs a human decision.

roles.title: Fast Food Worker

rationales.taskAutomation: Order-taking, order routing, and following standardized assembly steps are scripted work; AI drive-thru voice ordering already performs the ordering half end-to-end.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI voice-ordering systems are live in production at major chains, alongside mature kiosk and app-ordering technology.
rationales.adoption: Major fast-food chains have adopted AI drive-thru ordering and kiosks at meaningful scale, spreading from pilots to standard rollout.
rationales.agenticExposure: Order intake, routing to the kitchen display, and payment form a full agent-traversable pipeline; food assembly and packaging remain manual.

tasksAtRisk: Order entry into kitchen display systems
tasksGrowing: Food assembly and packaging speed
Handling order errors and customer complaints
Equipment troubleshooting during rush periods
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 35-3023, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Working alongside AI drive-thru and kiosk ordering systems
Cross-training into shift-lead or scheduling roles

roles.insight: AI voice ordering is already live at drive-thru windows for several national chains, taking over the exact scripted exchange this job used to require. What's left is assembly speed and handling the orders the AI gets wrong.

roles.title: Concierge

rationales.taskAutomation: Recommendations, reservation booking, and local-information Q&A are largely digital information-matching tasks that chatbots already handle well.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI concierge chatbots are mature and deployed at scale across major hospitality brands and luxury residential buildings.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is meaningful at large hotel chains and upscale properties but still spreading into independent and boutique properties.
rationales.agenticExposure: Recommendation matching, reservation booking, and itinerary suggestions form a strongly agent-ownable pipeline.

tasksGrowing: High-touch VIP and complex request handling
Building relationships with local vendors for exclusive access
Real-time problem-solving for guest emergencies
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 39-6012, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI concierge chatbot oversight and escalation management
Local vendor relationship-building for exclusive access

roles.insight: AI concierge chatbots now handle the reservation-and-recommendation baseline well enough that guests rarely notice. The role has shifted toward the requests a chatbot escalates: the impossible booking, the emergency, the guest who wants a person, not a menu of options.

roles.title: Barista

rationales.taskAutomation: Mobile order-ahead has automated the ordering and payment half of the job; drink assembly for standardized recipes is routine but still requires hands-on execution.
rationales.toolMaturity: Mobile ordering apps are commodity technology at major chains, and robotic coffee kiosks are a live if niche product category.
rationales.adoption: Large coffee chains have widely adopted mobile order-ahead as a default channel; robotic barista kiosks remain a minority deployment.
rationales.agenticExposure: Order intake, customization tracking, and queue sequencing form an agent-ownable pipeline; espresso extraction and milk-texture judgment stay manual.

tasksAtRisk: Standard drink recipes for high-volume orders
tasksGrowing: Latte art and specialty drink craftsmanship
Customer relationship-building in cafe settings
Equipment maintenance and quality troubleshooting
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 35-3023, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Specialty beverage craft and latte-art skill

roles.insight: Mobile order-ahead already handles the ordering conversation, and a handful of robotic kiosks now pull shots too. The craft differentiation, latte art, dialing in espresso, building cafe regulars, is exactly what keeps human baristas ahead of a vending machine.

roles.title: Restaurant Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Scheduling, ordering, and reporting are increasingly software-driven, but staff management, guest recovery, and floor judgment remain human-gated.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted scheduling and inventory platforms are mature and widely embedded in restaurant operations software.
rationales.adoption: Adoption of AI scheduling and inventory tools is common among multi-unit chains and spreading into independent restaurants.
rationales.agenticExposure: Scheduling, reorder generation, and sales reporting are agent-ownable back-office workflows; live staff and guest management are not.

tasksGrowing: Staff coaching and conflict resolution
Guest complaint recovery and reputation management
On-the-floor service-quality judgment during peak hours
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-9051, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI scheduling and inventory platform fluency

roles.insight: Scheduling and inventory software now generate the labor and ordering numbers a manager used to build by hand. The job is shifting toward what the software can't do: coaching staff and fixing a bad guest experience in real time.

roles.title: Event Planner

rationales.taskAutomation: Vendor research, budgeting, and logistics scheduling have a real digital component, but on-site execution and relationship management with vendors and clients stay human.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI budgeting and vendor-matching tools are credible and improving but not yet a mature, standard part of the event-planning toolchain.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is concentrated among early-adopter planning firms; most independent planners still run logistics manually.
rationales.agenticExposure: Vendor sourcing, budget tracking, and scheduling are agent-ownable workflows; live on-site problem-solving is not.

tasksGrowing: On-site crisis management during live events
Client relationship management and vision translation
Vendor negotiation and relationship-building
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1121, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: On-site crisis management for live events
AI budgeting and vendor-matching tool fluency
Client vision translation and stakeholder management

roles.insight: AI can already shortlist vendors and track a budget against invoices. It cannot fix a collapsed tent an hour before guests arrive, which is still the job most clients are actually paying for.

roles.title: Security Guard

rationales.taskAutomation: Foot patrol, access control, and in-person response can't be automated, but AI video analytics now handles a real share of the monitoring workload.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-powered camera analytics for anomaly and intrusion detection are mature and deployed at scale in corporate and retail security.
rationales.adoption: Large corporate campuses, retail chains, and financial institutions have adopted AI video monitoring; government and healthcare sites lag on procurement and trust grounds.
rationales.agenticExposure: Camera-feed triage, alert generation, and incident logging form an agent-ownable monitoring pipeline; physical response and de-escalation stay human.

tasksGrowing: Physical response to active incidents
De-escalation and public-facing judgment calls
Coordinating with law enforcement during real events
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 33-9032, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI video-analytics platform operation and alert triage

roles.insight: AI camera analytics already does the watching, flagging anomalies across hundreds of feeds no human could track continuously. It still can't walk the floor, de-escalate a confrontation, or make the judgment call on when to intervene, which is why the job is shifting from watching monitors to responding to what the monitors flag.

roles.title: Hotel Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Revenue management, reporting, and pricing are increasingly software-driven, but staff leadership, guest-relations judgment, and property operations stay human-gated.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-driven revenue-management and dynamic-pricing platforms are mature and standard in mid-size and large hotel operations.
rationales.adoption: Revenue-management software adoption is common across hotel chains; broader operational AI adoption is still spreading.
rationales.agenticExposure: Pricing optimization, occupancy forecasting, and reporting are agent-ownable; staff management and on-property crisis handling are not.

tasksGrowing: Staff leadership and department coordination
Guest-relations judgment for VIP and crisis situations
Property strategy and brand-standard execution
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-9081, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI revenue-management and dynamic-pricing platform fluency

roles.insight: Dynamic pricing algorithms now set most room rates automatically. What a hotel manager still owns is running the property when something goes wrong, staffing gaps, VIP escalations, brand-standard enforcement, none of which the pricing engine touches.

roles.title: Tour Guide

rationales.taskAutomation: Self-guided AI audio-tour apps now deliver competent historical narration, but live storytelling, group management, and adapting to questions stay human.
rationales.toolMaturity: GPS-triggered AI audio-guide apps are mature and widely available as a direct substitute for a basic guided walk.
rationales.adoption: Adoption of self-guided AI tour apps among independent travelers is growing steadily but remains an early-adopter behavior overall.
rationales.agenticExposure: Route scripting and narration generation are agent-ownable; live group management and real-time Q&A are not.

tasksAtRisk: Standard historical narration for common routes
Basic FAQ handling about a site or itinerary
tasksGrowing: Live storytelling and audience-reading
Group management and pacing for varied traveler needs
Handling unscripted questions and local color
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 39-7011, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Group-management and safety judgment on the move

roles.insight: An AI audio guide can narrate a landmark competently for a fraction of the cost. What it can't do is read a group's energy, take a real question off-script, or turn a rained-out afternoon into a good story anyway.

roles.title: Personal Trainer

rationales.taskAutomation: Generic workout-plan generation and progress tracking are now handled well by AI fitness apps, but live coaching, form correction, and motivation stay human.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI workout-generation and computer-vision form-tracking apps are mature and widely used by consumers.
rationales.adoption: Consumer adoption of AI fitness apps is substantial and growing, though gym-employed trainers still deliver most in-person sessions.
rationales.agenticExposure: Program design, progress tracking, and check-in messaging are agent-ownable; live spotting, form correction, and motivation are not.

tasksGrowing: Live form correction and injury-prevention coaching
Motivational coaching and accountability
Programming for complex or rehab-specific goals
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 39-9031, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI fitness-app integration into client programming

roles.insight: AI apps now generate a competent generic workout plan for free. What clients still pay a trainer for is what an app can't do in the room: catching bad form before it causes an injury and providing the accountability that keeps people showing up.

roles.title: Restaurant Server

rationales.taskAutomation: Order-taking and payment are increasingly handled by QR-code menus and tablets, but table-side hospitality, upselling, and adapting to guest needs stay human.
rationales.toolMaturity: QR ordering, tableside tablets, and AI-driven POS upsell prompts are mature and already standard in casual dining chains.
rationales.adoption: Fast-casual and mid-tier chains have widely adopted self-order tech; full-service and fine dining still route ordering through servers.
rationales.agenticExposure: The order-payment-fulfillment loop is a clean agent-ownable pipeline once a guest is seated; the hospitality moment itself is not.

tasksAtRisk: Menu question answering via QR-code interfaces
tasksGrowing: Reading the table and personalizing service
Handling complaints and service recovery
Relationship-based upselling and menu storytelling
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 35-3031, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Working alongside AI ordering and payment kiosks

roles.insight: QR ordering and tableside tablets have already automated the mechanical half of the job: taking orders and running payment. What's left is exactly what pays the tips: reading a table, recovering from a bad moment, and making a stranger feel looked after.

roles.title: Line Cook

rationales.taskAutomation: Line cooks execute standardized recipes under time pressure, a physically demanding task that's automatable only at the margins (prep, portioning).
rationales.toolMaturity: Some high-volume kitchens use robotic fry stations and portioning tools, but general-purpose line-cooking robotics remain immature.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is confined to a handful of high-volume chains piloting single-station robotics; most kitchens run entirely on human line cooks.
rationales.agenticExposure: Prep-list generation and ingredient reordering are agent-ownable; executing the line during service is not.

tasksAtRisk: Ingredient prep and portioning to spec
tasksGrowing: Multitasking across stations during a rush
Quality control and plate consistency under pressure
Adapting to last-minute menu or ticket changes
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 35-2014, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Multi-station speed and consistency under service pressure
Kitchen inventory and prep-planning tool fluency
Cross-training toward sous chef or kitchen management

roles.insight: Single-station robotics (fry baskets, portioning arms) have entered a handful of high-volume kitchens, but running a full line during a dinner rush is still beyond what any deployed system handles end to end.

roles.title: Chef

rationales.taskAutomation: Cooking, plating, and real-time kitchen judgment in an unstructured environment remain physical craft; only menu costing and reordering are cleanly automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI meal-kit and recipe-generation tools are widespread, but purpose-built kitchen robotics remain pilot-stage outside a handful of high-volume chains.
rationales.adoption: Independent restaurants and most chains still run kitchens on human judgment; AI shows up in back-office ordering and menu engineering, not on the line.
rationales.agenticExposure: Inventory reordering, prep-list generation, and food-cost tracking are agent-ownable back-office slices; live cooking is not.

tasksAtRisk: Basic recipe scaling for catering orders
tasksGrowing: Menu creativity and seasonal dish development
Live service judgment during a rush
Staff training and kitchen culture leadership
Guest-facing tasting and event appearances
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 35-1011, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Kitchen team leadership under live service pressure

roles.insight: Line cooking and plating still require dexterity and split-second judgment no kitchen robot handles at scale, so chefs stay largely insulated. The exposure sits in the office: AI now drafts prep lists, forecasts food cost, and reorders inventory, shrinking the admin half of the job faster than the culinary half.

roles.title: Funeral Director

rationales.taskAutomation: Coordinating services, handling remains, and supporting grieving families is licensed, high-trust, in-person work; only permit paperwork and scheduling are cleanly automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generic scheduling and document-generation tools apply, but purpose-built funeral-industry AI tooling has little traction.
rationales.adoption: The funeral industry is conservative and highly regulated; AI adoption is rare and confined to back-office administration.
rationales.agenticExposure: Death-certificate paperwork, permit filing, and scheduling are agent-ownable; family support and ceremonial judgment are not.

tasksGrowing: Grief support and family counseling during arrangements
Ceremonial judgment and personalization of services
Handling sensitive or complex family situations
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 39-4031, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Grief support and family counseling skills
Licensing and continuing education in mortuary science
Digital paperwork and permit-filing tool fluency

roles.insight: The paperwork around death, permits, certificates, scheduling, is genuinely automatable and slowly is being automated. Nothing about supporting a grieving family through a funeral is, and that's the part of the job that was never really about paperwork anyway.

roles.title: Bartender

rationales.taskAutomation: Drink-making, pacing a bar, and reading a room are dexterity- and judgment-heavy; only tab management and simple pour tracking are automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: Robotic bartending kiosks exist in a handful of novelty venues and casinos but have not moved into mainstream bar tooling.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is confined to isolated novelty installations; the vast majority of bars run entirely on human bartenders.
rationales.agenticExposure: Tab tracking and drink-order queuing are agent-ownable slices; mixing, pacing, and the social role of the bar are not.

tasksAtRisk: Standard pour measurement for high-volume wells
Basic drink-order queuing at high-traffic events
tasksGrowing: Craft cocktail development and flavor pairing
Reading the room and pacing service responsibly
Building repeat-customer relationships
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 35-3011, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Craft cocktail development and flavor innovation
POS and tab-automation fluency for high-volume service

roles.insight: Robotic bar kiosks are a novelty act, not a threat: mixing well drinks at volume while reading a room's mood and pace is still squarely human work. The real automation is in the back end, tab tracking and pour reconciliation, not behind the bar.

roles.title: Esthetician

rationales.taskAutomation: Skincare treatments require hands-on technique and licensed judgment about skin conditions; only intake and basic product recommendations are automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI skin-analysis apps (imaging-based diagnostics) are a real and improving product category used in consultations.
rationales.adoption: A growing share of spas and dermatology-adjacent practices use AI skin analysis for intake, but treatment delivery itself is untouched.
rationales.agenticExposure: Client intake, skin analysis, and product recommendation are agent-ownable; the physical treatment is not.

tasksAtRisk: AI-assisted skin analysis and product matching
tasksGrowing: Hands-on treatment technique for facials and peels
Judgment on contraindications and skin sensitivities
Building client trust for ongoing skincare plans
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 39-5094, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI skin-analysis and diagnostic tool fluency

roles.insight: AI imaging can now flag skin concerns and suggest a regimen during intake, but delivering the actual treatment, and judging contraindications on a real client, still requires a licensed, hands-on professional.

roles.title: Barber

rationales.taskAutomation: Cutting hair and shaping facial hair are precise, physical, client-specific tasks that current tools can't perform end-to-end.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI styling-preview apps exist for consultation, but no product performs the physical cut or shave.
rationales.adoption: AI adoption in barbershops is limited to booking and occasional style-preview apps; the core service is untouched.
rationales.agenticExposure: Scheduling and reminder messaging are agent-ownable; the haircut and shave are not.

tasksAtRisk: Inventory tracking for retail hair products
tasksGrowing: Precision cutting and shaving technique
Client relationship and neighborhood-shop culture
Building a personal client book
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 39-5011, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Precision cutting and traditional shave technique
AI booking and client-management app fluency

roles.insight: No AI tool has come close to performing a haircut or straight-razor shave. Exposure here is confined entirely to scheduling and admin, not the chair.

roles.title: Hair Stylist

rationales.taskAutomation: Cutting, coloring, and styling require fine motor skill and real-time adaptation to a client's head and hair; almost none of it is end-to-end automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI styling-preview and virtual try-on apps exist and are improving, but no tool performs the physical service itself.
rationales.adoption: A minority of chain salons use AI consultation or booking tools; most independent stylists use none.
rationales.agenticExposure: Booking, reminders, and color-formula recordkeeping are agent-ownable; the haircut itself is not.

tasksAtRisk: Retail product recommendation based on hair profile
tasksGrowing: Precision cutting and color technique
Real-time client consultation and style adaptation
Building a personal client book and referral base
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 39-5012, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Advanced cutting and color technique specialization
AI virtual try-on and style-consultation tools

roles.insight: Virtual try-on apps can preview a haircut; no product can perform one. The stylist's exposure is entirely in scheduling and formula recordkeeping, not the chair.

roles.title: Janitor

rationales.taskAutomation: Cleaning varied commercial spaces is unstructured physical work; only floor care in large open areas is meaningfully automatable today.
rationales.toolMaturity: Autonomous floor-scrubbing robots are mature and deployed in large retail and warehouse spaces.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is concentrated in big-box retail and large commercial floors; most janitorial work in smaller or mixed spaces stays manual.
rationales.agenticExposure: Task scheduling and supply reordering are agent-ownable slices; general cleaning and maintenance response are not.

tasksAtRisk: Large open-floor cleaning in retail and warehouse spaces
tasksGrowing: Cleaning irregular and detail-heavy spaces (restrooms, offices)
Building maintenance and minor-repair response
Handling spills, hazards, and emergency cleanup
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 37-2011, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Autonomous scrubbers already handle open floor space in big-box stores and warehouses overnight. Everything irregular, restrooms, offices, spills, hazards, still needs a person, and increasingly that person also supervises the robot.

roles.title: Childcare Worker

rationales.taskAutomation: Supervising, comforting, and physically caring for young children is presence-dependent, safety-critical work with essentially no end-to-end automatable share.
rationales.toolMaturity: Baby-monitor cameras and basic learning-app content exist, but no tool substitutes for hands-on child supervision.
rationales.adoption: Adoption of AI in childcare is limited to monitoring cameras and administrative apps; direct care itself remains untouched.
rationales.agenticExposure: Attendance logging, parent-communication updates, and activity planning are agent-ownable slices; supervision and care are not.

tasksGrowing: Direct supervision and safety monitoring
Emotional support and social-skill coaching
Individualized developmental attention
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 39-9011, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Nothing about supervising and comforting a room of toddlers is automatable at scale, so the core job is essentially untouched. Adoption shows up only in the paperwork around it: attendance logs, meal records, and parent updates.

roles.title: Housekeeper

rationales.taskAutomation: Cleaning hotel rooms is physically variable, unstructured work (different layouts, mess types, guest belongings) with almost no reliable end-to-end automation.
rationales.toolMaturity: Commercial cleaning robots exist for open floor areas but nothing automates full guest-room turnover.
rationales.adoption: Adoption of any cleaning automation in guest rooms is negligible; task-assignment software adoption is more common than physical automation.
rationales.agenticExposure: Room-assignment scheduling and supply-restocking flags are agent-ownable; the physical cleaning is not.

tasksGrowing: Full room turnover and quality inspection
Handling guest requests and special situations
Deep-cleaning judgment for varied room conditions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 37-2012, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Task-management app fluency for room assignments

roles.insight: No deployed robot handles the variability of a real guest room, scattered belongings, different layouts, spills. Automation touches only the scheduling around the job, not the cleaning itself.

roles.title: Pet Groomer

rationales.taskAutomation: Handling live animals safely while cutting, bathing, and styling coats is unpredictable physical work with essentially no reliable automation today.
rationales.toolMaturity: Automated pet-washing stations exist as a niche product but nothing performs a full groom on an uncooperative animal.
rationales.adoption: Adoption of any grooming automation is negligible; booking-software adoption is far more common than physical automation.
rationales.agenticExposure: Appointment scheduling and reminder messaging are agent-ownable; handling and grooming the animal are not.

tasksAtRisk: Basic retail product recommendations for pet owners
tasksGrowing: Handling anxious or difficult animals safely
Breed-specific cut and styling technique
Building trust with regular clients and their pets
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 39-2021, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Safe handling of anxious or aggressive animals
AI booking and client-reminder app fluency

roles.insight: No automated station can safely handle an anxious, wriggling animal through a full groom. The job's small digital footprint, booking and reminders, is where its modest exposure is concentrated.

roles.title: Massage Therapist

rationales.taskAutomation: Therapeutic touch, technique adaptation, and injury-aware judgment define the work; there is no credible end-to-end automation path.
rationales.toolMaturity: Massage chairs and devices exist as consumer products but are not a substitute for licensed therapeutic technique.
rationales.adoption: AI adoption in the profession is essentially confined to scheduling and intake software.
rationales.agenticExposure: Booking, intake forms, and session-note documentation are agent-ownable slices; the treatment itself is not.

tasksGrowing: Therapeutic technique tailored to injury or condition
Client rapport and ongoing care planning
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 31-9011, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Specialized therapeutic technique (sports, prenatal, medical massage)

roles.insight: Therapeutic touch calibrated to an individual body and injury has no automated substitute today. The role's small digital footprint, booking and intake, is where nearly all of its modest exposure lives.

roles.title: Statistician

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can run standard hypothesis tests, build regression and forecasting models, and generate results write-ups end to end; designing novel study methodology and defending causal claims stay human-led.
rationales.toolMaturity: Statistical and ML tooling (R, Python stacks, AutoML, agentic notebook assistants) is mature and widely embedded in analytics workflows.
rationales.adoption: Large employers in tech, pharma, insurance, and government agencies routinely use AI-assisted statistical modeling in production analytics.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run full analysis pipelines, from data cleaning through model fitting to draft reporting, under a statistician's review of assumptions and study design.

tasksAtRisk: Standard hypothesis testing and regression modeling
Automated data cleaning and exploratory analysis pipelines
Forecasting model builds using established methods
Drafting results sections and statistical summary reports
tasksGrowing: Novel study design and causal inference methodology
Defending statistical conclusions to regulators and stakeholders
Auditing AI model bias and validity in high-stakes decisions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-2041, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Agentic notebooks now run entire analysis pipelines from cleaning to draft reporting. Statisticians who own study design, causal reasoning, and validity auditing of AI-generated results are moving from technician to the analysis's final authority.

roles.title: Quality Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can run statistical process control analysis, flag defect-rate anomalies, and draft nonconformance reports end to end, but root-cause investigation on the physical line and supplier audits stay human.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted SPC, defect-detection vision systems, and quality-analytics platforms are credible and improving fast across manufacturing.
rationales.adoption: Large manufacturers increasingly deploy AI-assisted quality analytics and vision inspection; smaller operations still rely on manual checks.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run full SPC-monitoring-to-report loops and flag out-of-control processes, but physical root-cause investigation on the shop floor needs an engineer.

tasksAtRisk: Statistical process control monitoring and reporting
Nonconformance report and CAPA documentation drafting
tasksGrowing: Physical root-cause investigation on the production line
Supplier quality audits and on-site process review
Cross-functional quality-system design for new products
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2199, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI vision systems and SPC dashboards now catch defects and flag anomalies automatically, but tracing the anomaly back to its physical cause on the line still needs a quality engineer walking the floor.

roles.title: Industrial Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can run discrete-event simulations, optimize line balancing, and generate throughput reports end to end, but negotiating floor-level process change with operators stays human.
rationales.toolMaturity: Digital-twin and simulation-optimization tools are credible, production-deployed products at large manufacturers.
rationales.adoption: Large manufacturing and logistics operations increasingly run AI-driven simulation and scheduling tools; smaller operations lag.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run full simulation-optimize-report loops for line balancing and scheduling with minimal supervision.

tasksGrowing: Floor-level change management with operators and supervisors
Cross-functional process redesign for new product lines
Ergonomics and safety-driven workstation design
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2112, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Simulation-driven line balancing and scheduling now run largely on their own. Industrial engineers who own operator-facing change management and ergonomics design keep the roles AI can't run unsupervised.

roles.title: Epidemiologist

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can run outbreak-model simulations and surveillance-data analysis end to end, but designing studies and communicating risk to policymakers stay human-led.
rationales.toolMaturity: Disease-modeling and surveillance-analytics tools with AI-assisted forecasting are mature and widely used in public health agencies.
rationales.adoption: Large public health agencies and health systems routinely use AI-assisted surveillance analytics; adoption is thinner in smaller local health departments.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run surveillance-data pipelines and flag emerging clusters, but risk communication and policy recommendation require a human epidemiologist.

tasksAtRisk: Outbreak model simulation and forecasting runs
Routine statistical report generation for case data
tasksGrowing: Study design for novel disease and exposure questions
Risk communication to policymakers and the public
Field investigation of active outbreaks
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 19-1041, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Novel Study Design for Emerging Threats

roles.insight: Surveillance data pipelines and forecasting models increasingly run themselves, but translating a cluster of numbers into a policy recommendation the public will trust remains an epidemiologist's job.

roles.title: Materials Scientist

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can screen candidate material compositions computationally and draft characterization reports, but physical synthesis, testing, and failure analysis remain lab-bound.
rationales.toolMaturity: Materials-informatics and generative-composition tools are credible and improving quickly at research labs and materials companies.
rationales.adoption: Large materials and semiconductor companies increasingly use AI-driven composition screening; adoption is thinner in traditional metallurgy and ceramics shops.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run high-throughput virtual screening loops across composition space, but physical synthesis and destructive testing require lab work.

tasksAtRisk: Computational screening of candidate material compositions
Literature synthesis for materials property prediction
tasksGrowing: Physical synthesis and novel material fabrication
Failure analysis on fielded material samples
Cross-disciplinary application engineering with product teams
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 19-2032, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI-driven composition screening now narrows candidate materials before a single sample is made. Materials scientists who master physical synthesis and failure analysis on real parts stay essential downstream of the algorithm.

roles.title: Meteorologist

rationales.taskAutomation: AI weather models now generate routine short-range forecasts at high skill, but severe-weather warning judgment and public communication stay meteorologist-led.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-driven numerical weather prediction (e.g., large-scale ML forecast models) is mature and increasingly used alongside traditional physics-based models.
rationales.adoption: National weather agencies and broadcast media widely use AI-assisted forecast models as one input among several.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run ensemble forecast pipelines and flag anomalies, but issuing severe-weather warnings and communicating risk to the public require a meteorologist.

tasksGrowing: Severe-weather warning judgment and timing decisions
Public and emergency-management risk communication
Novel forecast-model validation and bias correction
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 19-2021, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI forecast models now rival traditional physics-based models on routine forecasts, but the decision to issue a tornado warning, and the words used to communicate it, still rest with a meteorologist.

roles.title: Electrical Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can auto-route PCB layouts, generate schematic-to-netlist checks, and draft compliance documentation, but power-system safety margins and novel circuit topology design stay with the engineer.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted EDA tools (auto-placement, DRC checking, signal-integrity analysis) are mature in mainstream PCB and chip-design suites.
rationales.adoption: Semiconductor and consumer-electronics firms use AI-assisted layout and verification broadly; industrial and utility electrical engineering adopts more slowly.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run design-rule checks, regression test suites, and compliance-doc generation end to end, but field commissioning and safety-critical sign-off require a licensed engineer.

tasksAtRisk: Automated PCB placement, routing, and design-rule checking
Drafting standard compliance and test documentation
Running routine signal-integrity and power-analysis simulations
tasksGrowing: Novel circuit topology and power-system architecture design
Field commissioning and safety-critical system validation
Cross-domain integration with mechanical and software teams
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2071, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Layout, routing, and compliance paperwork are increasingly automated by AI-assisted EDA tools. Electrical engineers who specialize in power-system architecture, safety-critical validation, and system integration hold the ground AI can't reach yet.

roles.title: Chemist

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can propose candidate reaction pathways and draft analytical reports, but bench synthesis, purification, and instrument calibration remain physical lab work.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted retrosynthesis and spectral-analysis tools are credible and gaining traction in pharma and specialty-chemical labs.
rationales.adoption: Adoption concentrates in large pharmaceutical and chemical-manufacturing R&D labs; academic and small-lab settings lag.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can propose synthesis routes and interpret routine spectra, but physical bench work and safety judgment in the lab require a chemist present.

tasksAtRisk: Reaction pathway proposal and retrosynthesis screening
tasksGrowing: Bench synthesis, purification, and hands-on lab technique
Novel compound discovery and hypothesis generation
Lab safety judgment and hazardous-material handling
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 19-2031, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI can now suggest reaction routes and read routine spectra, but someone still has to run the bench, handle the hazards, and judge when a novel result is real. That physical and hypothesis-generation work keeps chemists in the lab.

roles.title: Mechanical Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can generate first-pass CAD variants, run parametric FEA sweeps, and draft design calc reports, but stamped drawings, tolerance trade-offs, and prototype validation still require an engineer's sign-off.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generative-design add-ons inside SolidWorks, Fusion 360, and Ansys are shipping and improving quickly, but full autonomous design-to-manufacture loops are not yet standard.
rationales.adoption: Generative design and simulation copilots have reached large aerospace, automotive, and industrial-equipment OEMs but are still uneven at small and mid-size manufacturers.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run overnight simulation sweeps, flag out-of-tolerance results, and pre-populate BOMs, but physical prototyping and PE-stamped release remain human gates.

tasksAtRisk: Generating parametric CAD variants for early design exploration
Running routine finite element and thermal simulation sweeps
Drafting design calculation reports and bill-of-materials documentation
tasksGrowing: Physical prototype testing and failure investigation
Cross-disciplinary systems integration and trade-off judgment
Design-for-manufacturability review with production teams
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2141, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now runs simulation sweeps and drafts CAD variants overnight, compressing the early design loop. Mechanical engineers who own physical validation, manufacturability trade-offs, and final design sign-off stay indispensable.

roles.title: Biologist

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can analyze sequencing data and draft literature reviews, but field sampling, wet-lab experimentation, and specimen judgment stay hands-on.
rationales.toolMaturity: Bioinformatics and AI-assisted sequence-analysis tools are mature for genomics work but less developed for field ecology and specimen study.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is strongest in genomics and biotech labs; field-based ecology and wildlife biology still rely on manual methods.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run sequence-analysis and literature-synthesis pipelines, but fieldwork, specimen collection, and experiment design need a biologist present.

tasksAtRisk: Literature review synthesis for research proposals
tasksGrowing: Field sampling and ecological survey work
Wet-lab experimental design and execution
Specimen-based taxonomic and morphological judgment
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 19-1029, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Genomic analysis and literature synthesis are increasingly AI-assisted, but fieldwork and wet-lab experimentation still need a biologist's hands and judgment on the actual organism.

roles.title: Biomedical Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can accelerate device simulation and draft regulatory submission sections, but device safety validation and FDA-facing risk analysis remain firmly human.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted simulation and regulatory-drafting tools exist and are improving, but device-specific validation tooling is still maturing.
rationales.adoption: Adoption concentrates at large medical-device manufacturers; smaller device shops still rely on manual validation workflows.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can pre-populate 510(k) sections and run simulation batches, but device risk classification and clinical validation stay with engineers.

tasksAtRisk: Device simulation and modeling parameter sweeps
Draft sections of regulatory submissions (510(k), technical files)
tasksGrowing: Device safety risk classification and clinical validation
Novel implant and diagnostic device design
FDA and notified-body regulatory negotiation
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2031, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI drafts routine submission sections and runs device simulations, but clinical validation and regulatory risk sign-off still require an engineer to own patient safety.

roles.title: Physicist

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can run large-scale numerical simulations and data-reduction pipelines, but formulating novel theory and designing new experiments remain deeply human.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted simulation, symbolic-math, and data-analysis tools are mature in computational physics workflows.
rationales.adoption: National labs and large research universities have adopted AI-assisted simulation broadly; smaller institutions lag on compute and tooling access.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run simulation batches and reduce experimental data, but hypothesis generation and experimental apparatus design are not agent-reachable.

tasksAtRisk: Large-scale numerical simulation and parameter sweeps
Experimental data reduction and statistical analysis pipelines
Literature survey synthesis for grant proposals
tasksGrowing: Novel theoretical framework development
Experimental apparatus design and instrumentation
Cross-disciplinary collaboration on frontier research questions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 19-2012, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Simulation and data reduction increasingly run on AI-assisted pipelines, but designing the experiment and formulating the theory behind it remain the physicist's irreplaceable core.

roles.title: Research Scientist

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can run literature synthesis, propose experiment variants, and draft manuscript sections, but designing the core hypothesis and validating results experimentally stay with the scientist.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI research-assistant tools (literature review, hypothesis generation, lab-notebook copilots) are mature and widely used in industry R&D.
rationales.adoption: Large pharma, biotech, and industrial R&D labs have adopted AI research assistants broadly; smaller labs and academia trail.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run full literature-to-draft-experiment loops and manage lab-notebook documentation, but experimental execution and validation need a scientist.

tasksAtRisk: Literature review synthesis and gap-analysis drafting
tasksGrowing: Core hypothesis formulation and research direction-setting
Experimental validation and results interpretation
Cross-team scientific leadership and grant strategy
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 19-1042, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI research assistants now draft literature reviews and propose experiment variants, but deciding which hypothesis is worth testing, and validating the result, is still a scientist's call.

roles.title: Environmental Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can draft permit applications, run standard dispersion and contaminant-transport models, but site remediation judgment and regulatory negotiation stay engineer-led.
rationales.toolMaturity: Modeling software with AI-assisted scenario generation exists but is not yet a mature, embedded standard across the field.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is uneven, concentrated at larger environmental consultancies serving industrial clients.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run compliance-scenario modeling and draft reports, but field sampling and agency negotiation require a human engineer on site.

tasksAtRisk: Permit application drafting and standard compliance reports
tasksGrowing: Site remediation strategy and field judgment
Regulatory agency negotiation and public-comment response
Novel pollution-control system design
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2081, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Permit drafting and dispersion modeling are increasingly AI-assisted, but remediation strategy and agency negotiation still depend on an engineer's field judgment and standing with regulators.

roles.title: Civil Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can draft preliminary site layouts, run routine structural load checks, and generate permit paperwork, but stamped drawings, public-safety liability, and site-specific judgment remain firmly human.
rationales.toolMaturity: BIM-integrated AI plug-ins for clash detection and quantity takeoffs are credible and improving, but full autonomous civil design is far from mature.
rationales.adoption: Large engineering firms use AI-assisted BIM and takeoff tools; most municipal and small-firm civil engineering work still runs on traditional workflows.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can pre-check drawings for code compliance and flag clashes across disciplines, but site inspection, stakeholder negotiation, and PE stamping are not agent-reachable.

tasksAtRisk: Preliminary site layout and grading drafts
Routine structural load and code-compliance checks
Quantity takeoffs and permit application paperwork
tasksGrowing: Site inspection and field condition assessment
Public-safety liability sign-off on stamped drawings
Stakeholder negotiation on public infrastructure projects
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2051, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now handles quantity takeoffs and BIM clash checks, but stamped drawings still carry an engineer's legal liability. Civil engineers who lean into field inspection and public-project stakeholder work stay ahead of the automation curve.

roles.title: Chemical Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can run process-simulation sweeps and draft mass/energy balance reports, but plant safety analysis, scale-up judgment, and regulatory sign-off remain engineer-led.
rationales.toolMaturity: Process simulation software (Aspen, gPROMS) has credible AI-assisted optimization add-ons, but they are not yet the industry default.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is concentrated in large petrochemical and pharma manufacturers; most mid-size process plants still run traditional simulation workflows.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run parameter sweeps across a process model and flag deviations, but physical plant commissioning and hazard review need an on-site engineer.

tasksAtRisk: Process simulation parameter sweeps and optimization runs
Mass and energy balance report drafting
tasksGrowing: Plant safety and hazard analysis (HAZOP) leadership
Scale-up judgment from lab to production
Regulatory compliance sign-off for new processes
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2041, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Simulation sweeps and balance calculations increasingly run themselves, but hazard analysis and scale-up decisions still ride on an engineer's judgment. Chemical engineers who own plant safety leadership hold the highest-value ground.

roles.title: Aerospace Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can accelerate aerodynamic CFD sweeps and structural sizing iterations, but flight-certification analysis and safety-margin decisions are tightly regulated and engineer-owned.
rationales.toolMaturity: CFD and structural-optimization tools with AI-assisted surrogate modeling are improving fast but remain specialist, not commodity, tooling.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is concentrated at major primes and a handful of well-funded startups; most of the supply chain still relies on traditional analysis cycles.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run overnight CFD and FEA batch sweeps and surface anomalies, but certification documentation and airworthiness sign-off stay with licensed engineers.

tasksGrowing: Flight-certification and airworthiness analysis
Novel propulsion and materials system integration
Failure investigation on physical test articles
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2011, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Design-iteration cycles are compressing as AI-assisted CFD and structural tools mature, but certification and airworthiness sign-off remain tightly regulated, engineer-owned work that AI cannot touch.

roles.title: Structural Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can run preliminary load-path analysis and draft standard structural calc packages, but stamped design responsibility and seismic/wind risk judgment stay with the licensed engineer.
rationales.toolMaturity: Structural-analysis software with AI-assisted optimization plug-ins is credible but not yet a mature, universal standard.
rationales.adoption: Larger structural firms use AI-assisted analysis tools on complex projects; most routine building work still runs traditional calc-and-review cycles.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run structural-analysis batches and flag code-check failures, but stamped drawing sign-off and forensic failure investigation need a licensed engineer.

tasksGrowing: Stamped drawing sign-off and licensed design responsibility
Seismic and wind-risk judgment for novel structures
Forensic structural failure investigation
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2051, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Load-path analysis and calc-package drafting are increasingly AI-assisted, but the stamp on a structural drawing, and the legal liability it carries, still belongs to a licensed engineer.

roles.title: Geologist

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can process seismic and remote-sensing datasets and draft standard reports, but field mapping, core-sample judgment, and site-specific interpretation stay hands-on.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted seismic-interpretation and remote-sensing tools are credible in energy and mining exploration but not universally deployed.
rationales.adoption: Adoption concentrates at large oil, gas, and mining companies; environmental and academic geology still rely on manual field methods.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can flag anomalies in seismic or satellite data, but physical fieldwork, sample collection, and hazard assessment need a geologist on site.

tasksAtRisk: Routine subsurface data visualization and mapping prep
tasksGrowing: Field mapping and physical site assessment
Core-sample interpretation and hazard judgment
On-site exploration and drilling-program decisions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 19-2042, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: AI now chews through seismic and satellite datasets fast, but reading an actual outcrop or core sample in the field is still a geologist's call. Fieldwork keeps this role grounded, literally.

roles.title: Petroleum Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can run reservoir-simulation sweeps and drilling-data analysis, but well-site safety decisions and field operations judgment remain firmly human.
rationales.toolMaturity: Reservoir-simulation and drilling-optimization software has AI-assisted modules, but adoption across the industry is uneven and specialist.
rationales.adoption: Adoption concentrates at major operators with large digital-oilfield programs; independents and smaller operators lag.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run reservoir-model sweeps and flag production anomalies, but rig-site safety calls and well-control decisions need an engineer on location or on call.

tasksGrowing: Well-site safety and well-control decision-making
Novel enhanced-recovery technique design
Field operations judgment under changing conditions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2171, p25-p75 rounded, upper end reflects operator market postings
roles.insight: Reservoir simulation and drilling-data analysis increasingly run on AI-assisted models, but a well-control decision at the rig site still needs a petroleum engineer who owns the physical risk.

roles.title: Tax Examiner

rationales.taskAutomation: Matching returns against third-party data, flagging discrepancies, and routine adjustment notices are routine digital production that AI already performs reliably.
rationales.toolMaturity: Return-matching and fraud-detection systems have been in production at tax agencies for years and are a mature, embedded toolchain.
rationales.adoption: Government revenue agencies have actually deployed automated matching at scale, though procurement and policy still lag private-sector tax-prep automation.
rationales.agenticExposure: Discrepancy triage and reconciliation queues are a textbook agent-ownable workflow, while complex audits and taxpayer disputes require human review.

tasksAtRisk: Discrepancy flagging and routine adjustment notices
tasksGrowing: Complex audit judgment on ambiguous filings
Resolving taxpayer disputes and appeals
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-2081, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Automated matching and fraud-detection system fluency
Taxpayer dispute resolution and communication skills

roles.insight: One of the most exposed public-sector roles here: matching and flagging is exactly the routine digital work automation already handles well.

roles.title: Court Clerk

rationales.taskAutomation: Docketing, filing, records requests, and fee processing are routine document handling that current AI and e-filing systems already do at high reliability.
rationales.toolMaturity: E-filing, document management, and OCR systems are mature and widely deployed in modern court administration.
rationales.adoption: Court systems are government bodies that adopt e-filing and automation more slowly than private legal-services firms, and many still run on legacy systems.
rationales.agenticExposure: Filing, docketing, and routine public-counter queries form an agent-traversable pipeline of digital handoffs.

tasksGrowing: Handling unusual or contested procedural questions
In-person courtroom support during proceedings
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-4031, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: E-filing and court case-management system fluency
Public-facing customer service and courtroom support skills

roles.insight: A document-heavy government role where mature e-filing and OCR tools already cover much of the routine workload.

roles.title: Policy Analyst

rationales.taskAutomation: Literature review, data analysis, and memo drafting make up most of the visible output and are automatable at a high reliability today.
rationales.toolMaturity: LLM-based research-synthesis and drafting tools are mature and widely used for exactly this kind of document-heavy analytical work.
rationales.adoption: Government policy shops lag private-sector think tanks and consultancies in rolling these tools into daily workflows, though usage is spreading.
rationales.agenticExposure: Research-summarize-draft is close to the canonical agent-ownable workflow, leaving final recommendation and political judgment as the human core.

tasksAtRisk: Data analysis and chart generation for briefs
First-draft policy memo and briefing-document writing
tasksGrowing: Framing recommendations for political feasibility
Briefing decision-makers and answering follow-up questions
Synthesizing conflicting stakeholder input
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 19-3099, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI research-synthesis and drafting tool fluency

roles.insight: One of the highest-automation roles in this category: the drafting and research pipeline is squarely inside what agentic tools do well today.

roles.title: Urban Planner

rationales.taskAutomation: GIS analysis, zoning-compliance checks, and comprehensive-plan drafting are substantially digital, but public hearings and political judgment gate final decisions.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted GIS analysis, scenario modeling, and zoning-compliance software are credible products improving quickly, though not yet standard everywhere.
rationales.adoption: Planning departments are government employers that adopt new software more slowly than private-sector real estate and architecture firms.
rationales.agenticExposure: Zoning research, data analysis, and draft-plan generation are a research-summarize-draft loop an agent can meaningfully own.

tasksAtRisk: Zoning-compliance checks and permit data analysis
tasksGrowing: Public hearings and community stakeholder engagement
Negotiating trade-offs among competing interests
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 19-3051, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI-assisted GIS and scenario-modeling tool fluency
Public engagement and stakeholder facilitation skills

roles.insight: The analytical half of planning is automating steadily; the public-hearing, political-negotiation half is not.

roles.title: 911 Dispatcher

rationales.taskAutomation: Call intake, location lookup, and unit routing are largely scripted digital work, but life-safety triage judgment under time pressure keeps most of the role gated.
rationales.toolMaturity: Next-gen 911 systems, automatic location/translation, and AI-assisted call triage are already embedded in modern CAD platforms.
rationales.adoption: Only well-funded metro 911 centers have deployed the newer AI-assisted triage tools; most county and rural centers still run on older CAD systems.
rationales.agenticExposure: Call intake, routing, and unit dispatch form a pipeline of digital decisions an agent can traverse, tempered by the liability of getting a life-safety call wrong.

tasksAtRisk: Caller location lookup and unit routing
Call transcription and CAD data entry
tasksGrowing: Live triage judgment on ambiguous or high-stress calls
Cross-agency coordination during major incidents
De-escalating callers in crisis
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-5031, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Next-gen 911 (NG911) and AI-assisted CAD platform fluency
High-stress call triage and de-escalation skills

roles.insight: Dispatch is one of the most digitally automatable public-safety jobs on this index, and NG911 tooling is already mature. What keeps adoption slow is money and liability, not capability: the gap between a well-funded metro center and a rural county center is enormous.

roles.title: Building Inspector

rationales.taskAutomation: Plan review against code checklists is increasingly automatable, but on-site inspection judgment and sign-off liability keep most of the job gated.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted code-compliance plan review tools are a credible, fast-improving product category, though not yet embedded everywhere.
rationales.adoption: Municipal building departments are budget-constrained government offices that adopt new review software slower than private engineering firms.
rationales.agenticExposure: Automated plan-check against code is a workflow an agent can meaningfully own, while physical site inspection stays human.

tasksGrowing: On-site structural and safety inspection judgment
Explaining violations and remediation to owners and contractors
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 47-4011, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: On-site inspection and client communication skills

roles.insight: Plan-check paperwork is a good fit for automated compliance review; the physical walkthrough and sign-off liability are not.

roles.title: Detective

rationales.taskAutomation: Records searches, digital forensics triage, and report writing are automatable, but interviewing witnesses, judgment calls on leads, and courtroom testimony are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted case-linkage and digital-forensics tools are improving fast but are not yet a standard part of most department toolchains.
rationales.adoption: Larger metro and federal agencies use analytics tools; most local detective units still rely on manual database searches.
rationales.agenticExposure: Cross-referencing records, evidence logs, and cold-case databases is a research-summarize loop an agent can meaningfully assist, though final investigative judgment stays human.

tasksAtRisk: Digital forensics triage on seized devices
tasksGrowing: Witness interviewing and rapport-building
Courtroom testimony and evidence presentation
Judgment calls on which leads to pursue
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 33-3021, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI-assisted digital forensics and case-linkage tools
Advanced interview and interrogation technique certification

roles.insight: Records work is automating fast, but the interview room and the witness stand still require a human.

roles.title: Probation Officer

rationales.taskAutomation: Risk-assessment scoring, case notes, and check-in scheduling are largely digital, but supervision decisions and the client relationship remain judgment-gated.
rationales.toolMaturity: Risk-assessment algorithms are already embedded in many probation departments' case-management systems and have been for years.
rationales.adoption: Courts and probation departments have adopted risk-assessment tools faster than most public-safety functions, though still trailing private-sector norms.
rationales.agenticExposure: Case-note generation and scheduling form an agent-ownable slice, while in-person supervision and relapse-risk judgment stay with the officer.

tasksAtRisk: Risk-assessment score generation and case notes
tasksGrowing: In-person supervision and relationship-building with clients
Judgment calls on treatment referrals and sanctions
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 21-1092, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Risk-assessment algorithm interpretation and case-management software

roles.insight: Risk-scoring tools already do a chunk of the analytic work here; the supervisory relationship is what remains distinctly human.

roles.title: Emergency Management Director

rationales.taskAutomation: Hazard-mitigation plans, grant paperwork, and resource-tracking are digital, but cross-agency coordination and live crisis leadership are not automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted disaster-modeling and resource-allocation tools are improving but remain niche products, not a standard part of emergency-management toolchains.
rationales.adoption: Emergency management offices are small, government-funded operations that are slow to adopt even proven planning software.
rationales.agenticExposure: Grant and plan drafting is a moderate agent-ownable slice, while live multi-agency crisis coordination stays firmly human.

tasksGrowing: Live multi-agency coordination during disasters
Public risk communication under uncertainty
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-9161, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Planning paperwork automates readily; the job's value is in the live coordination no model can run during an actual disaster.

roles.title: Social Worker

rationales.taskAutomation: Intake screening, case notes, and benefits-matching are increasingly automatable, but home visits, crisis intervention, and the therapeutic relationship are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Case-management and benefits-eligibility AI tools are maturing quickly inside major EHR and human-services platforms, though far from universal.
rationales.adoption: Public and nonprofit social-services agencies are chronically underfunded and slow to roll out new software relative to the private sector.
rationales.agenticExposure: Intake triage and eligibility-matching are back-office slices an agent can plausibly own, but the core relationship-based casework cannot be delegated.

tasksAtRisk: Intake form completion and benefits-eligibility screening
tasksGrowing: Crisis intervention and in-person safety assessment
Building trust with clients over time
Navigating complex family and legal situations
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 21-1029, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: The paperwork half of social work is getting real automation help, which is good news if it frees caseworkers for the in-person visits and trust-building that no tool can substitute for. Agencies that adopt these tools fastest are still the exception, not the rule, in the public sector.

roles.title: City Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Budget oversight, personnel decisions, council relations, and public accountability define the role and rest on judgment and relationships AI cannot replace.
rationales.toolMaturity: Budget-analytics and reporting-dashboard tools are useful but generic; nothing purpose-built for municipal executive leadership has real traction.
rationales.adoption: Municipal government is one of the slowest sectors to change executive workflows, and most cities still run budget and reporting processes manually.
rationales.agenticExposure: Cross-department coordination and public accountability require human authority and relationships that autonomous agents cannot hold.

tasksGrowing: Council and community relationship management
Personnel leadership and organizational decision-making
Crisis and public-accountability leadership
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-1011 (closest detailed occupation), adjusted toward ICMA municipal-manager survey data

defenseSkills.skill: Council relations and public communication skills

roles.insight: An executive role where AI helps with reporting mechanics but not the accountability and relationship work that defines the job.

roles.title: Police Officer

rationales.taskAutomation: Patrol, use-of-force decisions, and in-person community contact dominate the job; only incident report drafting and records lookups are reliably automatable today.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI report-writing assistants (e.g. body-cam-to-narrative tools) and license-plate/analytics platforms are real and improving fast, but remain add-ons rather than the core toolchain.
rationales.adoption: Most departments are still piloting or budget-constrained; only well-funded metro forces have moved report-writing and analytics tools into daily use.
rationales.agenticExposure: The core of the job is physical presence and split-second judgment in the field, which autonomous software cannot reach.

tasksAtRisk: Drafting routine incident and arrest reports
Transcribing body-camera footage into narrative form
tasksGrowing: De-escalation and crisis-intervention response
Community relations and neighborhood policing
Interpreting AI-flagged leads and evidence with legal judgment
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 33-3051, p25-p75 rounded
roles.insight: Patrol work itself sits outside AI's reach, but the paperwork half of the job is being rewritten: departments that adopted AI report-drafting tools report officers spending far less time behind a keyboard. Officers who master those tools free up the in-person hours that actually define the job.

roles.title: Correctional Officer

rationales.taskAutomation: Supervising incarcerated people, physical security, and in-person response to incidents dominate the role and cannot be automated end-to-end.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted surveillance and behavior-flagging tools exist in some large facilities but are far from a standard toolchain.
rationales.adoption: Corrections departments are among the most conservative and underfunded public employers, slowing even proven tool adoption.
rationales.agenticExposure: The job is physical presence and security response in an unstructured, high-risk environment that agents cannot reach.

tasksGrowing: De-escalating conflicts among incarcerated individuals
Emergency response to physical altercations
Rehabilitation-oriented supervision
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 33-3012, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Facility surveillance and behavior-monitoring system literacy

roles.insight: One of the most physically-grounded roles in this index; AI's reach is limited to logging and monitoring at the margins.

roles.title: Park Ranger

rationales.taskAutomation: Patrol, visitor safety response, search-and-rescue, and interpretive education happen outdoors in unstructured environments AI cannot perform.
rationales.toolMaturity: Camera-trap and wildlife-monitoring analytics exist but are research-grade tools, not a mature standard toolchain for ranger work.
rationales.adoption: Park agencies are small, chronically underfunded government employers with little budget for new AI tooling.
rationales.agenticExposure: The role is overwhelmingly physical fieldwork in remote, unstructured terrain that autonomous software has no way to reach.

tasksGrowing: Search-and-rescue response
In-person interpretive education and wildlife-conflict response
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 19-1031 (closest detailed occupation), p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Wildlife-monitoring analytics and camera-trap data tools

roles.insight: Among the least automatable roles in this index; the job exists precisely where digital tools don't reach.

roles.title: Firefighter

rationales.taskAutomation: Suppression, rescue, and emergency medical response are physical, unstructured, and licensed acts that AI cannot perform end-to-end.
rationales.toolMaturity: Predictive-dispatch routing and drone/thermal-imaging tools exist and help incident commanders, but nothing role-specific has reached the standard toolchain.
rationales.adoption: Fire departments are conservative, budget-limited public employers; adoption of even mature dispatch-support tools is still early-adopter only.
rationales.agenticExposure: Firefighting is entirely physical-world work; autonomous software agents have no path into suppression or rescue.

tasksAtRisk: Post-incident report and NFIRS data entry
tasksGrowing: Live incident command decision-making under uncertainty
Technical rescue and hazmat response
Public fire-safety education and community engagement
roles.salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 33-2011, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Technical rescue and hazmat response certification
Incident command software and predictive-dispatch tool literacy

roles.insight: Firefighting sits at the physical floor of this index: suppression and rescue can't be automated at any reliability AI offers today. The only real AI pressure is on the administrative tail of the job.
